1000 needles.description: Shoots multiple needles at enemies within range.
1000 needles.name: 1000 Needles
notes: This spell can not be partially resisted. Instead it will just fail to take effect.
Damage is divided equally among the targets, but each can separately receive a "fails to take effect" message.
Magic bursting this spell will only increase accuracy and not damage.
A positive or negative Monster Correlation only affects accuracy and not damage.
Reduced in PvP to 750 damage.

absolute terror.name: Absolute Terror
notes: May only be cast with the ability Unbridled Learning .
Effect can be resisted by NMs while some are susceptible to it. Success rate and duration is enhanced via Blue Magic Skill , Magic Accuracy , Elemental Seal , etc.

absorb-attri.description: Steals an enemy's beneficial status effects.
absorb-attri.name: Absorb-Attri
notes: Absorbs one beneficial status effect.
Nether Void allows for two beneficial status effects to be absorbed.
Effective against Undead .
Most abilities that can be dispelled are absorb-able.

accession.description: Extends the effect of the next Healing or Enhancing White Magic spell to party members within range. MP cost is doubled and casting time is doubled.
accession.duration: 00:01:00 or once the effect is applied to a spell, whichever first
accession.name: Accession
notes: Accession functions on most enhancing and healing spells that SCH learns natively or can utilize from a sub job.
* Notable exceptions include all Reraise spells, all Raise spells, and Haste.
* Accession typically does not work on spells that can be used only by other main jobs while subbing SCH, such as Cure V, Cure VI, and Sacrifice.
When casting a compatible spell on a player outside your party, Accession will extend its effect to players in the target's party.
Activating this Stratagem doubles the MP cost and recast time of the spell and nullifies the Light Arts recast time and MP cost reduction.
* When used from Scholar support job, Accession will triple spell recast.
The maximum number of targets affected by a spell enhanced by Accession is 15.
Compatible Spells:
* Cure I - IV
* Protect I - V
* Shell I - V
* Erase
* -na spells
* Regen I - V
* Refresh
* Adloquium
* Phalanx
* Stoneskin
* Blink
* Aquaveil
* Barspells
* Enspells
* -storm spells
* Animus Augeo
* Animus Minuo
* Invisible
* Sneak
* Deodorize
* Embrava

accomplice.description: Steals 50% of the target party member's enmity and redirects it to the thief.
accomplice.name: Accomplice
notes: Takes half the CE and VE of the target, as long as the user is within 20.6' of the monster.
Timer is tied to Collaborator's timer.

notes: Unknown Magic Accuracy bonus, probably equal to Flash 's +200.

activate.name: Activate
notes: If Deactivate is used when the automaton is at full HP, Activate's reuse timer will be set to 0:00.
If defeated, the automaton cannot be resummoned until the ability's recast timer is back to 0.
Cannot be used with Call Wyvern, Charm, any Summoner summon, or any other ability that allows use of a pet.

afflatus misery.description: Inspires you to draw strength from the damage you take.
afflatus misery.name: Afflatus Misery
notes: Afflatus Misery is one of two White Mage "stances" (along with Afflatus Solace) that bestows a number of bonuses. This stance grants bonuses intended for a front-line, meleeing white mage frequently in range of their party and enemies with bonuses for Esuna, Cura Spells, Banish Spells, and Auspice.
*Allows Esuna to remove up to 2 Status Effects, including the ones normally removed by Erase.
*Adds 1-10 HP healed to the base and increases the potency of Cura, Cura II, and Cura III proportionally to the most recent damage taken.
**Increases them at most to approximately the level of Curaga II, Curaga III, and Curaga IV, respectively, after a 200 damage hit, and resets after the modified spell is cast.
*Increases the potency of Banish Spells proportionally to the most recent damage taken.
**Caps at 300 magic damage, following a 200 damage hit, and resets after the modified spell is cast.
*Enhances Auspice by adding an Enlight effect to the White Mage's weapon along with an increasing Accuracy Bonus following a miss.
** Approximately 10 Accuracy per missed attack round.
** The Enlight effect only triggers on the first swing of each attack round.
** Damage determined by enhancing skill, calculated on hit.
Use will overwrite Afflatus Solace if active.
Not available via support job.

afflatus solace.description: Inspires you to draw strength from the healing spells you cast.
afflatus solace.name: Afflatus Solace
notes: Afflatus Solace is one of two White Mage "stances" (along with Afflatus Misery) that bestows a number of bonuses. This stance grants bonuses intended for a back-line, support white mage located away from their party and out of range of their enemies, with bonuses for Cure Spells, Barspells, Sacrifice, and Holy Spells.
* Grants a Stoneskin after "Cure" spells are cast equal to 25% of the HP that could have been restored by the spell. This does not apply to Curaga or Cura spells.
** This effect caps at 300 HP, but can be exceeded by any gear that enhances Afflatus Solace.
*** 2026-Feb-09: Testing with an m50 WHM indicates that this cap might now be 325. Need further testing with other WHM's of different levels to verify if this is exclusive to an m50 WHM.
** Gear which enhances Afflatus Solace affects all targets hit when used with Accession.
** Stoneskin strength: min(floor(0.25 * cure_potential), afflatus_solace_cap) + floor(gear_bonus * cure_potential)
*** where:
**** afflatus_solace_cap: 325?
**** gear_bonus: Afflatus Solace bonus from gear
**** cure_potential:  The HP that could have been restored by the spell
* Adds 5 Magic Defense Bonus of the related element to Barspells.
* Allows Sacrifice to transfer up to 7 status effects instead of just 1.
* Increases the base damage of Holy and Holy II proportional to HP healed since ability activation or last holy spell cast (casting Holy or Holy II resets the accumulated value).
** Base damage bonus caps out at 380, or 1300 HP cured.
The Cure spell and Barspell bonuses are enhanced by Orison Bliaud +1 and Orison Bliaud +2 (see below).
Will overwrite Afflatus Misery if it is active.
Not available via support job.

aggressor.effect: Decreases recast by 00:00:10 for every rank.

notes: Raises 25 accuracy and lowers evasion by 25.

In the past, Aggressor natively enhanced Ranged Accuracy. However, this effect on Ranged Accuracy was removed. Instead, the merit Job Trait Aggressive Aim allows Ranged Accuracy to partially be effected by Aggressor.

allies' roll.description: Enhances skillchain damage and skillchain accuracy for party members within area of effect.
allies' roll.name: Allies' Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using an Allies' Die.
The skillchain damage enhancement from this roll is added as Skillchain Bonus, and is subject to the related cap.

This roll's "skillchain accuracy" effect is no longer relevant. Skillchain damage could be resisted like magic damage, prior to the following change from the June 2014 Version Update: "Skillchains have been adjusted to that they may no longer be resisted, but their potency will now vary depending on the resistance values of the target." https://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/42614-Jun-17-2014-%28JST%29-Version-Update June 17, 2014 Version Update Notes

arise.name: Arise
notes: Restores 90% of lost experience points to targets level 50 or above upon revival. Targets below level 50 will only receive 50% of lost experience points.
Revived target will have weakness status for three minutes following revival and will be revived with 100% of the reduced maximum HP.
Revived target will have a full duration Reraise III effect bestowed upon them.

armor break.description: Lowers enemy's defense. Duration of effect varies with TP.
armor break.name: Armor Break
notes: Defense Down effect is -25% defense.
* Additional Effect is -based, so it is unlikely to land on  or  enemies.
* Duration has three resist states, so "unresisted duration" is shown above, "resisted duration" is half of that, and it is possible for the monster to fully resist.
** Duration scales continuously with TP, i.e. 1500 TP unresisted duration is 270 seconds.
Accuracy of WS additional effect has increased June 2017
Armor Break's Defense down effect can overwrite itself, regardless of current or new duration.
*Can actually reduce the duration if re-applied with less TP.
Armor Break's Defense down effect can still apply if WS damage is 0 depending on magic accuracy.
Armor Break's Defense down effect cannot override Full Break's Defense down.
* Full Break's weaker Defense down  effect can still overwrite Armor Break's Defense down effect.
Defense Down effects do not stack with each other.
*Dia, Box Step, Defense Down, and Geo-Frailty all stack together.

*Defense Down effects such as Acid Bolts, Angon, Spirit Surged Jumps, Tachi: Ageha, Full Break, Shell Crusher, Tenebral Crush, Sweeping Gouge, Tourbillion, and Frightful Roar, etc do not stack. Only one may be active at a time.

aspir.description: Steals an enemy's MP. Ineffective against undead.
aspir.name: Aspir
notes: MP drained can exceed MP absorbed (if your MP bar isn't empty enough), but won't be indicated in the chat log.

The potency is directly associated with dark magic skill: The maximum potential potency below 300 skill is (Dark Magic Skill/3 + 20)×(1+Potency) The maximum potential potency above 300 skill is (Dark Magic Skill)×0.4×(1+Potency)
The minimum potency is 50% of the maximum potency, and the actual potency of the spell (when unresisted) will randomly fall anywhere between the minimum and maximum possible potency.

aspir ii.description: Steals an enemy's MP. Ineffective against undead.
aspir ii.name: Aspir II
notes: MP drained can exceed MP absorbed (if your MP bar isn't empty enough), but won't be indicated in the chat log.
The potency is directly associated with dark magic skill: The maximum potential potency is approximately (Dark Magic Skill)×0.6×(1+Potency)
The minimum potency is 50% of the maximum potency, and the actual potency of the spell (when unresisted) will randomly fall anywhere between the minimum and maximum possible potency.

aspir iii.description: Steals an enemy's MP. Ineffective against undead.
aspir iii.name: Aspir III
notes: MP drained can exceed MP absorbed (if your MP bar isn't empty enough), but won't be indicated in the chat log.

The potency is directly associated with dark magic skill: Dark magic skill cap is either 600+ or uncapped. (Magic Attack Bonus) and (Magic Damage) have no effect on potency. The maximum potential potency appears to be approximately: (Dark Magic Skill)×0.8×(1+Potency)

The minimum potency is 50% of the maximum potency, and the actual potency of the spell (when unresisted) will randomly fall anywhere between the minimum and maximum possible potency. [?] Pixie Hairpin +1 is applied as a separate 1.28 multiplier after potency is applied.

notes: Aspirs approximately between 1 and 25%  of damage dealt in MP, up to some limit determined your weapon's effective delay.
*Target must have MP.
The various equipment that modifies Sambas is listed on the Samba category page.

aspir samba ii.description: Inflicts the next target you strike with Aspir daze, allowing all those engaged in battle to drain its MP.

notes: Forces a Triple Attack or Quadruple Attack on your next swing or Weapon Skill.

notes: Instantly recovers 100% MP.
Reduces Blood Pact recast timers to 0 upon use, and keeps them at 0 for the duration.

astral flow.description: Allows Avatars to use their full strength.
astral flow.name: Astral Flow
notes: While Astral Flow is in effect, perpetuation cost for any summon is zero. The Summoner's innate Auto Refresh trait and refresh equipment will therefore restore their MP.
Astral Flow unlocks powerful (but resistible) area-of-effect magical attacks for each avatar. These are found under Blood Pact: Rage.
Use of an Astral Flow ability will consume all MP. Minimum MP cost of an Astral Flow ability is Main Job Level * 2.
Odin and Alexander can only be summoned while under the effect of Astral Flow.

With the exception of the Astral Flow pacts from Odin and Alexander, which immediately end Astral Flow, it will remain in effect for three minutes, so it is possible to use several Astral Flow pacts before the two-hour ability's effect wears off.

asuran fists.name: Asuran Fists
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set MNK / PUP as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest The Walls of Your Mind
* Or while equipped with Kaja Knuckles or Karambit regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
** This is the only way for WAR / RDM / THF / DRK / BST / NIN / DNC to use the weapon skill.
The "" damage boost from Kaja Knuckles and Karambit applies to all hits in the Weapon Skill.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

asylum.description: Grants party members a powerful resistance to enfeebling magic and Dispel effects.
asylum.name: Asylum
notes: Players with the effect active will be nearly immune to all debuffs and dispelling effects.

atonement.description: Delivers a twofold attack.  Enmity varies with TP.
atonement.name: Atonement
notes: Category:Elemental Weapon Skill
Can only be used if you set PLD as your main job.
Damage dealt is based on current Enmity, as per the following formula, rounded up:
* CE  6 - 1 + VE  6 - 1 ≤ (mainhand item)Level  10
** e.g. A level 99 player can never deal more than 990 damage with Atonement; and with a 119 sword equipped, Atonement can never deal more than 1190 damage, regardless of how much hate a player has.
It deals  type damage in two hits, one for CE and one for VE, both will always hit whenever Atonement hits.
*Each hit's damage can not exceed 665 for a 119 mainhand sword.
**The total is still subject to the overall cap of: (mainhand item)Level  10
* Weapon Skill Damage equipment applies to both hits.
*Gear such as Ardor Pendant with "Breath damage dealt +n%" have no effect on Atonement.
Enmity gained is a fixed amount, regardless of the amount of damage dealt by .
Maximum damage does not vary with TP, but higher TP allows you to gain more Enmity from .
*This is multiplicative with other sources of enmity+, and caps at 2x at 3000 TP.
**With , this enmity multiplier is increased by 1; so at 1000 TP Atonement has an enmity fTP multiplier of 2x, and at 3000 TP Atonement's enmity fTP multiplier is 3x.
Elemental Gorgets and Elemental Belts do affect this WS, but they affect it by increasing the enmity multiplier similar to a TP Bonus.
* Each is approximately analogous to +100 TP Bonus each, so they increase the enmity bonus from fTP by +10% when used together.
* This is still capped at a 2x enmity multiplier, making Belts and Gorgets do nothing for  at 3000 TP.

atra. libations.description: Steals HP from enemies within range. Ineffective against undead .
atra. libations.name: Atra. Libations
notes: Amount drained is ( Blue Magic Skill x .11) x 9
Not enhanced by "Drain Potency + x" gear.

aurorastorm ii.description: Changes the weather around target party member to "auroras."

notes: Grants all players Subtle Blow +10. This counts towards the cap of +50 Subtle Blow I. Auspice overwrites and is overwritten by tier 1 Enspells . Tier 2 Enspells overwrite and prevent the application of Auspice.

When cast under the effect of Afflatus Misery , the caster (but not party members) will deal additional light damage on melee attacks and gain a cumulative physical accuracy bonus with each missed attack. Caps at +30 Accuracy. The accuracy bonus resets if you switch to Afflatus Solace and back to Afflatus Misery but does not reset if you reapply Auspice before it wears off.

avalanche axe.description: Delivers a single-hit attack. Damage varies with TP.

avatar's favor.description: Utilizes the avatar's power towards a beneficial status effect for party members within range but increases perpetuation cost.
avatar's favor.name: Avatar's Favor
notes: Effect is granted to any party member within 10' of the Avatar.
Potency of favor builds over time and is reset any time the avatar is defeated or dismissed.
* "Favor Potency+" Tier increases by one every 9s tock until the potency is capped, only the capped potency per skill tier is displayed below.
** Cap is determined by Summoning Magic Skill and currently equipped "Avatar's Favor"+ from gear and the 550 Job Point Gift (Avatar's Favor Effect).
*** Each ''"Avatar's Favor +1"'' increases maximum potency by 1 tier, regardless of your current summoning skill tier.
**** Current maximum obtainable potency is at the 735+ skill tier +6 bonus tiers.

**** Obtaining maximum potency depends only on your total skill and equipped "Avatar's Favor"+ in your idle gear, skill/favor+ worn during avatar summoning and Avatar's Favor activation do not matter.
* Using a Blood Pact will "charge" the current potency of Favor.
** The more MP that is consumed by the Blood Pact, the greater the increase, with Blood Pact: Rages increasing the potency more than Blood Pact: Wards.
* Maximum Favor potency depends on the skill cap tier of the avatar. Max value charge times are displayed below without using any blood pacts.
**Maximizing the Job Point category for Avatar's Favor will reduce this by 1 minute.
'''The Avatar does not receive the effects of Favor.'
=== Effect on the Summoner ===
While this ability is active, Avatar Perpetuation Cost is increased by 25% (floored) after subtracting perpetuation cost equipment.
* Formula: 'Perpetuation Cost during Avatar's Favor = FLOOR( (Avatar Base Cost - Equipment)  1.25 )'''
* Spirits are unaffected.
* Avatar's Favor will be free of cost if your adjusted Perpetuation Cost is less than 4:  e.g. FLOOR ( 3  1.25 ) = 3.
** For Carbuncle and Cait Sith, favor is free at -8 Perpetuation Cost equipment or better, up to -10.
** For Fenrir and Siren, favor is free at -10 Perpetuation Cost equipment or better, up to -12.
** For the 6 element avatars and Diabolos, favor is free at -12 Perpetuation Cost equipment or better, up to -14.
* Increasing the favor potency does not change the cost.
Avatar's Favor decreases Blood Pact Ability Delay up to 10 seconds, scaling with the current potency.
* Currently, the maximum reduction is 10 seconds, at Skill Tier 13's cap (20th Favor Potency+ tier).
* Without using Blood Pacts to speed up charging, -1 Blood Pact Delay is granted after each 18 seconds until reaching a summoning skill cap tier.
* Maximum potency for Blood Pact Delay reduction depends only on your total skill and equipped "Avatar's Favor"+ in your equipment worn as you send the Blood Pact command to the avatar.
** There's a 75 Delay pause before a pet executes the command which allows another /equipset command in the same macro time to increase the Blood Pact effect.
* The Blood Pact charges the timer as it activates, so summoners can opt not to idle in "Avatar's Favor"+ gear while capping Blood Pact Delay reduction.
=== Aura Potencies ===

bad breath.description: Deals earth damage that inflicts multiple status ailments on enemies within a fan-shaped area originating from the caster.
bad breath.name: Bad Breath
notes: Damage formula is (Current HP)/8 + (Level)/3

The status ailments inflicted seem to be of relatively weak potency. Also, their duration is short, usually lasting less than 1 minute with a shared timer. Inflicts 20% Slow Gravity effect is about -14% movement speed, which is weaker than the spell Gravity itself.
A positive Monster Correlation against Beasts will increase damage by 25% while a negative Monster Correlation against Vermin will decrease damage by 25%.
This spell is enhanced from equipment that increases breath damage and Convergence .

banish.description: Deals Light damage to an enemy.
banish.name: Banish
notes: The Banish line of spells reduces damage resistances from Undead mobs (e.g. attacks would do more damage versus Skeletons ). Damage resistance is reduced by 50%. That is, a Skeleton 's resistance will go from 50% to 25%, so it will take 75% of normal damage. Damage resistance reduction lasts 15 seconds.
Spell damage is increased by 50% against Undead mobs.

banish ii.description: Deals Light damage to an enemy.
banish ii.name: Banish II
notes: The Banish line of spells reduces damage resistances from Undead mobs (e.g. attacks would do more damage versus Skeletons ). Damage resistance is reduced by 70% (to 19/64, to be more precise). That is, a Skeleton 's resistance will go from 50% to 15%, so it will take 85% of normal damage. Damage resistance reduction lasts for 30 seconds.
Spell damage is increased by 50% against Undead mobs.

banish iii.description: Deals Light damage to an enemy.
banish iii.name: Banish III
notes: The Banish line of spells reduces damage resistances from Undead mobs (e.g. attacks would do more damage versus Skeletons ). Damage resistance is reduced by 97% (62/64, to be more precise). That is, a Skeleton 's resistance will go from 50% to 3%, so it will take 98.5% of normal damage. Damage resistance reduction lasts 45 seconds.
This effect will overwrite itself, as will all equal tier or higher banishes.
Spell damage is increased by 50% against Undead mobs.

Not currently available to players

barrage.name: Barrage
notes: Unlike multi-hit weapon skills, Barrage will not fire the next shot once the current hit misses the target; hence, it requires more accuracy to land the same number of hits compared to similar multi-hit weapon skills.
Barrage can fire up to 4 shots at level 30, 5 shots at level 50, 6 shots at level 75, 7 shots at level 90, and 8 shots with the 550 Gift.
* As of the May 10, 2011 Version Update: "the accuracy calculation will be eased with the maximum number of shots."

* Total number of shots firable by Barrage is currently 13 (level 99 [7], 550 gift [1], ASA Legs [1], Orion Bracers +3 [3], and Lionsquall/Phineus' Gun/Quelling Bolt [1]), but due to a lack of ilvl stats on ASA legs, using them in most situations isn't applicable on difficult content. In practice, a RNG will fire a max of 12 shots on difficult content, and non-Afterglow Gastraphetes owners 11 shots.
Barrage does not work on Throwing weapons.
Cannot be stacked with Unlimited Shot. Overwrites Unlimited Shot if used when Unlimited Shot is active.
Barrage is reported in the log as the sum of all fired shots, even if it passes the normal reported damage cap (99,999).
The hidden triple damage effect of Yoichinoyumi and Annihilator has a 13% chance to proc. If it procs, it affects all hits of Barrage.

Gives full TP return for each hit, and also gives full TP as if each hit landed to the enemy it was used upon.  Ammunition with additional on-hit effects do not proc during Barrage, so using high base damage ammunition is preferred.
* Due to this, after a successful Barrage, monsters will often perform TP moves and it can in some situations lead to death, so caution and communication are advisable in such situations.

==Translation/Localization==

"Metsu" means more than the other Katana Weapon Skills. The kanji for the WS in Japanese is 生者必滅, which means "All living things must eventually die". In short it could also mean "Return to nothing". In the English version, it is shortened from "shou ja hitsu metsu" to simply "Blade: Metsu".
生者必滅 is also Final Fantasy VII's Yuffie Kisaragi's second level 3 Limit Break, localized as "Doom of the Living" in the English version.

The bolded, italicized Skillchain property is only available while under the effects of Aeonic Aftermath.
Requires Katana Combat Skill level 397, Ninja at level 96 or above, and unlocking the 24px|link= Heart of the Bushin Key Item.

blade: teki.description: Delivers a water elemental attack. Damage varies with TP.
blade: teki.name: Blade: Teki

blade: ten.description: Delivers a single-hit attack.  Damage varies with TP.
blade: ten.name: Blade: Ten
notes: Can only be used if you set NIN as your main job.

blade: to.description: Delivers an ice elemental attack. Damage varies with TP.
blade: to.name: Blade: To

blade: yu.description: Delivers water elemental damage. Additional effect: Poison. Duration of effect varies with TP.
blade: yu.name: Blade: Yu
notes: Can only be used if you set NIN as your main job.
Poison effect is -10 HP/tick.
* Additional Effect is -based, so it is unlikely to land on  or  enemies.
* Duration has two resist states, so "unresisted duration" is shown above, and "resisted duration" is half of that.

blank gaze.description: Removes one beneficial magic effect from an enemy.
blank gaze.name: Blank Gaze
notes: This is a light based dispel gaze type attack. You do not need to be facing the enemy, but the enemy must be facing you for this spell to take effect.

blast arrow.description: Delivers a melee-distance ranged attack. Accuracy varies with TP.
blast arrow.name: Blast Arrow
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG as your main job.
Despite being an Archery weapon skill, it is only usable from within melee distance.
Despite being only usable from melee distance, it does not stack with Sneak Attack

blast shot.description: Delivers a melee-distance ranged attack. Accuracy varies with TP.
blast shot.name: Blast Shot
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG as your main job.
Despite being a Marksmanship Weaponskill, this must be used from melee range.

blastbomb.description: Deals fire damage to enemies within area of effect. Additional Effect: Bind
blastbomb.name: Blastbomb
notes: Bypasses shadow images.
Area of effect is based on the target.
The bind occurs after damage so it is possible to reapply it before it wears off.

blaze of glory.description: Increases the effects of your next applicable geomancy spell. Consumes half of that luopan's HP.
blaze of glory.effect: Reduces recast by 20 seconds per merit level.
blaze of glory.name: Blaze of Glory
notes: Luopan Potency +50% (rounded down), including the effect of Geomancy+ equipment.
* Stacks additively with Ecliptic Attrition.
* Potency increase does not stack with Bolster.
* Can be used with Bolster to get the full effect of a Widened Compass Luopan.
Reduces Luopan's current HP at the time of casting by 50%, but does not affect max HP.
Unlike Ecliptic Attrition, must be used before placing the Luopan you wish to enhance.

blaze spikes.description: Grants the player Fire Armor which does damage to the foe.
blaze spikes.name: Blaze Spikes
notes: Damage Dealt = floor ( floor ( (INT + 50) ÷ 12 ) × (1 + MAB ÷100 )
Potency (from INT) is determined at the time of casting, while Magic Attack Bonus is determined at the time of the hit that procs spikes.
Caps at 250 INT, or 25 base damage.

blazing bound.description: Deals fire damage to an enemy.
blazing bound.name: Blazing Bound
notes: Uses Magic Defense Bonus in place of Magic Attack Bonus to calculate damage done. This essentially negates the negative effects of Weakness . This means Magic Attack Bonus has no effect on the damage of the spell.
Unlike the version used via Limules this one does not apply a burn effect.

blind.description: Blinds an enemy, lowers its accuracy.
blind.name: Blind
notes: This spell reduces the target's accuracy based on player INT - enemy INT with this formula: Potency = (1/4)(dINT +80) for dINT > -39; Potency = (1/8)(dINT +120) for dINT <-40; potency caps at 5 and 50.
The NIN spell Kurayami: Ni and RDM spell Blind II will overwrite Blind; casting Blind when either have already landed on a mob will result in no effect.
The accuracy decreasing effect from Flash is different than the blind effect from Blind , Blind II , and Kurayami: Ni .

blind ii.description: Blinds an enemy, lowers its accuracy.
blind ii.name: Blind II
notes: Physical accuracy is decreased based on player INT - enemy INT . Lowest value at -80 difference between player INT and enemy INT: 19 Highest value at +120 difference between player INT and enemy INT: 94
The accuracy decreasing effect from Flash is separate from the blind effect from Blind , Blind II , and Kurayami: Ni and stacks with the effect.
+10 Magic Accuracy .

blinding fulgor.description: Deals light damage to enemies within range. Additional effect: Flash
blinding fulgor.name: Blinding Fulgor

blindna.name: Blindna
notes: Target can be any player character

blink.description: Creates shadow images that each absorb a single attack directed at you.
blink.duration: 5 minutes or until removed by attacks
blink.name: Blink
notes: Creates 2 shadow images per cast. Will overwrite itself. AoE abilities remove the shadows still. Unlike Utsusemi , Blink shadows will absorb entire multi-hit single-target physical TP moves if they proc, similar to Third Eye .
Proc rate of shadows is not 100%.

blistering roar.description: Enemies within an area of effect are frozen with fear.
blistering roar.name: Blistering Roar
notes: Can only be cast with job ability Unbridled Learning .

blitz.description: Delivers a fivefold attack. Damage varies with TP.
blitz.name: Blitz
notes: Prime Aftermath (Including Pets): Physical Damage Limit+

blitzer's roll.description: Reduces melee attack delay for party members within area of effect.
blitzer's roll.name: Blitzer's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Blitzer's Die.
The delay reduction from this job ability reduces TP per hit, unlike Haste.
*It still contributes to the 80% Delay reduction cap.
This delay reduction term is capped at -15%. It does not appear to be a part of any other delay reduction term.
|rollplus=+1%

blitzstrahl.description: Deals lightning damage to an enemy. Additional effect: "Stun."
blitzstrahl.name: Blitzstrahl

blizzaga.description: Deals Ice damage to enemies within area of effect.
blizzaga.name: Blizzaga

blizzaga ii.description: Deals Ice damage to enemies within area of effect.
blizzaga ii.name: Blizzaga II

blizzaga iii.description: Deals Ice damage to enemies within area of effect.
blizzaga iii.name: Blizzaga III

blizzaga iv.description: Deals Ice damage to enemies within area of effect.
blizzaga iv.name: Blizzaga IV
notes: Enemy-only spell used by high level Notorious Monsters.

blizzaja.description: Deals ice damage to enemies within area of effect. Successive use enhances spell potency.
blizzaja.name: Blizzaja

blizzara.description: Deals Ice damage to enemies within area of effect .
blizzara.name: Blizzara

blizzara ii.description: Deals Ice damage to enemies within area of effect .
blizzara ii.name: Blizzara II

blizzara iii.description: Deals Ice damage to enemies within area of effect .
blizzara iii.name: Blizzara III

blizzard.description: Deals Ice damage to an enemy.
blizzard.name: Blizzard

blizzard ii.description: Deals Ice damage to an enemy.
blizzard ii.name: Blizzard II

blizzard iii.description: Deals Ice damage to an enemy.
blizzard iii.name: Blizzard III

blizzard iv.description: Deals Ice damage to an enemy.
blizzard iv.name: Blizzard IV
notes: +20 Magic Accuracy .

blizzard v.description: Deals Ice damage to an enemy.
blizzard v.name: Blizzard V

blizzard vi.description: Deals Ice damage to an enemy.
blizzard vi.name: Blizzard VI

blood drain.description: Steals an enemy's HP. Ineffective against undead.
blood drain.name: Blood Drain
notes: HP Drained is Blue Magic Skill *.33.
Not modified by INT.

blood pact: rage.description: Orders the Avatar to use special attacks.
blood pact: rage.name: Blood Pact: Rage
notes: Summoning skill affects the accuracy and magic accuracy of rage Blood Pacts.
* There is an Accuracy and Magic Accuracy bonus based on the Summoning skill above your level's skill cap (parallel to the duration bonus on ward Blood Pacts).
* Summoners with a summoning magic skill lower than the skill cap will not experience any penalties, such as a decrease in accuracy or shortened effect durations.
The first hit of  and  Blood Pacts receive a +100 Accuracy Bonus.
Each damaging Blood Pact has a WSC just like the player's Blue Magic and Weapon Skills.
* Unlike Blue Magic and Weapon Skills, the WSC values change based on level.
See Blood Pact Ability Delay for a list of Blood Pact Ability Delay reduction gear.

blood pact: ward.command: /pet "(name of Blood Pact)"  or  depending on the bloodpact.
blood pact: ward.description: Orders the Avatar to use support abilities.
blood pact: ward.name: Blood Pact: Ward
notes: Blood Pact: Wards have different possible base durations, some of which are enhanced by skill.
* Instant - Enfeebling and Healing BPs.
* For 1, 2, and 3 minute duration BPs, every 1 Skill over 300 grants an additional second of duration.
* 15 Minute flat duration - Aerial Armor, Earthen Ward.
* 60 Minute flat duration - Reraise II.
** Job Points for Astral Conduit grant a 1% bonus to duration per job point. This is applied after the skill boost.
** Unlike other enhancements, Blood Pact: Wards do not suffer any level correction when used on low level party members.
Spell potency is fixed and unaffected by Summoning Magic Skill.
* Typically, it is a function of the player's level.
Summoning Magic Skill affects the magic accuracy of enfeebling Blood Pacts.
Area of Effects are centered on the Avatar and hits all party members within ~14 yalms.
See Blood Pact Ability Delay for a list of Blood Pact Ability Delay reduction gear.

blood rage.description: Enhances critical hit rate for party members within area of effect.
blood rage.name: Blood Rage
notes: Grants +20% Critical Hit Rate.
Overwrites and is overwritten by Warcry from Warrior main or sub job.
Is overwritten and prevented by Crimson Howl.

blood saber.description: Steals HP from enemies within range. Ineffective against undead.
blood saber.name: Blood Saber
notes: HP drained is ( Blue Magic Skill * .11) * 3.5
A positive or negative Monster Correlations only affect accuracy and not damage.
Elemental Affinity effects both accuracy and damage.

blood weapon.description: Allows you to drain your target's HP with your melee attacks.
blood weapon.name: Blood Weapon
notes: Drains target HP with melee attacks. Amount drained is same as damage dealt.
HP returned can be reduced on mobs with Shell or Phalanx.
Often combined with Souleater.

bloodrake.description: Delivers a threefold attack. Damage varies with TP. Additional effect: HP Drain.
bloodrake.name: Bloodrake
notes: May only be cast with job ability Unbridled Learning .
Possesses an attack boost.
Absorbs 100% of damage dealt
Inflicts damage to Undead targets but does not absorb HP from them.

bludgeon.description: Delivers a threefold attack. Accuracy varies with TP .
bludgeon.name: Bludgeon

body slam.description: Delivers an area attack. Damage varies with TP.
body slam.name: Body Slam
notes: Not learnable from all the Dragon Family monsters.
Apply Silence to increase chances for Body Slam to be used by the dragon.

bolster.description: Enhances the effects of your geomancy spells.
bolster.name: Bolster
notes: Doubles the potency of all Geomancy effects for the duration, including the effect of Geomancy+ equipment.
If used while an Indicolure is active it will retroactively grant the potency increase.
If used while a Geocolure is placed it will NOT retroactively buff the bubble; a new one must be cast to gain the effect.
Entrusted Spells DO NOT get the potency increase.
When Bolster wears off, both Indicolures and Geocolures lose their potency bonus, even if the Luopan is still alive.
* Character stats may not visibly reflect this on the client until an equipment or status change.
Potency increase does not stack with Blaze of Glory, but Blaze of Glory's effects will persist after Bolster wears off.
* Prevents the use of Ecliptic Attrition entirely.

bolter's roll.description: Increases movement speed for party members within area of effect.
bolter's roll.name: Bolter's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Bolter's Die.
Wears off when taking offensive action or being the target of an offensive action by an NPC
Crooked Cards will add 20% boost to the movement speed increase, applied after Roll+ gear .
Stacks multiplicatively with Movement Speed equipment and additively with Mazurka or Quickening. See Movement Speed for details.
*With equipment of 12% Movement Speed, you need at least 43% from Bolter's Roll to hit the 60% cap.
**With roll+5 Barataria Ring, you cannot cap without a Crooked Cards 11.
**With roll+6 Commodore's Knife, you need a roll of 11 .
**With roll+7 Regal Necklace or Lanun Knife, you need a roll of 3 or 11.
**With roll+8 Rostam, you need a roll of 3, 6, 7, 8, 10, or 11.
*With equipment of 18% Movement Speed, you need at least 36% from Bolter's Roll to hit the 60% cap.
**With roll+5 Barataria Ring, you need a roll of 3 or 11.
**With roll+6 Commodore's Knife, you need a roll of 3, 8, 10, or 11 .
**With roll+7 Regal Necklace or Lanun Knife, you need a roll of 3 or higher (except unlucky 9)
**With roll+8 Rostam, you need any roll (even unlucky 9)
|rollplus=4%

bomb toss.description: Throws a bomb at an enemy.
bomb toss.name: Bomb Toss

boost.description: Enhances the power of your next attack.
boost.duration: 4.0 x Your current attack delay. Minimum duration: 5 sec
boost.name: Boost
notes: Autoattack delay will triple while active.
* This is affected by various sources of delay reduction.
The next autoattack will deal increased damage and generate increased TP if it connects, but never multi-attacks.
* Damage is multiplied by (820/256 + STR/400) and 400% increase in TP for level 99 Monk main, combining the entire attack round into one swing (so 800% of one fist).
* Damage is multiplied by (444/256 + STR/400) and 200% increase in TP for Monk sub.
** Sources of additional base damage (like Sneak Attack or Trick Attack) are applied after this multiplication.
While active, the next weapon skill used will deal increased damage proportional to the time waited after activating Boost.
* For a level 99 Monk, this is approximately 1.4x damage if used at the end of the Boost timer.
* You do not receive the extra TP if a weaponskill is used.
Effect stacks with Weapon Skills and other damaging Job Abilities such as Jump.
Increases damage of Chi Blast (see page for details).
Effect immediately wears off if weapon is changed.

boost-agi.description: Enhances Agility for party members within area of effect.
boost-agi.name: Boost-AGI
notes: See Boost Spell Category Page for duration and potency calculation details, along with enhancing equipment.

boost-chr.description: Enhances Charisma for party members within area of effect.
boost-chr.name: Boost-CHR
notes: See Boost Spell Category Page for duration and potency calculation details, along with enhancing equipment.

boost-dex.description: Enhances Dexterity for party members within area of effect.
boost-dex.name: Boost-DEX
notes: See Boost Spell Category Page for duration and potency calculation details, along with enhancing equipment.

boost-int.description: Enhances Intelligence for party members within area of effect.
boost-int.name: Boost-INT
notes: See Boost Spell Category Page for duration and potency calculation details, along with enhancing equipment.

boost-mnd.description: Enhances Mind for party members within area of effect.
boost-mnd.name: Boost-MND
notes: See Boost Spell Category Page for duration and potency calculation details, along with enhancing equipment.

boost-str.description: Enhances Strength for party members within area of effect.
boost-str.name: Boost-STR
notes: See Boost Spell Category Page for duration and potency calculation details, along with enhancing equipment.

boost-vit.description: Enhances Vitality for party members within area of effect.
boost-vit.name: Boost-VIT
notes: See Boost Spell Category Page for duration and potency calculation details, along with enhancing equipment.

bora axe.description: Binds target. Chance of binding varies with TP.
bora axe.name: Bora Axe
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / DRK / BST / RUN as your main OR sub job.
A Ranged Attack, like Mistral Axe. Maximum distance for use slightly over 15'.

bounty shot.description: Increases the rate at which the target yields treasure.
bounty shot.name: Bounty Shot
notes: Base potency of Treasure Hunter Debuff effect level 2.

100% chance of proc if there is no Treasure Hunter already on the monster. If there is, there is a chance Bounty Shot will increase the level by one, similar to Sneak Attack or Trick Attack from a Thief main (with lower probability at higher levels of TH).

box step.description: Lowers a target's defense. If successful, will earn you finishing move.
box step.name: Box Step
notes: Applies a stackable debuff called Sluggish Daze, which can be applied up to 10 times (the current level is shown as the effect is applied to the target).
*May only reach up to level 5 when applied with a Dancer subjob.
*Box Steps past the first extend Sluggish Daze duration by 30 seconds, to a maximum of 2 minutes. (or +50 seconds, up to a maximum of 2m20 with job points)
**Lowers defense by 5% for the first level and 2% for each level after.
Grants 2 Finishing moves if it hits (can miss, be Blinked, or be Utsusemi-ed).
*Grants only 1 Finishing move if the target is already afflicted with Sluggish Daze (lv.10) or if used with Dancer support job.

brainshaker.description: Stuns target.  Chance of stunning varies with TP.
brainshaker.name: Brainshaker
notes: Stun effect is -based, so it is less likely to land on - or -based enemies.
Stun can be used to trigger  staggers on certain enemies.

brazen rush.description: Increases your chance to execute a double attack.
brazen rush.name: Brazen Rush
notes: The effect begins at 100% double attack rate and diminishes over the duration of the effect.

break.description: Petrifies an enemy, preventing it from acting.
break.name: Break
notes: The petrify effect from player-casted Break will end immediately if damage (except DoT ) is dealt to the petrified monster.
Interrupts all enemy actions; thus can be used as an alternative to Stun .
Petrified enemies cannot perform any actions; thus can be used as an alternative to Sleep .
Stacks safely with Sleep / Sleep II , so is a practical method to move from Sleep II -> Petrified -> Sleep / Sleep II with zero awake time.

breakga.description: Petrifies enemies within area of effect, preventing them from acting.
breakga.name: Breakga
notes: The petrify effect from player-casted Breakga will end immediately if damage (except DoT ) is dealt to a petrified monster.

building flourish.description: Enhances potency of next weapon skill. Requires at least one finishing move.
building flourish.effect: Increases Building Flourish accuracy by 2, attack power by 1%, and critical hit rate by 1%.
building flourish.name: Building Flourish
notes: Increases the strength of the next weapon skill used.
Boost is dependent upon the number of Finishing moves consumed.
*1 Finishing move gives Accuracy+40.
*2 Finishing moves give Accuracy+40 and Attack+25%.
*3 Finishing moves give Accuracy+40, Attack+25%, and Critical Hit Rate+10%.
With 20 Job Points in the relevant category, Building Flourish also grants 20% Weapon Skill Damage regardless of the number of Finishing Moves spent.
All bonuses (including WSD) apply to all hits of Weapon Skill.
The Critical Hit Rate bonus from using 3 Finishing Moves only affects weapon skills that can crit (unlike Striking or Climactic).
Notably while all the effects of Building Flourish normally do not apply to magical weapon skills the job ability enhancement of Weapon Skill Damage does (ex. when using Aeolian Edge).

bully.name: Bully
notes: Grants approximately a 15% Intimidation rate solo against Too Weak monsters, and generally doesn't work on Notorious Monsters.
*This intimidation rate is treated as an enfeeble on the monster and will apply regardless who the monster is attacking.
*Intimidation rate scales with party size.
Removes the directional requirement from Sneak Attack for main job Thieves when active.

burn.description: Deals Fire damage that lowers an enemy's intelligence and gradually reduces its HP.
burn.name: Burn
notes: Part of the Elemental Debuff family of spells.
Overwrites Frost
Is overwritten by Drown
Can be stacked with Choke and Shock or Rasp
Damage over time increases with Intelligence .

burning blade.description: Deals fire elemental damage to enemy. Damage varies with TP.
burning blade.name: Burning Blade

burst.description: Deals Lightning damage to an enemy and lowers its resistance against earth.
burst.name: Burst
notes: Earth Resistance -30 for 10 seconds after the spell lands.

burst affinity.description: While in effect, enables magical blue magic to Magic Burst.
burst affinity.duration: 00:00:30 or first blue magic cast, whichever first
burst affinity.name: Burst Affinity
notes: Doubles the WSC (Attribute Modifier) for the affected spell.
* Increases damage of the spell cast, even if not used to Magic Burst.
* Mavi Attire Set +2's set proc will triple this bonus, for a WSC multiplier of 4 (or 4.3 with Mavi Basmak +2).

burst ii.description: Deals Lightning damage to an enemy and lowers its resistance against earth.
burst ii.name: Burst II
notes: Earth Resistance - for seconds after the spell lands.

calamity.description: Delivers a single-hit attack. Damage varies with TP.
calamity.name: Calamity
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / BST as your main job.

call beast.description: Calls a beast by using the contents of a jug.
call beast.effect: Reduces recast by 10 seconds per merit level, for a minimum recast of 00:04:10.
call beast.name: Call Beast
notes: Call Beast requires a jug equipped in the ammo slot. It is consumed on use.
Pets will spawn 0-2 levels below the Beastmaster.
*This limitation is removed when reaching ilevel ranges or with Relic Gloves.
Level of pet has a cap specific to the jug it is summoned with (see Jug Pets).
*This cap can only be increased by Beast Affinity merits and Relic Gloves (Max 15 Levels).
*Pets with cap 99 or lower must be summoned with Relic Gloves equipped in order to reach ilevel ranges.
Level+ equipment (ie Gleti's Boots) only affects iLevel pets, and are not needed for Call Beast.

call wyvern.description: Calls a wyvern to fight by your side.
call wyvern.name: Call Wyvern
notes: Call Wyvern was the Dragoon's two-hour ability until it was replaced by Spirit Surge.
The Wyvern's job traits can be modified with equipment equipped at the time of use.
Any job trait modifications will persist even if the equipment is removed until the wyvern dies or is dismissed, or the dragoon zones.
*If the modifying equipment is worn while zoning, the job traits will persist.
If Dismiss is used while the Wyvern is at 100% HP, Call Wyvern's recast timer is reset to 00:00:00.
Only one Wyvern can be out at any time.
If the Dragoon is under the effect of Spirit Surge a Wyvern cannot be summoned until the effect wears off.
While alive the Wyvern gives a variety of effects to the Dragoon.
*20% Attack Bonus
*20% Defense Bonus
*10% Job Ability Haste
*15% Double Attack (Only possible though Job Points)

Attack Bonus and Defense Bonus potency begins at 10% but increases by 2% each time the Wyvern's 'attributes increase' for a maximum of 20%, the Haste effect begins at 0% but likewise increases in increments of 2% until it reaches 10%.
Source: http://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/43912-dev1229-Job-Adjustments

camlann's torment.description: Delivers a triple damage attack that ignores target's defense. Amount ignored varies with TP.
camlann's torment.name: Camlann's Torment
notes: Can only be used if you set DRG as your main job.
Cerastes, +1, +2, +3, and Daboya do not give Aftermath
This weapon skill gains the ability to close a Radiance skillchain with Aeonic Weapon Aftermath.

camouflage.effect: Decreases recast by 00:00:10 for every rank.
camouflage.name: Camouflage
notes: Camouflage can be handy in an emergency. However, its duration is far too random for it to be considered a reliable form of Invisible (Status).

Camouflage reduces the enmity generated by ranged attacks while it is in effect. While ranged weapon skills and abilities will immediately make the effect of Camouflage wear off, normal ranged attacks may still be used and have only a small chance to make the effect of camouflage wear off.

cannonball.name: Cannonball
notes: Cannonball uses the caster's defense as a modifier of its attack.

caper emissarius.description: Transfers enmity to a party member of your choice.
caper emissarius.name: Caper Emissarius
notes: All enmity from all party members will be transferred to the target party member.
*The target party member must actively be on the monster's hate list, otherwise, Caper Emissarius will have "No Effect".

carcharian verve.description: Enhances attack and magic attack. Reduces spell interruption rate.
carcharian verve.name: Carcharian Verve
notes: Can only be cast with job ability Unbridled Learning .
Attack Bonus of +20% and Magic Attack Bonus of +20 last for one minute. Attack Bonus do not stack with Nature's Meditation .
Aquaveil effect lasts 15 minutes.
Prevents 10 interruptions. Aquaveil + gear and Blue Magic Skill have no effect on interuptions absorbed.

carnage elegy.name: Carnage Elegy
notes: Attack Speed reduction caps at 50% (512/1024). Skill cap is < 818.

cascade.description: Grants a damage bonus to the next elemental magic spell cast based on TP consumed.
cascade.name: Cascade
notes: Grants a Magic Damage bonus equal to 10% of your current TP; this consumes all current TP.

caster's roll.description: Enhances "Fast Cast" effect for party members within area of effect.
caster's roll.name: Caster's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Caster's Die.
Fast Cast from this roll stacks normally with other sources.

cataclysm.description: Delivers an area attack that deals darkness elemental damage. Damage varies with TP.
cataclysm.name: Cataclysm
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / MNK / WHM / PLD / GEO as your main or sub job.

catastrophe.description: Drains target's HP. Bec de Faucon/Apocalypse: Additional Effect: Haste
catastrophe.name: Catastrophe
notes: Relic Aftermath: 10% Equipment Haste (+102/1024). When fully upgraded to Level 119 III, this is changed to 10% Job Ability Haste and Accuracy+15.
* Crisis Scythe does not give Aftermath
Bypasses Utsusemi, but not Third Eye.
30-70% of the damage done by Catastrophe is given to the player as HP, unless the target is Undead.

celerity.description: Reduces the casting time of your next White Magic spell by 50%
celerity.duration: 00:01:00 or first white magic cast, whichever first
celerity.name: Celerity
notes: Celerity grants -40% cast time and Spell Recast, additive with the -10% from Light Arts.
When used with any version of the Relic footgear, gains the ability to surpass the 80% recast reduction cap, to a maximum of 90%.

cesspool.description: Deals Water damage to enemies within range. Additional Effect: Plague
cesspool.name: Cesspool
notes: May only be cast with job ability Unbridled Learning or Unbridled Wisdom .
Plague effect is -5 MP/ tick and -100 TP/ tick for 60 seconds.

chain affinity.description: While in effect, enables physical blue magic to Skillchain and increases damage.
chain affinity.duration: 00:00:30 or first blue magic cast, whichever first
chain affinity.name: Chain Affinity
notes: Consumes all of the blue mage's TP after casting a physical spell while active.
The Skillchain property & TP effect vary by spell.
The ability will wear off 30 seconds after activation without consuming TP if no physical spell is cast.
Will double the WSC of the physical blue magic.
Skillchain Damage increase from Job Point ranks applies to the Skillchain Bonus term.
Chain Affinity+ equipment increases the base damage of the spell.
* Base damage increase applies to all hits.
* Unlike blue magic skill and weapon item level, the Chain Affinity+ base damage increase isn't limited to the spell's damage cap.
* Chain Affinity+ doesn't modify the Skillchain damage.
When Sekkanoki and Chain Affinity are both active, it will cause the spell to cost 1000 TP, but the Sekkanoki buff will not be consumed allowing for multiple weapon skill follow-ups.
* If you have less than 1000 TP, the spell still costs 1000 and your remaining TP will go into negative range.

chainspell.name: Chainspell
notes: While active, all spells will have a very short casting time and zero recast time.
Does not reset recast of spells cast before activating.
Spells can be interrupted while Chainspell is active. This includes being hit by your opponent, Character movement/resting, or by being Paralyzed, Silenced, or Stunned.
Useful for mass raising, warping or escaping quickly, or with Dark Knight or Black Mage Support Job for stunning.

chakra.description: Cures certain status effects and restores a small amount of HP to user.
chakra.name: Chakra
notes: The ability restores HP equal to the character's [ VIT2+ƒ(Lv,MaxHP) ](1+M)+20JPs.
*ƒ(Lv,MaxHP) is a function of the MNK's level and Max HP. If using MNK as a subjob, use the subjob level.
**Estimated to be: (MNK level+1)/5
**20% of Max HP at level 99.
**15% of Max HP at levels 74~78.
**10% of Max HP at levels 49~53.
**9% of Max HP at levels 44~48.
**7% of Max HP at level 35~38.

charm.description: Allows you to control your target.
charm.name: Charm
notes: The duration of the Charm effect depends on the level difference between the player and the enemy being charmed, the amount of Charm+ gear worn, and player CHR.
*See the Charm Duration page for more information, as well as Charm formula.

Charm accuracy is primarily based on the level difference between the player and monster. Thus, the success rate of Charm can be improved by increasing a player's CHR, Light/Apollo's Staff, and possibly Magic Accuracy.
*The exact impact of Light Staff/Iridal Staff or Apollo's Staff/Chatoyant Staff on Charm is unknown, but they are believed to increase Charm rate by 10% and 15% respectively.

* Charm, like melee attacks or most magic, is never 100% accurate. No matter how weak a mob is in relation to the player, there's always the chance of a failed charm. Similarly, assuming the monster is charmable, there's always a small chance to succeed.
* There does appear to be a dependence of Charm accuracy on Beastmaster main job level. See the references.
Some families of mobs have a stronger resistance to Charm than others. Most notably Slimes, but also Opo-opos, Flytraps, Raptors, Mandragoras, Sabotenders, Snow Hares and Slugs.
* This likely comes from a combination of Light resistance (like Sabotenders) and Resist Charm traits.
If Charm is used on an enemy that is immune, it will be bound for a short duration.

chi blast.description: Releases chi to attack an enemy.
chi blast.name: Chi Blast
notes: Chi Blast cannot miss and is impossible to resist, though certain monsters may take greatly reduced damage.
*It can be absorbed by Stoneskin and is affected by Phalanx.
This ability does not cause user or target to gain any TP.
The damage formula for Chi Blast is as follows:
*DMG = floor( floor( MND  Rand )  ( #Boost  BoostIncrease% + 1 ) )
** MND is the player's MND stat.
** Rand is a game-determined number between 0.5 to 1.0, represented to least to 10-bit precision.
** #Boost is the number of times the player has boosted.

** BoostIncrease% is the percentage increase in Chi Blast damage given by each Boost usage. This is always 4 times the percentage increase in Attack given by Boost, so without any enhancing gear, Boost will give +12.5% Attack and +50% BoostIncrease%.
Does Spirit Damage (aka Breath Damage and sometimes called Non-Elemental damage or propertyless damage).

cover.description: Allows you to protect party members by placing yourself between them and the enemy.
cover.effect: Increases duration by 00:00:04 for each rank.
cover.name: Cover
notes: This ability allows you to take damage for a party member who currently has hate, as long as you are standing between them and the enemy.
* Cover cannot intercept AoE attacks or spells. Both the PLD and covered player will be hit.
* While Cover can intercept ranged attacks and spells (occasionally), the cover target must be within melee range of the monster for attacks to be intercepted.
Duration is based on dSTAT between user and target. The formula is something like:
* 15 + min(max(floor((user VIT + user MND - target VIT*2)/4),0),15)
* Duration is determined on use. Gear can then be removed.
* Duration can be further modified by PLD Job Points, up to +20 seconds.
When a hit is intercepted the PLD gains a static +200 Cumulative Enmity, and the covered player's Cumulative Enmity is reduced by 10%.
* The hit must land. Hits that deal 0 damage count. Misses don't count. Shadows/Anticipates/Parries untested.
* Volatile Enmity is untested.
* JA activation does not trigger this effect.
