cursna.description: Removes curse and bane from target.
cursna.name: Cursna
notes: Target can be any player character

Although it is not indicated by the help text, Cursna also has a chance to remove Doom from player characters. Doom Removal Rate = Base Rate * (1+(y/100)) Base Rate = (10+(Total Healing Magic Skill / 30)) y = Total Cursna+ stat from gear Example: With 613 Healing Magic Skill, 70 Cursna+ on gear, rate of removal = 30.433333% * 1.7 = 51.74% Need additional testing to confirm how "Cursna effects received" stat fits into the formula, whether it is additive or multiplicative with the above formula. Healing Magic Skill affects the removal chance. At 0 skill, the chance is 10%. At 500 skill the chance is 26%. At 600 skill the chance is 30% and at 1000 skill the chance is 43% as per the new JP Dev post info. There is no known cap on how much Healing Magic Skill one can have to affect the removal rate. This may be tier based or formula based. Overall, equipment that increases the effects of Cursna (Above) will multiply with the base removal rate (26%) rather than simply adding to the percentage value. This effect is 1% for every +1 effect on equipment. With this, it is possible to raise the removal rate to 100%.
Cannot remove Zombie or Haunt , despite those also being Curse-type debuffs.

cutting cards.description: Decreases the recast times of party members' special abilities.
cutting cards.name: Cutting Cards
notes: The ability is targeted on one party member (can be self-targeted) and reduces the recast timer of the target party member's level 1 and level 96 SP Abilities by a percentage of the remaining recast dependent on the result of the die roll:
*1: 5% Reduction
*2: 10% Reduction
*3: 20% Reduction
*4: 30% Reduction
*5: 40% Reduction
*6: 50% Reduction

The Job Point Category "Cutting Cards Effect" is additive with the recast timer reduction from the rolled number. As an example, a Corsair who has mastered the Job Point Category "Cutting Cards Effect" at rank 20 with a roll of "1" would reduce the recast timer of their target by 25% (5% from the rolled number "1" + 20% from the job point category rank).

cyclone.description: Delivers an area of effect wind elemental attack. Damage varies with TP.
cyclone.name: Cyclone
notes: Can only be used if you set RDM / THF / BRD / RNG / NIN / DNC as your main or sub job.
Can be used at range (15 yalms) and the area of effect (8 yalms or closer) is target-centered.

dagan.description: Restores HP and MP. Amount restored varies with TP.
dagan.name: Dagan
notes: Can only be used if you set WHM as your main job.
Canne de Combat +1, +2, +3, and Rose Couverte do not give Aftermath
Self-targeted WS, so it can be used without claiming a monster in any situation where you can pull out your weapon (within 30' of a monster).

dagda.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Damage varies with TP.
dagda.name: Dagda
notes: Prime Aftermath:
Originally had INT/MND modifiers. Fixed in the [https://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/61562?p=659633&viewfull=1#post659633 March 2024 update].

dancer's roll.description: Gradually restores HP to party members within area of effect.
dancer's roll.name: Dancer's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Dancer Die.
|rollplus=2

dancing edge.description: Delivers a fivefold attack. Accuracy varies with TP.
dancing edge.name: Dancing Edge
notes: Can only be used if you set THF / DNC as your main job.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

dark arts.description: Optimizes black magic capability while lowering white magic proficiency. Grants a bonus to enfeebling, elemental, and dark magic.
dark arts.name: Dark Arts
notes: Along with Light Arts, Dark Arts is one of two grimoires available to scholars. Once activated, Dark Arts cannot be removed except by zoning, dying, or applying Light Arts.

Grants a bonus to Dark Magic, Elemental Magic, and Enfeebling Magic. The bonus is equivalent to the amount required to bring the skill ranking to a B+ grade. Magic skills that are above this rating will not be enhanced. Bonuses from merits are not included in this calculation. This also applies when subbed, skill ranking will raise to a B+ ranking of your MAIN job.

Reduces casting costs of black magic-based spells by 10% (rounded down, spells with casting cost of 9 MP or less are not affected). The MP cost of White Magic-based spells is increased in by 20% (rounded up).
Cuts effective casting time and recast of black magic spells by approximately 10%.
Increases casting time of white magic spells by approximately 20%.
Dark Arts provides a bonus to the Helix line of spells. [http://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/15845 Source]
* The bonus is (Job level/5)-3 damage (initial and per tick, +16 at level 99). [http://www.bluegartr.com/threads/106679-Test-Server-Findings?p=4829085&viewfull=1#post4829085 Source]
Recast timer can be reduced by up to 10 seconds with scholar-specific Grimoire Recast merits (2 seconds per merit).
There is no recast timer while Tabula Rasa is in effect.

dark carol.description: Increases resistance against darkness for party members within area of effect.
dark carol.name: Dark Carol
notes: Elemental Resistance caps at 80 before Carol-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥900.

dark carol ii.description: Increases resistance against darkness and sometimes nullifies darkness damage for party members within area of effect.
dark carol ii.name: Dark Carol II
notes: Elemental Resistance caps at 100, and Damage Nullification chance at 15% before Carol-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥900.

dark harvest.description: Delivers a dark elemental attack. Damage varies with TP.
dark harvest.name: Dark Harvest
notes: Magic Accuracy increased by the June 2014 Version Update

dark maneuver.description: Enhances the effect of dark attachments. Must have an animator equipped.
dark maneuver.name: Dark Maneuver
notes: Enhances dark attachments (IE: Mana Tank), and allows for the use of automaton job abilities granted by dark attachments.
Encourages Cannibal Blade weaponskill usage for Valoredge frame automatons.
Encourages enfeebling or dark magic spellcasting for mage-type automatons.
Does not grant any bonuses to your automaton's stats.
Gear that affects Maneuvers in general is listed on the Maneuver Category page.

dark orb.description: Deals dark damage to an enemy.
dark orb.name: Dark Orb

dark seal.description: Enhances the accuracy of your next dark magic spell.
dark seal.duration: Effect stays for 00:01:00 or the first spell.
dark seal.effect: Each merit level after the first reduces Dark Magic casting time by 10%.
dark seal.name: Dark Seal
notes: Enhances Magic Accuracy of the next Dark Magic spell cast, with no effect on potency.

dark spirit.description: Summons a Dark Spirit to fight by your side.
dark spirit.name: Dark Spirit

dark threnody.description: Reduces an enemy's resistance against Darkness.
dark threnody.name: Dark Threnody
notes: Dark Magic Evasion down caps at 50 before Threnody-enhancing gear. Skill cap is .

dark threnody ii.description: Reduces an enemy's resistance against Dark.
dark threnody ii.name: Dark Threnody II
notes: Dark Magic Evasion down caps at 160 before Threnody-enhancing gear. Skill cap is .

deactivate.name: Deactivate
notes: If Deactivate is used when your automaton is at full HP, the reuse timer on Activate will be set to 0:00.
If Deactivate is used after a monster is subjected to Flashbulb or Sharpshot's Ranged Attack and the enemy is still unclaimed it will lose interest and despawn only to reappear a moment later.

death.description: Consumes all MP. Has a chance to knock out the target. If Death fails to knock out the target, it will instead deal darkness damage. Ineffective against undead.
death.name: Death
notes: (MP×3 + Magic Damage)×(MAB/MDB)×(Affinity)×(Day/Weather) Unaffected by Zodiac Ring . Magic Damage term caps at 32 for Death.
Capped Magic Hit Rate appears to grant about a 30% chance of landing the Death effect on unresistant monsters.
If the spell does damage and the Death effect does not land, Occult Acumen will generate TP.
Spae. Coat +1 does not work with it.
Manafont and Manawell do not affect MP usage.

death blossom.description: Delivers a threefold attack that lowers target's magic evasion.  Chance of lowering target's magic evasion varies with TP.
death blossom.name: Death Blossom
notes: Can only be used if you set RDM as your main job.
Magic Evasion Down effect is thought to be about -10 Magic Evasion.
* Effect duration is 60 seconds.

death ray.description: Deals dark damage to an enemy.
death ray.name: Death Ray

death scissors.name: Death Scissors
notes: Unknown Attack bonus.

decimation.description: Delivers an aerial attack comprised of three hits. Accuracy varies with TP.
decimation.name: Decimation
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set WAR / DRK / BST / RNG / RUN as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest Axe the Competition.
* Or while equipped with Kaja Axe or Dolichenus in the main hand regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
WAR / BST cannot complete the Axe the Competition quest before level 71 due to the necessity of equipping Pick of Trials.
The "" damage boost from Kaja Axe and Dolichenus applies to all hits in the Weapon Skill.
This weaponskill has its fTP transferred across all hits, making Elemental Gorgets and Elemental Belts excellent neck and waist gear options.Category:fTP Replicating WS

decoy shot.description: Enmity transferred will fade over time from the point the ability is used until it is at the base value when the effect expires.
decoy shot.name: Decoy Shot
notes: 80% of base enmity generated by ranged attacks (not including enmity effects from gear or abilities) is transferred from the ranger to the target PC.
* If the ranger is equipped with -20 or more enmity as a from abilities (such as Camouflage) and/or gear, all enmity will be diverted to the target PC.
If there are multiple party members between the monster target and the ranger, the enmity will be transferred to the party member closest to the monster target.
Transfers hate alliance wide.
There is no upper limit on the enmity that can be transferred from Decoy Shot.
Does not work with WS or Barrage.
Does not work on pets.
Shares same slot as Hover Shot.

deep breathing.description: Enhances the effect of next wyvern breath.
deep breathing.effect: Increases Wyvern breath potency by 10% per merit level past the first.
deep breathing.name: Deep Breathing
notes: This ability doubles the potency (+100%) and increases the accuracy of the wyvern's next offensive/healing breath.
The increase stacks with the increase in strength of breaths from Wyrm Armet and Wyrm Armet +1.

defender.effect: Decreases recast by 00:00:06 for every rank.
defender.name: Defender
notes: Raises Defense by 25% and lowers Attack and Ranged Attack by 25%.
* Defense effect increases 2% at levels 50, 60, 70, 80, and 90, capping at +35% while the penalty is a static -25%.
Since the duration is the same as the recast, it can be used full-time while tanking.

delta thrust.description: Delivers a threefold attack on a single target. Additional effect: Plague
delta thrust.name: Delta Thrust
notes: Plague effect is -6MP/ tick and -100TP/ tick .

As with all Blue Magic additional effects, Plague does not always land on the target. This is especially true with Notorious and high level monsters. Magic Accuracy and Blue Magic Skill increase the odds of landing the additional effect.

Bug: In the English localization, the spell description is incorrect. As of June 2022, the spell description says "Delivers a threefold attack. Additional effect: Plague. Chance of effect varies with TP.". It should read "Delivers a threefold attack. Additional effect: Plague. Effect duration varies with TP." (difference bolded). The help text on the quick menu is correct.

dematerialize.description: Prevents your luopan from receiving damage.
dematerialize.effect: Reduces recast by 20 seconds per merit level.
dematerialize.name: Dematerialize

demoralizing roar.description: Weakens the attack of enemies within range.
demoralizing roar.name: Demoralizing Roar
notes: -20% Attack down for 30 seconds.
Wivre only use Demoralizing Roar attacking from the side of the enemy.

deodorize.description: Lessens chance of being detected by smell.
deodorize.name: Deodorize
notes: Duration is variable, but has a minimum of 5 minutes.
This spell can be used to lose the attention of monsters that track by Smell, similar to walking over water or having squalls active ( Water Water weather ), but it has no other purpose.
The Deodorize status will wear off immediately upon being attacked.

deploy.name: Deploy
notes: Must be used for your Automaton to use any abilities.
Depending on which type of head is equipped, the Automaton may either run up to engage the target or stay at a distance to cast or shoot.

desperate flourish.description: Weighs down a target with a low rate of success. Requires one finishing move.
desperate flourish.name: Desperate Flourish
notes: Instantly weighs the targeted enemy down, slowing its movement speed and reducing its evasion (-10) for a limited time.
Possesses an unquantified hit rate penalty, somewhere on the order of 10-20%.

despoil.description: Steals items from an enemy while inflicting a status ailment upon it.
despoil.name: Despoil
notes: Attempts to steal an item from the enemy, and if successful inflicts a status ailment on it for 90 seconds.
*Enfeeble potency is -10% (25/256).
You may steal an item with Despoil as well as Steal on the same enemy.
Generally takes a different item than Steal, and inflicts one enfeeble based on the item stolen.
* Enfeeble options may be based on the target monster's job.
* Slow, Evasion Down, Attack Down, Accuracy Down, and Defense Down are possible enfeebles for melee jobs.
* Magic Defense Down, Magic Accuracy Down, and Magic Attack Down are possible enfeebles for magic jobs.
The "Despoil Effect" Job Point category for Thief adds a TP drain effect of 2% per point, for a maximum of 40% of the target's TP stolen.
* This effect will work even if Despoil fails to steal an item. It does not return any TP if the target has none.
Unlike Steal, the items obtained do not change based on the zone or subspecies. Families share the same Despoil results.

*There may potentially be some variations in Adoulin areas as Despoil items were not initially available and were added later in an update. Overall the items seem to be the same, but this requires testing.
**Example: Despoiling Dragon Blood from Adoulin Bats, but confirmation is required on if this specific blood is universally able to be Despoiled. .
*Not all species have items that can be Despoiled. Evil Weapons, Elementals, etc have no Despoil results.

detonator.description: Delivers a single attack. Damage varies with TP.
detonator.name: Detonator
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG or COR as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest Shoot First, Ask Questions Later
RNG / COR may complete the Shoot First, Ask Questions Later quest at level 71, however the skill level required to use the Weapon Skill is obtained at levels 72 and 75, respectively.

deus ex automata.description: Calls forth your automaton in an unsound state.
deus ex automata.name: Deus Ex Automata
notes: Similar to Activate, but the Automaton appears with low HP.
*Automatron appears with:
:HP% = Puppetmaster Level  3

devotion.description: Sacrifices HP to grant a party member the same amount in MP.
devotion.effect: Increases MP restored by 5% for each rank beyond the first.
devotion.name: Devotion
notes: Sacrifices 25% of your current HP to restore an equal amount of MP to the target.
Stoneskin can mitigate HP loss, but will not lower the ability's potency.
With 5 ranks in Devotion, the total MP restoration value will be increased by 20%.
* MP granted will be equal to 30% of the caster's HP.
With 5 ranks in Devotion and any augmented version of the Relic headgear equipped, the total MP restoration value will be increased by 70%.
* MP granted will be equal to 42.5% of the caster's HP.

dextrous etude.name: Dextrous Etude
notes: Attributes cap at 9 before Etude-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥450.

dia.description: Lowers an enemy's defense and gradually deals Light elemental damage.
dia.name: Dia
notes: Gives 1 damage/tick Damage Over Time effect and -10.16% defense reduction (-104/1024).
Overwrites nothing. Is overwritten by Bio , Dia II , Bio II , Dia III , and Bio III .
This effect cannot be resisted except by monsters with Magic Shield , or those who are immune.

After Dia has been applied to the enemy, Corsairs can use the Quick Draw job ability Light Shot to enhance the Defense Down Effect by an additional +2.73% (28/1024) and Damage Over Time +1 (Caps after one shot)
An Exclusive version of this scroll exists in the game, and is provided to Red Mages when starting the game.

dia ii.description: Lowers an enemy's defense and gradually deals Light elemental damage.
dia ii.name: Dia II
notes: Gives 2 damage/tick Damage Over Time effect and -15.23% defense reduction (-156/1024).
Overwrites Dia and Bio . Is overwritten by Bio II , Dia III , and Bio III .
This effect cannot be resisted except by monsters with Magic Shield , or those who are immune.

After Dia II has been applied to the enemy, Corsairs can use the Quick Draw job ability Light Shot to enhance the Defense Down Effect by an additional +2.73% (28/1024) and Damage Over Time +1 (Caps after one shot)

dia iii.description: Lowers an enemy's defense and gradually deals Light elemental damage.
dia iii.name: Dia III
notes: Gives 3 damage/tick Damage Over Time effect and -20.31% defense reduction (-208/1024). Receives +1 damage/tick for each "Enfeebling magic effect" on equipment like Lethargy Sayon +1 and Regal Gem .
Overwrites Dia , Bio , Dia II , and Bio II . Does not overwrite Bio III .
This effect cannot be resisted except by monsters with Magic Shield, or those who are immune to all effects.

After Dia III has been applied to the enemy, Corsairs can use the Quick Draw job ability Light Shot to enhance the Defense Down Effect by an additional +2.73% (28/1024) and Damage Over Time +1 (Caps after one shot)

diabolic eye.effect: Increases Accuracy by 5 for each rank beyond the first.
diabolic eye.name: Diabolic Eye
notes: Gives Accuracy+20 (base) and HP-15% when active.
Due to the nature of the ability, some gear which activates during specific HP windows (i.e., Parade Gorget, which activates at 85%) may not activate properly.

diaga.description: Lowers defense of enemies within area of effect and gradually deals Light elemental damage.
diaga.name: Diaga
notes: Gives 1 damage/tick Damage Over Time effect and -10.16% defense reduction (-104/1024).
Overwrites nothing. Is overwritten by Bio , Dia II , Bio II , Dia III , and Bio III .
This effect cannot be resisted except by monsters with Magic Shield , or those who are immune.

After Diaga has been applied to the enemy, Corsairs can use the Quick Draw job ability Light Shot to enhance the Defense Down Effect by an additional +2.73% (28/1024) and Damage Over Time +1 (Caps after one shot)

diamondhide.description: Gives party members within area of effect the effect of "Stoneskin."
diamondhide.name: Diamondhide
notes: Stoneskin formula is (Blue Magic Skill/3) * 2 Caps at 500 skill.
Overwritten by, but does not overwrite Stoneskin , Earthen Ward , Rampart .
Unaffected by Stoneskin enhancement equipment.

Can only be learned from Armored Trolls (WAR/PLD/DRK) Will only use Diamondhide after weapon broken and switches to H2H Armored Trolls in Wajaom Woodlands and Halvung did not require their weapon to be broken to use Diamondhide .

diarmuid.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Damage varies with TP.
diarmuid.name: Diarmuid
notes: Prime Aftermath:  Physical Damage Limit

diffusion.description: Causes next support spell to hit party members in range.
diffusion.effect: Each merit rank after the first increases buff duration by 5%.
diffusion.name: Diffusion
notes: Spells function at full potency.
AoE is centered on caster and hits all party members within 9 yalms.
Duration of buffs is enhanced for all players when using the Mirage Charuqs +2  or the Luhlaza Charuqs/+1
Works only with the spells listed below:

diffusion ray.description: Deals light damage to enemies within a fan-shaped area originating from caster.
diffusion ray.name: Diffusion Ray

digest.description: Steals an enemy's HP. Ineffective against undead.
digest.name: Digest
notes: HP drained is Blue Magic Skill * .55
A positive or negative Monster Correlations only affect accuracy and not damage.

dimidiation.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Damage varies with TP. Epeolatry: Aftermath effect varies with TP.
dimidiation.name: Dimidiation
notes: Can only be used if you set RUN as your main job.
Can be unlocked for use on any Great Sword via the quest Rune Fencing the Night Away

disaster.name: Disaster
notes: Prime Aftermath:

dismiss.name: Dismiss
notes: If Dismiss is used while wyvern is at 100% HP, Call Wyvern's recast timer is reset to 00:00:00.

dispel.description: Removes one beneficial magic effect from an enemy.
dispel.name: Dispel
notes: This spell has an innate +175 magic accuracy bonus.

dispelga.description: Removes one beneficial magic effect from all enemies within area of effect.
dispelga.name: Dispelga

disseverment.description: Delivers a fivefold attack. Additional effect: Poison. Accuracy varies with TP.
disseverment.name: Disseverment
notes: This spell posses a -10% attack penalty.
Poison DoT is Level/5 + 3, capping at 22/ tick .

distract.name: Distract
notes: Grants evasion down based on the casters Enfeebling Magic skill and the differential in caster MND to target MND . Lowest value at ±0 MND compared to enemy: 0 Highest value at +50 MND compared to enemy: 10 When enfeebling magic skill is 125, players will reach the highest value of -25 physical evasion. The value from MND above will be added to this. Caps at -35 Evasion .
This spell has an innate magic accuracy bonus of +150
Unlike most other black magic spells, this spell relies on player dMND to target dMND for magic accuracy correction rather than dINT.
Added in the July 2014 Version Update .

distract ii.name: Distract II
notes: Grants evasion down based on the casters Enfeebling Magic skill and the differential in caster MND to target MND. Lowest value at ±0 MND compared to enemy: 0 Highest value at +50 MND compared to enemy: 10 When enfeebling magic skill is 350, players will reach the highest value of -40 physical evasion. The value from MND above will be added to this. Caps at -50 Evasion .
This spell has an innate magic accuracy bonus of +150.
Unlike most other black magic spells, this spell relies on player dMND to target dMND for magic accuracy correction rather than dINT.
Added in the July 2014 Version Update .

distract iii.name: Distract III
notes: This spell reduces the target's evasion based on dMND and Enfeebling Skill with this formula: Potency = Floor[(6/21)(Enfeebling Skill - 190)] + Floor(dMND/5); where dMND/5 caps at 0 and 10. Lowest value at ±0 dMND compared to enemy: 0 Highest value at +50 dMND compared to enemy: 10 When Enfeebling Magic skill is 610, players will reach the highest value of -120 evasion. The value from MND above will be added to this. Caps at -130 evasion , before enfeebling potency equipment.
This spell has an innate magic accuracy bonus of +150.
Unlike most other black magic spells, this spell relies on player dMND to target dMND for magic accuracy correction rather than dINT.

divine caress.description: If the next spell you cast cures a status ailment, your target will gain enhanced resistance to that ailment.
divine caress.duration: 00:01:00 or until the next appropriate spell cast
divine caress.name: Divine Caress
notes: If a status removal spell is casted on a target while the effect of Divine Caress is active. The targets will receive an effect which grants guaranteed immunity to the first corresponding enfeeble to the one removed for three minutes following successful status removal.
*The effect will also wear off after the enfeeble is resisted one time (or additional times with the enhancing equipment listed below).
Divine Caress does not apply to debuffs which are removed with the Erase spell, Esuna, or Sacrifice.
Grants the effect to all targets that had an enfeeble removed (via effects of Divine Veil or Accession).
Only one Divine Caress resistance effect can be active on a target at a time.

divine emblem.description: Enhances the accuracy of your next divine magic spell and increases enmity.
divine emblem.name: Divine Emblem
notes: Increases the Special Enmity Bonus of the next Divine Magic cast by 50%.
*Special Enmity Bonus is multiplicative with all other sources of Enmity+.
**e.g. with Enmity +66, Divine Emblem will increase that to an effective total Enmity +149 (1.66  1.5 = 2.49x or +149) for your next divine magic spell.
Gives a damage bonus (multiplier) to Holy and Banish spells equal to:
:Damage = floor(Normal Damage(1 + Divine Magic Skill100) )
Grants an unknown amount of magical accuracy to divine spells. [https://wiki.ffo.jp/html/19970.html]
Additionally, gives Holy II a 20~25 second Amnesia additional effect when cast on Undead monsters.
The equipment enhancements below are added on JA activation, and retained even if the gear is removed.

divine seal.description: Enhances the potency of your next healing spell.
divine seal.effect: Each merit level shortens recast by 00:00:20.
divine seal.name: Divine Seal
notes: Doubles the potency of the next cure spell cast.
After gaining the Divine Veil trait, this ability can be used to instead make the next status removal spell cast AoE.

divine waltz.description: Restores the HP of all party members within a small radius.
divine waltz.name: Divine Waltz
notes: Instantly heals nearby party members for approximately the same as Curaga, using the following formula:
*Amount cured = floor((Dancer's CHR + Receiver's VIT)/4) + 60HP
While flagged for Campaign Battle, this ability only hits party members that are also flagged for Campaign.
A variety of gear modifies the Waltz family, and it is listed on the category page.

divine waltz ii.description: Restores the HP of all party members within a small radius.
divine waltz ii.name: Divine Waltz II
notes: Instantly heals nearby party members for approximately the same amount as Curaga III, using the following formula:
*Amount cured = floor((Dancer's CHR + Receiver's VIT)*0.75) + 270HP
While flagged for Campaign Battle, this ability only hits party members that are also flagged for Campaign.
A variety of gear modifies the Waltz family, and it is listed on the category page.

dodge.effect: Each merit level reduces recast by 00:00:04
dodge.name: Dodge
notes: Raises user's evasion and provides an additive bonus to guarding rate based on job level.
* Evasion = Level + 1
**+100 at level 99.
*Guard rate bonus appears to be approximately: (Monk level + 1) * 0.2.
**+~20% at level 99.
**This bonus is additive to your guard rate and surpasses the cap.
*Capped Job Points add another 40 evasion.

dokumori: ichi.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Kodoku ". Poisons an enemy.
dokumori: ichi.name: Dokumori: Ichi
notes: Inflicts a 3 damage/tick Poison (Status) Damage Over Time effect.

doton: ichi.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Makibishi ". Deals earth damage to an enemy and lowers its resistance against wind.
doton: ichi.name: Doton: Ichi
notes: See the Ninjutsu page for more information

doton: ni.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Makibishi ". Deals earth damage to an enemy and lowers its resistance against wind.
doton: ni.name: Doton: Ni
notes: See the Ninjutsu page for more information

doton: san.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Makibishi ". Deals earth damage to an enemy and lowers its resistance against wind.
doton: san.name: Doton: San
notes: See the Ninjutsu page for more information

double shot.description: Occasionally uses two units of ammunition to deal double damage.
double shot.name: Double Shot
notes: Grants a 40% chance of firing two simultaneous shots when performing a Ranged Attack.
* Each shot that hits gives full TP return.
* The Ranger Job Point category increases the activation rate by an additional 20%.

Shares a recast timer with Unlimited Shot. With Unlimited Shot merits, using Unlimited Shot will reduce the recast of both abilities, but using Double Shot will not reduce the recast of either ability.Due to the much higher utility of Double Shot, Unlimited Shot merits are not considered useful.
* Can be stacked with Barrage, despite the fact that Unlimited Shot cannot.
Equipment which enhances Double Shot does not grant it when the ability is not active.

double thrust.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Damage varies with TP.
double thrust.name: Double Thrust

double-up.description: Enhances an active Phantom Roll effect that is eligible for Double-Up.
double-up.name: Double-Up
notes: After using Phantom Roll, you have 45 seconds to use Double-Up until you Bust.
Double-Up rolls another time, resulting in another possible value between 1 and 6 that is added to your previous total.
This ability is used to obtain rolls higher than 6, including the elusive 11.

drachen roll.description: Enhances pet accuracy for party members within area of effect.
drachen roll.name: Drachen Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Dragoon Die.
This roll also increases pet ranged accuracy.
|rollplus=5

dragon blow.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Damage varies with TP.
dragon blow.name: Dragon Blow
notes: This weaponskill looks the same as Howling Fist.
Unlike Howling Fist, however, this weapon skill does not have fTP transfer or an attack bonus.
Details on weapon skill properties from [http://wiki.ffo.jp/html/38158.html JP Wiki].

dragon breaker.description: Lowers accuracy, evasion, magic accuracy, magic evasion and TP gain for dragons.
dragon breaker.name: Dragon Breaker
notes: Target must be of the Dragon family or else it will not work.
Provides a flat -20 to an enemy's accuracy, evasion, magic accuracy, magic evasion, and Store TP.

dragon kick.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Damage varies with TP.
dragon kick.name: Dragon Kick
notes: Can only be used if you set MNK / PUP as your main job, or by the following jobs with the weapons listed below:
* WAR / RDM / THF / DRK / BST / NIN / DNC while wearing a Hepatizon Baghnakhs/+1
During Footwork, Dragon Kick uses Foot base damage instead of Hand base damage.
* This includes "Kick Attacks" attack+ armor like Shukuyu Sune-Ate.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

dragonfoe mambo.description: Enhances evasion for party members within area of effect.
dragonfoe mambo.name: Dragonfoe Mambo
notes: Evasion bonus caps at 48 before Mambo-enhancing gear. The table below assumes your magic skill is capped Skill cap is < 900

drain.description: Steals an enemy's HP. Ineffective against undead.
drain.name: Drain
notes: Damage done can exceed the HP absorbed (if your HP bar isn't empty enough), but won't be indicated in the chat log.

The potency is directly associated with dark magic skill: At 300 dark magic skill or below, the maximum potential potency is (Dark Magic Skill + 20) At 300 dark magic skill or above, the maximum potential potency is (Dark Magic Skill)×5/8 + 132.5
The minimum potency is 50% of the maximum potency, and the actual potency of the spell (when unresisted) will randomly fall anywhere between the minimum and maximum possible potency.

drain ii.description: Steals an enemy's HP, potentially increasing maximum HP. Ineffective against undead.
drain ii.name: Drain II
notes: Damage done by Drain II can provide a temporary boost to maximum HP if it would fill the caster's HP bar beyond the normal maximum. This HP Boost lasts for 3 minutes.

drain iii.description: Steals an enemy's HP, potentially increasing maximum HP. Ineffective against undead.
drain iii.name: Drain III
notes: Damage done by Drain III can provide a temporary boost to maximum HP if it would fill the caster's HP bar beyond the normal maximum. This HP Boost lasts for 3 minutes.

drain samba.description: Inflicts the next target you strike with Drain daze, allowing all those engaged in battle to drain its HP.
drain samba.name: Drain Samba
notes: Drains approximately between 1 and 50%  of damage dealt in HP, up to some limit determined your weapon's effective delay.
The various equipment that modifies Sambas is listed on the Samba category page.

drain samba ii.description: Inflicts the next target you strike with Drain daze, allowing all those engaged in battle to drain its HP.
drain samba ii.name: Drain Samba II
notes: Drains approximately between 1 and 75%  of damage dealt in HP, up to some limit determined your weapon's effective delay.
The various equipment that modifies Sambas is listed on the Samba category page.

drain samba iii.description: Inflicts the next target you strike with Drain daze, allowing all those engaged in battle to drain its HP.
drain samba iii.name: Drain Samba III
notes: Drains approximately between 1 and % of damage dealt in HP, up to some limit determined your weapon's effective delay.
The various equipment that modifies Sambas is listed on the Samba category page.

drakesbane.description: Delivers a fourfold attack. Chance of critical hit varies with TP.
drakesbane.name: Drakesbane
notes: Can only be used if you set DRG as your main job.

dread spikes.description: Covers you with magical darkness spikes. Steals HP from enemies that hit you. Ineffective against undead.
dread spikes.name: Dread Spikes
notes: When you receive a melee attack, Dread Spikes drain HP from your enemy equal to the damage dealt. Total is corrected by the enemy's Magic Damage Taken -% amount. The Drain effect can also be resisted. The full damage of the absorb counts towards the cap regardless of reduction/resists. Monsters that attack via psuedo-TP moves (like Iron Giants) are not affected.
Continues draining to counter applicable damage until it reaches a cap, at which point it wears off.
Based on your HP when casted. The cap is 50% of your max HP when the spell is cast, so wearing HP+ gear increases potency. Potency increases by 10% for a total of 60% with 1,200 Job Points spent.
DRK's 1,200 Job Points Dread Spikes trait is added at the end of the formula. Formula should be as follows: Current HP x 0.5 x (1 + Equipment Modifiers) + Job Point Trait.
Please refer to the above equipment table for information on how equipment modifiers interact with Dread Spikes.

dream flower.description: Puts all enemies within range to sleep.
dream flower.name: Dream Flower
notes: Area of Effect is centered around the caster, like most Blue Mage spells.
Is not overwritten by BRD Lullaby. Does not overwrite BRD Lullaby either.
Does not overwrite Sleep II , Sleepga II , or itself.
Interrupts all enemy actions; thus can be used as an alternative to Stun .

droning whirlwind.description: Deals Wind damage to enemies within area of effect. Additional effect: Dispel.
droning whirlwind.name: Droning Whirlwind
notes: May only be cast with job ability Unbridled Learning .
Does not appear to be able to be resisted by monsters.
Assumed to be a full dispel based on removing all player status enhancements in PvP.

drown.description: Deals Water damage that lowers an enemy's strength and gradually reduces its HP.
drown.name: Drown
notes: Part of the Elemental Debuff family of spells.
Overwrites Burn
Is overwritten by Shock
Can be stacked with Rasp and Frost or Choke
Damage over time increases with Intelligence .

dulling arrow.description: Lowers enemy's INT. Chance of critical varies with TP.
dulling arrow.name: Dulling Arrow
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG as your main or sub job.
Additional effect gives INT-.
Accuracy of WS additional effect has increased June 2017 [http://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/52747?p=597349#post597349 SE official]

eagle eye shot.description: Delivers a powerful and accurate ranged attack.
eagle eye shot.name: Eagle Eye Shot
notes: Single-target, high accuracy, fivefold damage shot.
Low Enmity.
This attack can Critical Hit.
Ignores Blink and Utsusemi.

earth carol.description: Increases resistance against earth for party members within area of effect.
earth carol.name: Earth Carol
notes: Elemental Resistance caps at 80 before Carol-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥900.

earth carol ii.description: Increases resistance against earth and sometimes nullifies earth damage for party members within area of effect.
earth carol ii.name: Earth Carol II
notes: Elemental Resistance caps at 100, and Damage Nullification chance at 15% before Carol-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥900.

earth crusher.description: Delivers an area of effect earth attack. Damage varies with TP.
earth crusher.name: Earth Crusher
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / MNK / WHM / PLD / GEO as your main or sub job.

earth maneuver.description: Enhances the effect of earth attachments. Must have an animator equipped.
earth maneuver.name: Earth Maneuver
notes: Enhances earth attachments (IE: Armor Plate), and allows for the use of automaton job abilities granted by earth attachments.
Increases your automaton's Vitality. See Maneuver for details.
Gear that affects Maneuvers in general is listed on the Maneuver Category page.

earth spirit.description: Summons a Earth Spirit to fight by your side.
earth spirit.name: Earth Spirit

earth threnody.description: Reduces an enemy's resistance against Earth.
earth threnody.name: Earth Threnody
notes: Earth Magic Evasion down caps at 50 before Threnody-enhancing gear. Skill cap is .

earth threnody ii.description: Reduces an enemy's resistance against Earth.
earth threnody ii.name: Earth Threnody II
notes: Earth Magic Evasion down caps at 160 before Threnody-enhancing gear. Skill cap is .

ebullience.description: Enhances the potency of the next Black Magic spell.
ebullience.duration: 00:01:00 or first black magic cast, whichever first
ebullience.name: Ebullience
notes: Gives a 20% damage bonus to your next Black Magic.

ecliptic attrition.description: Enhances the effects of your luopan. Increases the rate at which your luopan consumes its HP.
ecliptic attrition.effect: Reduces recast by 10 seconds per merit level.
ecliptic attrition.name: Ecliptic Attrition
notes: Luopan Potency +25% (rounded down), including the effect of Geomancy+ equipment.
* Stacks additively with Blaze of Glory.
* Cannot be used while under the effect of Bolster.
Increases Luopan's HP consumption by 6 HP/tick, decreasing lifespan from 70 ticks (210s) to 56 ticks (168s).
Operates on a shared recast timer with Lasting Emanation.
* Stacks with Lasting Emanation if the recast is up.
Unlike Blaze of Glory, only affects Luopans which have already been placed.

efflux.description: If the next spell you cast is a "physical" Blue magic spell, a TP bonus will be granted.
efflux.duration: 00:01:00 or first blue magic cast, whichever first
efflux.name: Efflux
notes: Grants a 1,000 TP Bonus to the next physical Blue Magic spell without consuming TP like Chain Affinity.
*Unlike Chain Affinity though, a player's current TP is not taken into account when using Efflux without Chain Affinity.
Efflux also increases the base damage of the spell it is used with by 50% (x 1.5).
Activates secondary effects of spell such as "Critical hit rate" or "Accuracy", etc  "varies with TP".
Stacks with Chain Affinity.
*To be more precise the WSC bonus and the base damage bonus stack together along with the TP bonus.
*Any TP bonus from Efflux that would surpass 3,000 is lost.
* TP bonus from Efflux does not stack with Enchainment (takes which ever is higher)

elemental seal.description: Enhances the Magic Accuracy of the player's next spell.
elemental seal.effect: Each merit level shortens recast by 00:00:20.
elemental seal.name: Elemental Seal
notes: Dramatically increases the Magic Accuracy of the next spell cast (somewhere on the order of +256).
Grants access to the spell Meteor, if the player has learned it.

elemental sforzo.description: Grants immunity to all magic attacks.
elemental sforzo.name: Elemental Sforzo
notes: With maximum Job Points in its category this ability will enable the player to absorb 40% of the damage a magic attack would have normally dealt.

elemental siphon.description: Drains MP from your summoned spirit.
elemental siphon.name: Elemental Siphon
notes: The formula for MP recovery = floor( floor( Summoning Magic Skill × 1.05 - 55 + Elemental Siphon Equipment ) × ( 1.0 ± Weather and Day factors ) ) + Elemental Siphon Effect Job Points.
*Weather and Day bonus caps at +40% (1.4) with no negative cap.
*The minimum amount of MP returned from Siphon is 50.
*This ability caps at 700 Summoning Magic Skill for a base 680 MP.
*This ability transfers MP from your Elemental to yourself and thus the MP restored cannot exceed the elemental's current MP.
For example, a 99 Summoner with 474 skill and 10/20 Elemental Siphon Effect JP ranks using Beckoner's Pigaches +1, Conveyance Cape, and a Chatoyant Staff with matching day and weather would recover:
===Weather and Day Factors===
Day, weather, and the subsequent equipment which enhance their effects for siphon may either enhance siphon (matching element to the spirit) or decrease (opposing element) this factor.

Elemental Staves (eg. Thunder Staff) have no effect on this ability.

embalming earth.description: Deals Earth damage to enemies within range. Additional effect : Slow
embalming earth.name: Embalming Earth
notes: Slow effect is -25%

embolden.description: Enhances the effects of the next enhancing magic spell you cast, but reduces effect duration.
embolden.name: Embolden
notes: Increases Enhancing Magic potency by 50% and decreases duration by 50% for spells cast on yourself.
* Combining Embolden and Accession will still only give the bonus to yourself.
*Potency bonus can be increased to 70% via Job Points.
The ability description is deceiving, as the spell can be self-cast or by another player. It should read "...magic spell cast on you...".
The potency enhancement is applied before buff gear enhancements like "Phalanx+".
*So (base buff*embolden bonus)+gear bonuses
*Example:If you have a base Phalanx of 34 and +17 Phalanx+ gear, you'd do (34*1.5)+17=68

embrava.description: Consumes 20% of your maximum MP. Gradually restores target party member's HP and MP, increases attack speed and ranged attack speed.
embrava.name: Embrava
notes: To learn this spell, speak to Erlene in the Eldieme Necropolis (S) at (J-8) while on Scholar . (Your SCH must be level 5 or higher.)

This spell is only accessible to SCH while under the effect of the SP Ability Tabula Rasa . Should Tabula Rasa wear off while you are casting this spell, you will get a message stating that you cannot cast this spell when the casting completes.

Embrava is a single status effect with four components which scale with enhancing magic skill, each of which cap at 500 skill: Regen : Potency = Enhancing Magic ÷7 + 1 HP/tick, rounded down (72 HP/tick maximum) Refresh : Potency = Enhancing Magic ÷100 + 1 MP/tick, rounded down (6 MP/tick maximum) Haste : Potency = Enhancing Magic ÷20 + 1 Haste (25.9% haste maximum 266/1024) Flurry : Potency = Enhancing Magic ÷20 + 1 Flurry (25.9% flurry maximum 266/1024)
Perpetuance can be used to extend Embrava's duration.
Accession allows Embrava effects to be extended to the target's full party within the AoE. It increases the MP cost to 40% of maximum MP.
Penury can be used to reduce the MP cost to 10% of maximum MP or back down to 20% if Accession is also being used.
Embrava can be cast on any player, it is not a party-only spell.
All stackable forms of Regen , Refresh , Haste , and Flurry will stack with Embrava's effects as it is a unique status effect.
Can not be removed by forms of Dispel , though it can be absorbed by Extreme Purgation .
The effect of Embrava is overwritten by (and overwrites) the effect of Kaustra .

empty thrash.description: Delivers an area attack. Accuracy varies with TP.
empty thrash.name: Empty Thrash

empyreal arrow.description: Delivers a single attack. Damage varies with TP.
empyreal arrow.name: Empyreal Arrow
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set RNG as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest From Saplings Grow
* Or while equipped with Kaja Bow or Ullr regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
** This is the only way for WAR / RDM / THF / PLD / DRK / BST / SAM / NIN to use the weapon skill.
Rangers may complete the From Saplings Grow quest at level 71, however the skill level required to use the Weapon Skill is obtained at level 72.

enaero.description: Adds wind damage to your attacks.
enaero.name: Enaero
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enaero ii.description: Adds wind damage to your initial attacks and lowers the target's resistance against ice.
enaero ii.name: Enaero II
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enblizzard.description: Adds ice damage to your attacks.
enblizzard.name: Enblizzard
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enblizzard ii.description: Adds ice damage to your initial attacks and lowers the target's resistance against fire.
enblizzard ii.name: Enblizzard II
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enchanting etude.name: Enchanting Etude
notes: Attributes cap at 9 before Etude-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥450.

endark.description: Adds darkness damage to your attacks.
endark.name: Endark
notes: Gives an Additional Effect: Darkness damage to your attacks that depends on your Dark Magic Skill at the time of casting.
The Additional Effect potency decays by 1 damage per hit until the effect wears off.
Also grants an attack bonus equal to the current Additional Effect potency.
Job Points put into enhancing this effect also grants an accuracy bonus equal to the level of the effect (1~20)

endark ii.description: Adds darkness damage to your attacks.
endark ii.name: Endark II

energy drain.description: Steals enemy's MP. Amount stolen varies with TP.
energy drain.name: Energy Drain
notes: Can only be used if you set RDM / THF / BRD / RNG / NIN / DNC as your main or sub job.
Does not do damage.
This Weapon Skill steals MP equal to: (floor( Dagger Skill  0.11 ) + WSC)  MP Multiplier
* See table above for MP Multiplier value which varies by TP
The target must have MP
This can empty a mob of MP

energy steal.description: Steals enemy's MP. Amount stolen varies with TP.
energy steal.name: Energy Steal
notes: Does not do damage.
This Weapon Skill steals MP equal to: (floor( Dagger Skill  0.11 ) + WSC)  MP Multiplier
* See table above for MP Multiplier value which varies by TP
The target must have MP
This can empty a mob of MP

enervation.description: Lowers the defense and magical defense of enemies within range.
enervation.name: Enervation
notes: Defense -10% (26/256) and Magic Defense Bonus -8.

Defense Down effects do not stack with each other. Dia , Box Step , Defense Down , and Geo-Frailty all stack together. Defense Down effects such as Acid Bolts , Angon , Spirit Surged Jumps, Tachi: Ageha , Full Break , Shell Crusher , Tenebral Crush , Sweeping Gouge , Tourbillion , and Frightful Roar , etc do not stack. Only one may be active at a time.
Can only be learned from light-armored Trolls (MNK/RDM/RNG/PUP)

enfire.description: Adds fire damage to your attacks.
enfire.name: Enfire
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enfire ii.description: Adds fire damage to your initial attacks and lowers the target's resistance against water.
enfire ii.name: Enfire II
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enlight.description: Adds light damage to your attacks.
enlight.name: Enlight
notes: First hit damage increases every 20 Divine Magic Skill , following this formula
Decreases by 1 point of damage per successful hit.
Grants an Accuracy bonus equal to the current damage of the en-effect.
Total damage dealt by Enlight (assuming no resists) can be determined using

enlight ii.description: Adds light damage to your attacks.
enlight ii.name: Enlight II
notes: First hit damage/accuracy bonus (D) determined by the following formulae: Up to 500 skill: D = 2×floor( (Divine Magic Skill+85)÷13 ) + floor( (Divine Magic Skill+85)÷26 ) + Enlight Job Points Above 500 skill: D = 2×floor((Divine Magic skill+400)÷20)+ floor((Divine Magic skill+400)÷40) + Enlight Job Points
No known skill cap. Tested at up to 600 skill.
Like Enlight , decreases by 1 point of damage per successful hit.

enlightenment.description: Optimizes both white and black magic capabilities and allows access to both addenda for your next spell.
enlightenment.duration: Effect stays for 00:01:00 or the first spell cast.
enlightenment.effect: Each additional merit grants a 5 INT and MND bonus to spells cast with Enlightenment active.
enlightenment.name: Enlightenment
notes: Allows casting of a spell from Addendum: White and Addendum: Black regardless of which Arts is active.
*Any spell cast will receive the normal penalties or bonuses from the active Arts.

enmity douse.description: Reduces the target's enmity towards you.
enmity douse.name: Enmity Douse
notes: Sets the targeted enemy's Enmity values to 1 Cumulative Enmity and 0 Volatile Enmity for the user.

enstone.description: Adds earth damage to your attacks.
enstone.name: Enstone
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enstone ii.description: Adds earth damage to your initial attacks and lowers the target's resistance against wind.
enstone ii.name: Enstone II
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enthunder.description: Adds lightning damage to your attacks.
enthunder.name: Enthunder
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enthunder ii.description: Adds lightning damage to your initial attacks and lowers the target's resistance against earth.
enthunder ii.name: Enthunder II
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

entomb.description: Deals earth damage to enemies within range. Additional effect: Petrification
entomb.name: Entomb
notes: Extremely potent petrify effect that easily last one minute.
Can be used on enemies that cannot be sleep easily like fomor in Sortie .
The effect is applied after the damage and therefore can be used to keep enemies petrified for a long period of time.
Especially useful to refresh sleeping enemies by waking them up and instantly petrifying them allowing to safely reapply sleepga .

entropy.description: Delivers a fourfold attack. Converts some of the damage into MP. Damage varies with TP.
entropy.name: Entropy
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / DRK / BST as your main job.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.
MP Recovery is about 15~25% of damage dealt.
* Is not considered an Aspir effect, and works against Undead enemies.
Ignores Utsusemi.
The bolded, italicized Skillchain property is only available while under the effects of Aeonic Aftermath.

entrust.description: Causes the next indicolure spell cast to be able to target a party member.
entrust.duration: 1 minute or until next Indicolure spell
entrust.name: Entrust
notes: Allows the next Indicolure spell to be cast on a party member.
*An Indicolure spell cast on another party member with Entrust active will not factor in Geomancy+ equipment from the caster.
*Indicolure potency is instead only affected by the Geomancer's combined Geomancy and Handbell skill.
*The Entrusted Indicolure spell is in addition to any other standard Indi/Geocolure spells.
Duration of the Entrust Indicolure is increased with Indicolure duration enhancement.

enwater.description: Adds water damage to your attacks.
enwater.name: Enwater
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

enwater ii.description: Adds water damage to your initial attacks and lowers the target's resistance against lightning.
enwater ii.name: Enwater II
notes: Please see the Enspell page for more information.

equanimity.description: Your next black magic spell will generate less enmity.
equanimity.duration: Effect stays for 00:01:00 or the first Black Magic spell cast.
equanimity.effect: Each additional merit rank reduces Enmity by a further -10.
equanimity.name: Equanimity
notes: Grants -10 Enmity per merit rank, to a maximum of -50 Enmity.
*Effect is separate from equipment Enmity reduction, and does not contribute to the cap.

erase.description: Removes one detrimental magic effect from the target party member.
erase.name: Erase
notes: Can remove: Bio , Dia , Gravity , Flash , Addle , Slow , Elegy , Requiem , Helix Spells , Elemental Debuffs , Attribute or Status Down effects, and various other negative status effects.
Cannot remove any effects which have another associated status removal spell (e.g. Poison )
Cannot remove: Terror , Amnesia , Muddle , Curse (Recovery)

erratic flutter.name: Erratic Flutter
notes: Grants Haste II , ~29.98 Haste (307/1024)
While it does not overwrite most forms of Slowga , Slow II , Slow II TP moves, Erratic Flutter does overwrite Hojo: Ni , Hojo: Ichi , and Slow .

escape.description: Transports party members within area of effect out of a dungeon.
escape.name: Escape
notes: Drops you outside of any area that is considered a "dungeon," including all indoor areas and beastman strongholds and the like.

esuna.description: Removes one enfeebling effect you have from yourself and party members within area of effect. Afflatus Misery : Increases effect removal potency.
esuna.name: Esuna
notes: Removes 1 negative Status Effect from the casting White Mage and anyone within 10' that is normally removed by Viruna , Cursna , Blindna , Poisona , or Paralyna . White Mage must have the negative Status Effect in order to remove it. Commonly cast after using Sacrifice to transfer status ailments to the white mage.

With Afflatus Misery active, it can remove up to 2 Status Effects , including those normally removed by Erase . In addition to removing spells removed by Erase , Esuna can also remove the effect of Curse (Recovery) .
Does not remove any enfeebling songs (such as Threnody, Elegy and Nocturne etc..)

evisceration.description: Delivers an attack comprised of five hits. Critical hit rate varies with TP.
evisceration.name: Evisceration
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set WAR / RDM / THF / BST / BRD / RNG / NIN / COR / DNC as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest Cloak and Dagger.
* Or while equipped with Kaja Knife or Tauret in the main hand regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
** This is the only way for PUP to use the weapon skill.
THF / COR / DNC cannot complete the Cloak and Dagger quest before level 71 due to the necessity of equipping Dagger of Trials.
The "" damage boost from Kaja Knife and Tauret applies to all hits in the Weapon Skill.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

evoker's roll.description: Gradually restores MP for party members within area of effect.
evoker's roll.name: Evoker's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Summoner Die.
'''Won't''' make you slowly lose MP on a bust but will cut any source of Refresh you get by -1 if busted.

evryone. grudge.description: Deals dark damage to an enemy.
evryone. grudge.name: Evryone. Grudge
notes: Before attempting to learn this spell, consider a Tonberry Hate Reset , otherwise you may be one-shotted by it.

exenterator.description: Delivers a fourfold attack that decreases target's accuracy. Duration of effect varies with TP.
exenterator.name: Exenterator
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / RDM / THF / BST / BRD / RNG / NIN / COR / DNC as your main job.
The Accuracy Down effect is -20 Accuracy.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.
The bolded, italicized Skillchain property is only available while under the effects of Aeonic Aftermath.

expiacion.description: Delivers a twofold attack.  Damage varies with TP.
expiacion.name: Expiacion
notes: Can only be used if you set BLU as your main job.

exudation.description: Attack varies with TP. Idris: Aftermath effect varies with TP.
exudation.name: Exudation
notes: Can only be used if you set GEO as your main job.
Can be unlocked for use on any Club via the quest Geomancerrific

exuviation.description: Restores HP and removes one detrimental magic effect.
exuviation.name: Exuviation
notes: Only removes status ailments removed by the spell Erase .
Soft cap of 85 HP recovered regardless of how many status ailemnts are on the target, unlike the Wamouras that it is learned from.
Can be used in conjunction with Diffusion which multiplies the enmity by the number of targets it hits.

eyes on me.description: Deals dark damage to an enemy.
eyes on me.name: Eyes On Me
notes: Ignores Copy Image , but can be absorbed by Blink (Status) .
Positive Monster Correlation against Dragons .

familiar.description: Enhances your pet's powers and lengthens the duration of charm.
familiar.name: Familiar
notes: In order to use this ability a player must have a pet.
Either a charmed pet or one acquired via Call Beast/Bestial Loyalty may be enhanced by this ability.
Increases Pet Max HP by 10%.
*Familiar bonuses affect Jug Pets for their entire duration.
This ability increases Charm Duration by 30 minutes regardless of level difference.
* Will not extend the stay of pets summoned through Call Beast or Bestial Loyalty past their normal time.

fan dance.description: Reduces physical damage taken and increases enmity but renders Samba unusable. Physical damage reduction gradually decreases with each hit taken.
fan dance.effect: Each merit level past the first reduces Waltz recast by 5%.
fan dance.name: Fan Dance
notes: Enmity +15.
The physical damage reduction effect begins at 90% damage reduction when activated or reapplied (if it is already active).
*This damage resistance does not count towards the Physical Damage Taken cap of 50%, but is multiplied with it and rounded down.
*Phalanx effects are applied after this damage reduction.
Every successful physical hit on the Dancer will reduce the effectiveness of Fan Dance by 10%, until it reaches the minimum of 20% damage reduction.
Activating this Job Ability automatically cancels out any Samba effect and the effect of Saber Dance, and prevents the player from using Sambas until it wears off or is cancelled.
The effects of Fan Dance on Waltz efficiency can be seen on the Waltz Efficiency page.

fantod.name: Fantod
notes: This Buff is similar to Monk's Boost; it only works on the next immediate attack.
This buff caps after 10 uses.
Stacks with other attack and magic attack spells such as Memento Mori , Plenilune Embrace , Triumphant Roar , Nature's Meditation , etc.
Affects both physical and magical Blue Magic, but not the damage of breath spells.
Boosts attack by +2.7-29.7% and magic attack by +2-20 depending on the number of casts

fast blade.description: Two-hit attack. Damage varies with TP.

fast blade ii.description: Two-hit attack. Damage varies with TP.

fealty.description: Grants a powerful resistance to enfeebling magic.
fealty.effect: Increases duration by 00:00:05 per merit level past the first.
fealty.name: Fealty
notes: This ability allows a PLD to resist not only enfeebling magic, but also added effects from monster TP moves, Charm, and some forms of non-elemental damage like Mijin Gakure and Meteor.
* It does not make you resist Dispel, and can be dispelled.
* Resists Elegies and Lullabies, but not other songs.

feather barrier.name: Feather Barrier
notes: +25 evasion .

feather step.description: Lowers a target's critical hit evasion. If successful, will earn you a finishing move.
feather step.name: Feather Step
notes: Applies a stackable debuff called Bewildered Daze to the target, stacking up to a maximum of 10 times. (When the effect is applied, the current level is shown in the log.)
*Feather Steps past the first extend Bewildered Daze duration by 30 seconds, to a maximum of 2 minutes.
*Increases Critical Hit Rate by 1% per level to a maximum of 10%.
Grants 2 Finishing moves if it hits (can miss, be Blinked, or be Utsusemi-ed).
Grants only 1 Finishing move if the target is already afflicted with Bewildered Daze (lv.10).

feather storm.description: Additional effect: Poison. Chance of effect varies with TP.
feather storm.name: Feather Storm

feather tickle.name: Feather Tickle
notes: Reduces TP by a random amount between 0 and 1500, without correlation bonuses.

feint.description: Your next attack will greatly reduce an enemy's evasion.
feint.duration: Next successful attack or 00:01:00, whichever first.
feint.effect: Increases chance of Treasure Hunter level increase by 25% for each rank beyond the first.
feint.name: Feint
notes: Evasion -150, which degrades by 10 each tick until wearing off at 50.
The Evasion Down effect lasts for 30 seconds once a feint-enhanced blow lands.
Feint is often utilized for "zerging" certain monsters, due to the severe nature of its Evasion Down effect.

Despite the somewhat ambiguous wording, additional merits into Feint increase the Treasure Hunter level up rate by 25% for each merit beyond the first rank. The Treasure Hunter level up rate can be increased to a maximum of +100% at 5/5 merits into Feint, or doubling of the base rate while the enemy has the Feint debuff active. It does not automatically make the hit which applies Feint to also level up Treasure Hunter.

fell cleave.description: Delivers an area attack. Radius varies with TP.

notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / DRK / RUN as your main job.
Radius scales to 5y at 2334 TP.

feral howl.effect: Increases the ability's accuracy by 5 per merit level past the first.
feral howl.name: Feral Howl
notes: This ability can miss and have no effect. The chance to miss appears to be based on level. Higher-level mobs tend to be unfazed by Feral Howl.
* Some Notorious Monsters are immune.
* Fire-based monsters also tend to be immune.

The below potency information comes via a now-defunct JP website. It is unclear where they got the information, but the tools to measure Double Attack with such accuracy did not exist in the time of the website. Take the numbers below with a grain of salt.

final sting.description: Deals damage proportional to HP. Reduces HP to 1 after use. Damage varies with TP.
final sting.name: Final Sting
notes: Unlike Self-Destruct this spell does not inflict weakness to the caster.
Damage is proportional to the HP of the caster.

Damage potential caps on a particular target if your current HP > 1/2 target's max HP. The lower the mob level the higher the cap damage: Max of 65% of monster's HP in damage to anything that checks "Too weak to be worthwhile" to weaker "Decent challenge" foes. Max of 50% of monster's HP in damage on harder "Decent challenge" to "Even match" foes.
Possesses properties of both physical and magical blue magic. You may skillchain with Chain Affinity , but resist rates on damage are light based.
Melee accuracy has nothing to do with the spell landing.
This spell ignores Utsusemi Shadow Images .
If the spell misses ("fails to activate") the user will lose no HP.

Mechanics
The way Flashy Shot affects damage is by adding D damage to the attack. [https://wiki.ffo.jp/html/3000.html]

Instead of ignoring Level Difference Penalty, this provides an equivalent damage increase based on level difference: designed to allow the ability to work in HTBF and UNM content. "The ability no longer ignores level difference penalties. Instead, a bonus to range accuracy and damage is granted based on the level difference between the target and the player." June 25, 2015 Version Update https://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/47481-Jun-25-2015-%28JST%29-Version-Update

foil.name: Foil
notes: Overwritten by some forms of Evasion Down .

Provides a substantial amount of Evasion when facing "Special Attacks" (starting at 150 evasion). This effect decays at a rate of 3 evasion loss per tick. "Special Attacks" typically means "Monster TP moves." It is unclear whether this works on the normal swings of monsters like Iron Giants . Since it is a self-targeting spell, Foil is an excellent way of quickly gaining Enmity on multiple targets. This is especially useful after using an AoE ability like Geist Wall or Sheep Song . Potency does not seem to depend on Enhancing Magic Skill . It is unknown what the floor of the evasion potency decay is.

formless strikes.description: While in effect, melee attacks will not be considered physical damage.No effect on weapon skills.
formless strikes.effect: Each merit level after the first reduces the 40% damage penalty by 5%.
formless strikes.name: Formless Strikes
notes: This ability changes auto-attacks from physical damage to spirit damage/breath damage (the same as Requiescat and Chi Blast).
Attacks with Formless Strikes active are less powerful than physical attacks. Normal damage is reduced to 60% when initially unlocked.
Your spirit-based auto-attacks will not be affected by physical resistances or barriers (Invincible.) It will now be affected by spiritual damage resistance.
Attacks can be "resisted".
It is best used against monsters that resist physical attacks, such as Elementals.
Formless Strikes attacks still give the user and the enemy normal TP.
This ability does not affect your Weapon Skills or counterattacks. They will have the same properties they had before activation.

frazzle.name: Frazzle
notes: Grants magic evasion down based on the casters Enfeebling Magic skill and the differential in caster MND to target MND . Lowest value at ±0 MND compared to enemy: 0 Highest value at +50 MND compared to enemy: 10 When enfeebling magic skill is 125, players will reach the highest value of -25 magical evasion. The value from MND above will be added to this. Caps at -35 magic evasion.
Added in the July 2014 Version Update .
This spell has an innate magic accuracy bonus of +150
Unlike most other black magic spells, this spell relies on player dMND to target dMND for magic accuracy correction rather than dINT.

frazzle ii.name: Frazzle II
notes: Grants magic evasion down based on the casters Enfeebling Magic skill and the differential in caster MND to target MND. Lowest value at ±0 d MND compared to enemy: 0 Highest value at +50 d MND compared to enemy: 10 When enfeebling magic skill is 350, players will reach the highest value of -40 magic evasion. The value from MND above will be added to this. Caps at -50 magic evasion.
Added in the July 2014 Version Update .
This spell has an innate magic accuracy bonus of +150.
Unlike most other black magic spells, this spell relies on player dMND to target dMND for magic accuracy correction rather than dINT.

frazzle iii.name: Frazzle III
notes: This spell reduces the target's magic evasion based on dMND and Enfeebling Skill with this formula: Potency = [Floor((6/21)(Enfeebling Skill - 205))] + Floor(dMND/5); where dMND/5 caps at 0 and 10. Lowest value at ±0 dMND compared to enemy: 0 Highest value at +50 dMND compared to enemy: 10 When Enfeebling Magic skill is 625, players will reach the highest value of -120 magical evasion. The value from MND above will be added to this. Caps at -130 magic evasion .
This spell has an innate magic accuracy bonus of +150.
Unlike most other black magic spells, this spell relies on player dMND to target dMND for magic accuracy correction rather than dINT.

notes: After learning the spell Indi-Acumen and reaching level 50 Geomancer , head to (J-7) (North of the pond) in Beaucedine Glacier and click the Geomantic Reservoir while on Geomancer to learn Geo-Acumen . Unity Warp 128 Fei'Yin
With 0 skill (lowest potency): +3 Magic Attack Bonus

With 900 skill (maximum potency): +15 Magic Attack Bonus Eminent Bell grants an additional: +9 Magic Attack Bonus Dunna and Nepote Bell grant an additional: +15 Magic Attack Bonus Idris grants an additional: +30 Magic Attack Bonus

geo-agi.description: Enhances Agility for party members within area of effect.

notes: After learning the spell Indi-AGI and reaching level 43 Geomancer , head to (H-8) of Map 6 on the second floor of Castle Oztroja and click the Geomantic Reservoir while on Geomancer to learn Geo-AGI . Enter Castle Oztroja (the fastest way is to take the Survival Guide ). Pass through the Brass Door at (I-8) on Map 1 . Go up the stairs to Map 2 then navigate to F (on the modified map) at (G-7). This will put you on Map 6 on the catwalk above an outdoor courtyard. Directly ahead of you (to the west) is the Geomantic Reservoir at (H-8).
With 0 skill (lowest potency): +1 agility
With 900 skill (maximum potency): +25 agility Eminent Bell grants an additional: +6 agility Dunna and Nepote Bell grant an additional: +10 agility Idris grants an additional: +20 agility

Easiest to zone in from Port Jeuno (Home Point #2) rather than teleporting directly to Qufim .

With 900 skill (maximum potency): -14.8% defense Eminent Bell grants an additional: -8.1% defense Dunna and Nepote Bell grant an additional: -13.5% defense Bagua Charm +1 grant an additional: -16.2% defense Bagua Charm +2 grant an additional: -18.9% defense Idris grants an additional: -27% defense

geo-fury.description: Enhances attack for party members within area of effect.
geo-fury.name: Geo-Fury
notes: After learning the spell Indi-Fury and reaching level 38 Geomancer , head to the northwest corner of (K-6) in Sauromugue Champaign and click the Geomantic Reservoir (in the northwest hole) while on Geomancer to learn Geo-Fury . The reservoir is at the bottom of a crater. The level 125 Unity warp will place you close to this point at (J-6)
With 0 skill (lowest potency): +4.6% attack and ranged attack

With 900 skill (maximum potency): +34.7% attack and ranged attack Eminent Bell grants an additional: +8.1% attack and ranged attack Dunna and Nepote Bell grant an additional: +13.5% attack and ranged attack Idris grants an additional: +27% attack and ranged attack

geo-gravity.description: Weighs down enemies within area of effect and lowers their movement speed.
geo-gravity.name: Geo-Gravity
notes: After learning the spell Indi-Gravity and reaching level 92 Geomancer, head to (I-7) in Foret de Hennetiel and click the Geomantic Reservoir while on Geomancer to learn Geo-Gravity . Take FS warp and head southwest. It's next to the castoff point.
With 0 skill (lowest potency): -0.1 movement speed

With 900 skill (maximum potency): -0.7 movement speed Eminent Bell grants an additional: -0.2 movement speed Dunna and Nepote Bell grant an additional: -0.3 movement speed Idris grants an additional: -0.5 movement speed

Movement speed reduction is not proportional to the monster's base movement speed. i.e. a Beetle and a Manticore get the same amount slower even though the Manticore starts much faster. The official forums explanation gave a relative measurement that was accurate but was only relevant to a monster with a base movement speed of 4.
Potency caps at -2.0 movement speed

geo-haste.description: Increases attack speed for party members within area of effect.
geo-haste.name: Geo-Haste
notes: After learning the spell Indi-Haste and reaching level 97 Geomancer , head to (J-6) in Marjami Ravine and click the Geomantic Reservoir while on Geomancer to learn Geo-Haste . From the Frontier Station head southwest to a Scalable Area and descend down the vine. Head north to a cave and ascend another vine. Proceed northeast and scale down the last vine to reach an area with a waterfall. Make a left out of the cave to reach the Geomantic Reservoir located in the NE corner of (J-6).
With 0 skill (lowest potency): +2.4% Attack Speed

With 900 skill (maximum potency): +29.9% Attack Speed Eminent Bell grants an additional: +3.3% Attack Speed Dunna and Nepote Bell grant an additional: +5.5% Attack Speed Idris grants an additional: +11% Attack Speed
Added in the July 2014 Version Update .

geo-refresh.description: Gradually restores MP for party members within area of effect.
geo-refresh.name: Geo-Refresh
notes: After learning the spell Indi-Refresh and reaching level 34 Geomancer , head to (H-10) in La Theine Plateau via Ordelle's Caves exit 3 and click the Geomantic Reservoir while on Geomancer to learn Geo-Refresh .
Quickest access: La Theine Plateau Proto-Waypoint . Nexus Cape can be used to reach this if someone can escort you.

Alternative access: Ordelle's Caves Survival Guide Warp, this places you on Map 1. Take the East path at the fork which will take you to Map 3(B), then take the exit path leading south at (H-9)(D) which will lead you back to Map 1. From here, go to the SE corner room and drop down the hole at southern border of (H-11), putting you on (H-11) of Map 3. Watch out for sight-aggro level 83-89 beetles. Go to the small path leading East to reach Map 2 and exit to La Theine Plateau at (G-10). The Geomagnetic Reservoir is south of a Geomagnetic Fount in the same area.
With 0 skill (lowest potency): +1 MP per tick

With 900 skill (maximum potency): +6 MP per tick Eminent Bell grants an additional: +3 MP per tick Dunna and Nepote Bell grant an additional: +5 MP per tick Idris grants an additional: +10 MP per tick

geo-regen.description: Gradually restores HP for party members within area of effect.

You will pass through an underground tunnel and come out into the Ravine that spans (J-5)/(K-5). In the lower left hand corner of (K-5), click the Geomantic Reservoir (in the middle of the purple ball flowers) while on Geomancer to learn the spell. The level 99 Unity warp ( Ironhorn Baldurno ) will place you at (H-8) of La Theine Plateau fairly close to the ravine entrance at (H-7).
