geohelix.description: Deals earth damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.
geohelix.name: Geohelix

geohelix ii.description: Deals earth damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.
geohelix ii.name: Geohelix II

glory slash.description: Delivers an area attack that deals triple damage. Additional effect: Stun. Damage varies with TP.
glory slash.name: Glory Slash
notes: Only available while participating in a Campaign Battle while equipped with a Lex Talionis.

goblin gavotte.description: Enhances resistance against bind for party members within area of effect .
goblin gavotte.name: Goblin Gavotte
notes: Unknown potency and unclear applications.

goblin rush.description: Delivers a threefold attack. Accuracy varies with TP.
goblin rush.name: Goblin Rush
notes: Gains 35% attack boost.

goddess's hymnus.description: Grants reraise to party members within area of effect .
goddess's hymnus.name: Goddess's Hymnus
notes: Skill has no bearing on potency

godfry sandbox.description: Lowers an enemy's defense and gradually deals Light elemental damage.
godfry sandbox.name: Godfry sandbox

gold capriccio.description: Enhances resistance against petrification for party members within area of effect .
gold capriccio.name: Gold Capriccio
notes: Unknown potency and unclear applications.

grand pas.description: Allows flourishes to be performed without finishing moves.
grand pas.name: Grand Pas
notes: Resets Flourish recast timers upon activation and eliminates the recast times and Finishing Move costs of Flourishes.
The effect ends after 30 seconds or once three Flourishes are used.

grand slam.description: Delivers an area attack. Damage varies with TP.
grand slam.name: Grand Slam
notes: Additional TP during Chain Affinity does not directly increase fTP . It is possible that TP increases attack .
AoE is centered 5' around the caster, rather than the target.

gravity.description: Weighs an enemy down and lowers its movement speed.
gravity.name: Gravity
notes: Lowers movement speed of the target by about -26%.
The potency of Gravity is not enhanced via Enfeebling Magic Skill or player stats, but instead only by the Red Mage job ability Saboteur and " Enfeebling Magic effect + " equipment.
Added in the July 8th, 2002 Version Update .
Prior to the addition of evasion-reducing debuff Distract in the July 2014 Version Update , Gravity would reduce a target's evasion by 10.

gravity ii.description: Weighs an enemy down and lowers its movement speed.
gravity ii.name: Gravity II
notes: Lowers movement speed of the target by about -32%. The duration can last over 1 minute.
The potency of Gravity is not enhanced via Enfeebling Magic Skill or player stats, but instead only by the Red Mage job ability Saboteur and " Enfeebling Magic effect + " equipment.
Added in the December 14th, 2011 Version Update .
Prior to the addition of evasion-reducing debuff Distract II in the July 2014 Version Update , Gravity II would reduce a target's evasion by 25.

ground strike.description: Delivers a single attack. Damage varies with TP.
ground strike.name: Ground Strike
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set WAR / PLD / DRK / RUN as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest Inheritance
* Or while equipped with Kaja Claymore or Nandaka regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
Rune Fencers cannot complete the Inheritance quest before level 71 due to the necessity of equipping Sword of Trials.

guillotine.description: Delivers a four-hit attack.Silences enemy. Duration of effect varies with TP.
guillotine.name: Guillotine
notes: Can only be used if you set DRK as your main job.

gust slash.description: Deals wind elemental damage to enemy. Damage varies with TP.
gust slash.name: Gust Slash
notes: Can be used at a range of <14.9'.

hagakure.description: Grants "Save TP" effect and a TP bonus to your next weapon skill.
hagakure.name: Hagakure
notes: Grants 400 Save TP.
Grants 1000 TP Bonus.

hailstorm.description: Changes the weather around target party member to "snowy."
hailstorm.name: Hailstorm

hailstorm ii.description: Changes the weather around target party member to "snowy."
hailstorm ii.name: Hailstorm II

hamanoha.description: Lowers accuracy, evasion, magic accuracy, magic evasion and TP gain for demons.
hamanoha.name: Hamanoha
notes: Target must be of the Demon family or else it will not work.
Provides a flat -20 to an enemy's accuracy, evasion, magic accuracy, magic evasion, and Store TP.

hard slash.description: Delivers a single-hit attack. Damage varies with TP.
hard slash.name: Hard Slash

harden shell.name: Harden Shell
notes: Can only be used with the abilities Unbridled Learning and Unbridled Wisdom .
Increases the user's defense by 100%.
Works with Diffusion .

hasso.description: Grants a bonus to attack speed, accuracy, and Strength when using two-handed weapons, but increases recast and casting times.
hasso.name: Hasso
notes: Hasso works only with two-handed weapons.
Hasso and Seigan cannot be used at the same time. Each will overwrite the other when activated.
While Hasso is active, a 50% increase in casting and recast time will be applied to any and all magic, songs and Ninjutsu.
Hasso allows Zanshin to proc as an additional attack, even when the first hit lands.
*The rate is approximately (total normal Zanshin Rate)4
*Only works for SAM main job.
*Is the last priority in the multi-hit check order.
Hasso grants +STR, +10 Accuracy and +10% haste for melee attacks.
*+STR is dependent on the level of the Samurai, up to a maximum of +14.
**STR Bonus = Floor(Samurai level7)
* If used when Samurai is set as a Sub Job, the subbed level is used in the above equation.
*A level 49 Samurai will gain +7 STR while a 98+ Samurai will gain +14 STR.
Hasso's attack speed component is considered as Job Ability Haste, and does not affect the amount of TP gained per hit.
Stacks with Desperate Blows and equipment that enhances Hasso.
*Neither of these will allow a player to pass the 25% cap on Job Ability Haste.

haste.name: Haste
notes: Grants 15% Magic haste (150/1024).
Does not stack with Accession

haste ii.name: Haste II
notes: Grants 30% Magic haste (307/1024).
Does not stack with Accession .
Added in the July 2014 Version Update .

haste samba.description: Inflicts the next target you strike with Haste daze, increasing the attack speed of all those engaged in battle with it.
haste samba.effect: Increases the Haste effect by 1% per merit level.
haste samba.name: Haste Samba
notes: Hitting the target while it is under this effect will give the attacker 5% Job Ability Haste (51/1024; same category has Hasso and Desperate Blows, subject to 25% cap).
*This can be increased up to 10% (101/1024) with merits
The various equipment that modifies Sambas is listed on the Samba category page.

head butt.description: Damage varies with TP. Additional effect: "Stun."
head butt.name: Head Butt
notes: Resistance of the stun effect is affected by Blue Magic Skill and magic accuracy/INT.
Knockback effect can interrupt a mob's spell casting.
Knockback is graphical only - target's position does not actually change.
Stun effect does not always land after the spell is cast.
Monsters that resist lightning or stun will also resist the stun effect from headbutt.

heady artifice.description: Allows automatons to perform a special ability that varies with the head used.
heady artifice.name: Heady Artifice
notes: The SP ability utilized depends on the head equipped to the automaton upon use of Heady Artifice. Effect durations are the same as those of the original SP abilities.
*Harlequin Head: Mighty Strikes - 45 seconds
*Valoredge Head: Invincible - 30 seconds
*Sharpshot Head: Eagle Eye Shot
*Stormwaker Head: Chainspell - 60 seconds
*Soulsoother Head: Benediction
*Spiritreaver Head: Manafont - 60 seconds
Sharpshot Head on Valoredge Body uses Mighty Strikes NOT Eagle Eye Shot presumably same with any other body that is not Sharpshot.

healer's roll.description: Increases potency of "Cure" effect received for party members within area of effect.
healer's roll.name: Healer's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a White Mage Die.
|rollplus=1%

healing breeze.description: Restores HP for party members within area of effect.
healing breeze.name: Healing Breeze
notes: The amount of HP cured increases with increasing MND as a primary statistic. VIT is a secondary, and less effective modifier.
This spell can be used with Divine Seal to increase the amount of HP restored.
This spell is also affected by Cure Potency equipment (eg: Light Staff , etc.).
Soft cap of 180 HP restored.

healing waltz.description: Removes one magic-induced ailment from a party member.
healing waltz.name: Healing Waltz
notes: Instantly removes 1 of most status effect that can be removed with Erase.
*Including, Curse, Paralysis, Blind, Poison, Disease, Virus, or Silence.
Cannot remove Amnesia, Doom, Helix, Petrification, Songs, Stun, Mute, Terror, or Kaustra.
Since it is a Job Ability, it can be used while Silenced, and does not attract monsters that aggro to magic, but to abilities (like Flans).
A variety of gear modifies the Waltz family, and it is listed on the category page.

heat breath.description: Deals fire damage to enemies within a fan-shaped area originating from the caster.
heat breath.name: Heat Breath
notes: Base damage is equal to players current HP divided by 2, but is subject to fractional resists.
Not affected by Magic Attack Bonus .
Damage is not affected by Magic Burst or Monster Correlation bonus, but accuracy is increased.
Damage is increased by Convergence .
Ignores Magic Defense Bonus , but is subject to direct magic % resistance or weakness.

heavy shot.description: Delivers a single-hit attack.  Chance of critical varies with TP.
heavy shot.name: Heavy Shot
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG as your main job, or by the following jobs with the weapons listed below:
* WAR / THF / DRK while wearing a Exalted Crossbow/+1

heavy strike.name: Heavy Strike
notes: Automatic critical hit.
Stacks with +Crit Damage equipment
Possesses a huge accuracy penalty (-100+)

heavy swing.description: Delivers a single-hit attack. Damage varies with TP.
heavy swing.name: Heavy Swing
notes: Stat modifier updated July 8, 2014. Source: https://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/42615-Weapon-Skill-Adjustments

hecatomb wave.description: Deals wind damage to enemies within a fan-shaped area originating from the caster. Additional effect: Blindness.
hecatomb wave.name: Hecatomb Wave
notes: Damage formula is (Current HP)/4 + (Level)/1.5
This spell is enhanced from equipment that increases breath damage and Convergence .
Gains a 25% damage boost when used against Dragons .

heel.description: Commands the pet to cease attacking and return to you.
heel.name: Heel
notes: Calls your pet back to you.
*Despite the description, this also calls the pet to you if you had recently used Stay.
Shares a recast timer with Stay and Leave.
Seems to have infinite range.

helldive.name: Helldive
notes: Knockback effect can interrupt a mob's spell casting.
Knockback is graphical only - target's position does not actually change.

herb pastoral.description: Enhances resistance against poison for party members within area of effect .
herb pastoral.name: Herb Pastoral
notes: Unknown potency and unclear applications.

herculean etude.name: Herculean Etude
notes: Attributes cap at 15 before Etude-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥475.

herculean slash.description: Delivers an ice elemental attack. Paralyzes target. Duration of effect varies with TP.
herculean slash.name: Herculean Slash
notes: Has no dSTAT term

hexa strike.description: Delivers a sixfold attack. Critical hit chance varies with TP.
hexa strike.name: Hexa Strike
notes: Can only be used if you set WHM / GEO as your main job, or by the following jobs with the weapons listed below:
* WAR / PLD while wearing a Beryllium Mace/+1 in the main hand
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

hide.description: Allows you to evade enemies by hiding.
hide.effect: Each merit level reduces recast by 00:00:10.
hide.name: Hide
notes: Gives you the effect of Invisible (Status).
Allows you to lose aggro on certain types of enemies.
* Generally works on monsters that don't track by scent, regardless of detection method.
** Can work on monsters that track by scent if the proper conditions are met (double rain weather, crossing over water, etc.)
* Will reset Thief Enmity if used when at the top of the hate list on these enemies.
** When using Thief as a subjob, Hide will not break the attention of enemies already targeting the user.
You can Sneak Attack from any direction when Hide is active on targets where Hide works.
Using Trick Attack does not make you lose Hide status.

high jump.description: Delivers a high jumping attack on a targeted enemy.
high jump.effect: Each merit level decreases recast by 00:00:04.
high jump.name: High Jump
notes: High Jump is an instant attack round that sheds a portion of the user's enmity.
*Enmity loss is 50% for Dragoon main job, or 30% with Dragoon sub job.
*When attacking with two weapons (due to Dual Wield or Hand-to-Hand), accuracy is determined for each hand, adding to the total damage and TP return of High Jump.
*High Jump is affected by Double Attack, Triple Attack, Multi-Hit Weapons, Additional Effects, and Enspells.
While Spirit Surge is activated, High Jump's recast is reset and it will reduces an enemy’s TP by a percentage proportionate to the amount of damage inflicted when used.
High Jump does not give TP to the target.
When using equipment that modifies Jump TP gain, High Jump will still return the additional TP if it does 0 damage.

hojo: ichi.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Kaginawa ". Reduces an enemy's Attack Speed .
hojo: ichi.name: Hojo: Ichi
notes: This spell inflicts a static 15% (150/1024) Slow effect .

hojo: ni.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Kaginawa ". Reduces an enemy's Attack Speed .
hojo: ni.name: Hojo: Ni
notes: This spell inflicts a static 20% (200/1024) Slow effect .

holy.description: Deals Light elemental damage to an enemy.
holy.name: Holy
notes: Damage can be enhanced by Divine Emblem on Paladin or Afflatus Solace on White Mage .

holy circle.description: Grants resistance against undead to party members within area of effect.
holy circle.effect: Reduces recast time by 00:00:10 for each merit point.
holy circle.name: Holy Circle
notes: Gives or strengthens Undead Killer for all party members in range.
Main job gives a unique 15% damage bonus against undead, 15% damage resistance from undead, and likely +15% Undead Killer.
*When subbed, gives 5% of these bonuses.
Only one Circle may be active at any given time.

holy ii.description: Deals light elemental damage to an enemy. Afflatus Solace: Increases spell potency based on the amount of HP you restore.
holy ii.name: Holy II
notes: Damage can be enhanced by Divine Emblem on Paladin or Afflatus Solace on White Mage . Gives an Amnesia effect that lasts about 20~25 seconds when used with Divine Emblem against Undead enemies.

honor march.description: Increases Attack Speed , attack , accuracy , ranged accuracy , and ranged attack for party members within area of effect.
honor march.name: Honor March
notes: Increase in attack speed caps at 126/1024 (12.30%), Attack/Ranged Attack at 168, and Acc/Ranged Acc at 42 with only Marsyas equipped. Skill cap is 900.

horde lullaby.description: Puts enemies to sleep within area of effect.
horde lullaby.name: Horde Lullaby
notes: Light Sleep effect duration is 30 seconds before Lullaby-enhancing gear. String Skill has no bearing on potency, but does affect radius. See Lullaby for details. Wind Skill and Singing Skill do not affect radius or potency.

Mob Actions are interrupted as a side effect of falling asleep; meaning this spell can also be used as a different form of Stun . Recommended for mobs that are resistant to Stun but not resistant to Light-based Sleep.
Added in the July 8th, 2002 Version Update .

horde lullaby ii.description: Puts enemies to sleep within area of effect.
horde lullaby ii.name: Horde Lullaby II
notes: Light Sleep effect duration is 60 seconds before Lullaby-enhancing gear. String Skill has no bearing on potency, but does affect radius. See Lullaby for details. Wind Skill and Singing Skill do not affect radius or potency.

Mob Actions are interrupted as a side effect of falling asleep; meaning this spell can also be used as a different form of Stun . Recommended for mobs that are resistant to Stun but not resistant to Light-based Sleep.

hot shot.description: Deals fire elemental damage to an enemy.
hot shot.name: Hot Shot
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG / COR as your main or sub job.

hover shot.description: Increases range attack damage, ranged accuracy and magic accuracy, as well as decreases enmity, when attacking an enemy at range from a location at least one yalm from where you last attacked it at a range.
hover shot.name: Hover Shot
notes: "Attacked at a range" is defined as completing any Ranged Attack or Ranged Weapon Skill (Marksmanship or Achery Weapon Skills) regardless of your distance from the monster (as long as you are in range for the attack/skill to fire), and even if you deal 0 damage or miss. (Being interrupted by movement or being out of range does not count.) (Have not yet verified perfect dodge or utsusemi/blink type effects, but suspect would be treated same as a miss.)
Ranged Accuracy Bonus effect stacks up to a maximum of 25 times.
* 4 Ranged Accuracy Bonus per stack, for a maximum of +100.
* - enmity per stack, for a maximum of -50.
* Stacks start at 0 and reset to 0 whenever you activate Hover Shot (even if the buff is already active).
* 1 Stack is gained whenever you complete an attack at a range standing at a location that is at least 1 yalm from the last location you were standing at when attacking at a range.
* Stacks always reset to 1 if you attack from range less than 1 yalm from the location you were standing when you last attacked at a range.
* Stacks also reset to 1 when you attack at a range targeting a different monster than the last time you attacked at a range.
Overwrites and overwritten by Decoy Shot.
Increases all ranged damage, including ranged magic weaponskills, by +4% per shot up to +100% damage bonus after the 26th shot.

howling fist.description: Delivers a two-fold attack. Damage varies with TP.
howling fist.name: Howling Fist
notes: Can only be used if you set MNK / PUP as your main job.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

hundred fists.name: Hundred Fists
notes: Reduces delay by 75% after calculated delay.
* Calculated delay is delay after including Weapon Delay and Martial Arts traits and gear.
* Because your post-Arts delay is used, it is possible to break the normal 80% Attack Speed cap with this ability when using Hand-to-Hand weapons.
Works with any weapon a monk can equip.

If any Job Abilities, items, or ranged weapons are used, or spells cast, by a Monk while under the Hundred Fists effect it will be interrupted - there will be one "round" of normal-delay attacks before the ability takes effect again.
Kick attacks will proc at normal rate during Hundred Fists. This can be observed while wearing 100% KA rate equip and using Footwork while Hundred Fists is active.

hunter's prelude.description: Enhances target party member's ranged attack accuracy.
hunter's prelude.name: Hunter's Prelude
notes: Ranged Accuracy bonus caps at 45 before Prelude-enhancing gear. The table below assumes your magic skill is capped Skill cap is < 900

hunter's roll.description: Enhances accuracy and ranged accuracy for party members within area of effect.
hunter's roll.name: Hunter's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Ranger Die.
The effect of this roll is increased if a Ranger is a member of the user's party.
The Barataria Ring (+5) adds +25 accuracy to the effect of the roll.
|rollplus=5

ice carol ii.name: Ice Carol II
notes: Elemental Resistance caps at 100, and Damage Nullification chance at 15% before Carol-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥900.

ice maneuver.description: Enhances the effect of ice attachments. Must have an animator equipped.
ice maneuver.name: Ice Maneuver
notes: Enhances ice attachments (IE: Loudspeaker), and allows for the use of automaton job abilities granted by ice attachments.
Increases your automaton's Intelligence. See Maneuver for details.
Gear that affects Maneuvers in general is listed on the Maneuver Category page.

ice spikes.description: Covers you with magical ice spikes. Enemies that hit you take ice damage.
ice spikes.name: Ice Spikes
notes: Damage Dealt = floor ( floor ( (INT + 50) ÷ 20 ) × (1 + MAB ÷100 )
Potency (from INT) is determined at the time of casting, while Magic Attack Bonus is determined at the time of the hit that procs spikes.
Caps at 250 INT, or 15 base damage.
Paralysis effect is moderately potent and accurate, but has not been quantified.

ice spirit.description: Summons a Ice Spirit to fight by your side.
ice spirit.name: Ice Spirit

ice threnody.description: Reduces an enemy's resistance against Ice.
ice threnody.name: Ice Threnody
notes: Ice Magic Evasion down caps at 50 before Threnody-enhancing gear. Skill cap is .

ice threnody ii.description: Reduces an enemy's resistance against Ice.
ice threnody ii.name: Ice Threnody II
notes: Ice Magic Evasion down caps at 160 before Threnody-enhancing gear. Skill cap is .

ignis.description: Increases resistance against ice and deals fire damage.
ignis.effect: Increases potency of runes harbored by 2 points per rank.
ignis.name: Ignis
notes: Elemental Resistance for one Rune = 49  Level  99 + 5.5 + (2~10 ) + (1~20 ) Note: The level refers to the current level of the RUN job, not the player level.
Equipment with "Sword enhancement spell damage +n", such as  or , does 'not' affect the damage from this ability.

immanence.description: Makes it possible for your next elemental magic spell to be used in a skillchain, but not a magic burst.
immanence.duration: 00:01:00 or first black magic cast, whichever first
immanence.name: Immanence
notes: Allows spells to interact and Skillchain as if they each had their own level 1 Skillchain properties.
*Only single-target elemental families that SCH natively has access to (Does not wear off for ineligible spells)
*Example: Immanence -> Blizzard IV -> Immanence -> Water IV -> Fragmentation
*Closing a skillchain with an Immanence Helix will make the magic burst window two seconds longer than normal.

impact.description: Deals dark damage that lowers an enemy's Strength , Dexterity , Vitality , Agility , Intelligence , Mind , and Charisma .
impact.name: Impact
notes: Debuffs from Impact wear off 3 minutes after the spell and do not decay. The timer cannot be extended by casting Impact again before it has worn off. Each of the target's base stats is reduced by 20% of the value at the time of cast. Stat values are snapshotted on cast, so Impact will have the highest potency if applied before other reductions such as Elemental Debuffs . Overwrites the debuff from an Absorb -stat spell, but prevents an Absorb spell from taking effect if it is casted after Impact. Overwrite priority is based on effect potency. The stronger effect will overwrite and prevent weaker effects. The Debuffs cannot be fully resisted, but the duration may resist.

imperator.name: Imperator
notes: Prime Aftermath:  Physical Damage Limit

impetus.description: Enhances attack and critical hit rate with each successive melee attack you land.
impetus.name: Impetus
notes: Impetus gives +2 Attack and +1% Critical Hit Rate for each consecutive successful attack.
* Bonuses cap at +100 Attack and +50% Critical Hit Rate after landing 50 consecutive hits.
* Resets to 0 upon miss, missed ranged attacks do not count.
* Weapon Skill and Counter hits and misses are included in the count.
Overwritten by O. Counterstance if given to the player via Diffusion.

impulse drive.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Damage varies with TP.
impulse drive.name: Impulse Drive
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set WAR / SAM / DRG as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest Methods Create Madness
* Or while equipped with Kaja Lance or Shining One regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
** This is the only way for Paladin to use the weapon skill.
DRG cannot complete the Methods Create Madness quest before level 71 due to the necessity of equipping Spear of Trials.
The "" damage boost from Kaja Lance and Shining One applies to all hits in the Weapon Skill.

innin.description: Reduces enmity and impairs evasion. Grants a bonus to accuracy, critical hit rate, and ninjutsu damage when striking target from behind.

* The Ninjutsu damage bonus is separate from all other multipliers.
Not available via support job.

notes: Can only be used if you set DRK as your main job.

intervene.description: Strikes the target with your shield and decreases its attack and accuracy.

notes: Sets the enemy's accuracy and attack to 1 for the duration.
Base damage is multiplied by shield skill. (0 shield skill means 0 damage dealt.)
*Dealt 0 damage with 0 shield skill, 590 damage with Priwen (+112 shield skill). More testing needed.

inundation.description: Grants a bonus to skillchain damage based on the number of weaponskill weapon types used on the target.
inundation.name: Inundation
notes: Inundation provides a debuff which makes the target take more damage from skillchains IF different WEAPON TYPES are used in said skillchain.
Different players (or npcs/trusts) must do the weapon skills in said skillchain to grant the bonus multiplier. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ]

For example a skillchain only consisting of Sword weapon skills will get nothing from this spell whereas a skillchain consisting of Sword , Scythe and Polearm will receive a considerable bonus. SE has confirmed a x1.2 increase per weapon type (Reference below).
Including pets, there are 15 total types of weapons, which means that the maximum value that can be reached with Inundation is a 12.8x increase [ 4 ]
The damage bonus is retained across skillchains for a duration ; the maximum bonus does not require one continuous skillchain. [ 5 ]
This spell cannot be resisted, except by enemies which are completely immune to magic.
Can be cast on a target which already has the effect, and it will overwrite itself, resetting the timer.
SCH using Immanence does not count as a weapon type.

invincible.description: Grants immunity to all physical attacks.
invincible.name: Invincible
notes: All physical attacks incurred during the duration of Invincible will be reduced to 0 damage.
Draws a massive amount of spike hate to the user (equivalent to four simultaneous Provokes).
In endgame situations, this ability is typically used for hate control, not survival.
This does not provide any protection from spells or magic damage.

invisible.description: Lessens chance of being detected by sight.
invisible.name: Invisible
notes: Applies Invisible (Status) .
Base duration varies from 450 to 600 seconds.
Invisible can be recast to overwrite itself and extend its duration indefinitely.

ionohelix ii.description: Deals lightning damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

issekigan.description: Increases chance of parrying, and gives an enmity bonus upon a successful parry attempt.

notes: Substantially increases parrying rate.
Grants a 300 Cumulative Enmity and unknown Volatile Enmity boost upon successful parries.
* Cumulative Enmity and Volatile Enmity boost is affected by Enmity + equipment and effects. It is therefore possible to triple the gain with an optimised build.

jet stream.description: Delivers a threefold attack. Accuracy varies with TP.

notes: Enmity from this spell is gained regardless of if it takes effect or not.

jump.description: Delivers a short jumping attack on a targeted enemy.
jump.effect: Each merit level decreases recast by 00:00:02.
jump.name: Jump
notes: Jump is an instant attack that receives a base damage (+fSTR) multiplier of (1 + VIT/256)
*When attacking with two weapons (due to Dual Wield or Hand-to-Hand), accuracy is determined for each hand, adding to the total damage and TP return of Jump.
*Jump is affected by Double Attack, Triple Attack, and Multi-Hit Weapons.
** Only the first main hand hit receives the VIT bonus.
When Spirit Surge in activated, Jump's recast timer is reset and it will reduce the Defense of the target by 20% when used.
*This does not stack with other Defense Down effects aside from Dia and Box Step.
Jump does not give TP to the target.
When using equipment that modifies jump TP gain, Jump will still return the additional TP if it does 0 damage.
Store TP affects the extra TP gained from Jump TP Bonus

kaustra.description: Consumes 20% of your maximum MP. Relentless dark damage slowly devours an enemy.
kaustra.name: Kaustra
notes: This spell is only accessible while under the effects of SCH's 1-hour Tabula Rasa . Should Tabula Rasa wear off while you are casting this spell, you will get a message stating that you cannot cast this spell when the casting completes.
To learn this spell, speak to Erlene in the Eldieme Necropolis (S) at (J-8) while on Scholar . (Your SCH must be level 5 or higher.)

Does considerable initial damage (~Tier 5 spell), and gives a DoT that does about a fourth of the initial damage every tick . Works with 3-second ticks , unlike Helices . It is not considered a helix. Unresisted duration is approximately 1 + (Dark Magic Skill)/11 ticks (or three times that in seconds).

The base damage formula differs from regular magic damage calculations: floor(floor(0.067*Lv)*(37+floor(0.67*dINT))) When paired with Ebullience, the +40 Magic Damage from the "Stratagem Effect III" Job Point category is applied with the "37+floor(0.67*dINT)" term to become up to "37+40+floor(0.67*dINT)" The base damage depends on the main level (Lv), not on the level difference. 0.067*Lv is floored to one decimal digit, all other floors round down to the next integer. dINT is caster's INT vs. target INT and caps at 300. This formula is exact for most levels (including 99), but has some minor deviations at certain lower levels. Other multipliers, such as darkness elemental potency and Magic Attack Bonus , are applied on top of the base damage normally. This DoT effect caps at 24,999 damage per tick.
This spell cannot be erased , but can be overwritten by (and overwrites) the effects of Embrava , Benediction , or Full Cure .
The effect of this spell will wear off if an affected mob goes passive.

killer instinct.description: Grants your pet's Killer Effect to party members within area of effect.
killer instinct.effect: Each merit level past the first increases duration by 10 seconds.

notes: Ability adds Killer Trait +15%, damage dealt +15%, and damage taken -15% against monsters of the family matching the active pet's correlation when used (example: a Plantoid pet provides Beast Killer +15%, damage dealt +15% against beasts, and damage taken -15% from beasts).
*These bonuses are a separate multiplier that affect all hits in a Weaponskill as well as melee damage, in a separate step.
*These bonuses reduce damage taken as a set -% after Damage Taken -% from gear has been applied, in a separate step.
Like all circles, the effect is diminished to +10% when fighting NM's; this includes the Killer Effects that contribute to intimidation and the bonuses from Nukumi Gausape or Founder's Breastplate.
Despite the description, this is substantially stronger than the Killer Effect that pets natively possess.

* It is impossible to make Light using a Fragmentation property.
* It is impossible to make Distortion, Gravitation, Impaction or Reverberation using Konzen-ittai.

libra.name: Libra
notes: Can only be used on targets where at least one party or alliance member is on the monster's hate list.
Gives information about the relative Enmity levels of your party to all party members within 30' of the Scholar.
*Does not give information about alliance members' level of enmity, only the members of the party of the user.
Displays total CE+VE as a percentage.
*The percentage shown is based on the level of enmity the player with the most enmity has at the time of use.
**e.g. If player A has 500 CE 200 VE, and player B has 300 CE 50 VE; then Libra will state that player A's enmity = 100% and player B's enmity = 50%.
***Should both players gain 400 more CE, then when Libra is used again, it will state that player A's enmity = 100% and player B's enmity = 68%.

***Or should only player B gain 400 more CE, then when Libra is used again, it will either state: that player A's enmity = 100% and player B's enmity = 107%; or that player A's enmity = 93% and player B's enmity = 100%; depending on the timing of when Libra is used.

liement.description: Absorbs elemental damage. The types of elemental damage absorbed depend on the runes you harbor.
liement.name: Liement

* 1 Rune = 100% Absorbed
* 2 Runes = 115% Absorbed
* 3 Runes = 130% Absorbed
Damage is calculated before Magic Damage Taken but after Magic Defense Bonus.
Overwrites and prevents Vallation and Valiance
With Epeolatry, Liement becomes a 10' party-AOE

life cycle.description: Distributes one fourth of your HP to your luopan.

The increase in Life Cycle potency from Job points is applied in the same set as equipment bonuses.
* With Job points, Nantosuelta's Cape, and Geomancy Tunic +1, you would have a bonus of 40%.
** For example, someone with 2000 HP and +20% Life Cycle from 20 job points will lose 500 HP and the luopan will gain 600 HP.

light arts.description: Optimizes white magic capability while lowering black magic proficiency. Grants a bonus to divine, enhancing, and healing magic.
light arts.name: Light Arts
notes: Along with Dark Arts, Light Arts is one of two grimoires available to scholars. Once activated, Light Arts cannot be removed except by zoning, dying, or applying Dark Arts.

Grants a bonus to Divine Magic, Healing Magic, Enfeebling Magic, and Enhancing Magic. The bonus is equivalent to the amount required to bring the skill ranking to a B+ grade. Magic skills that are above this rating will not be enhanced. Bonuses from merits are not included in this calculation. This also applies when subbed, skill will raise to a B+ rank for the level of your MAIN job.
Reduces casting costs of light magic spells by 10% (rounded down, spells with casting cost of 9 MP or less are not affected). The MP cost of black magic spells is increased by 20% (rounded up).
Cuts effective casting time and recast of white magic spells by 10%.
Increases casting time and recast of black magic spells by 20%.
Provides a level-dependent bonus to the Regen line of spells.
* This applies to Scholar as a main job only.
* For all Regen spells, the potency bonus (in HP/tick) is: +floor( (Level - 10)/10 ) × 3
* The duration bonus is twice the potency bonus in seconds: +floor( (Level - 10)/10 ) × 6
** At level 50-59: +12 HP/tick, +24 seconds
** At level 70-79: +18 HP/tick, +36 seconds
** At level 90-99: +24 HP/tick, +48 seconds

* Tabula Rasa provides an improved bonus to Regen spells which overwrites the Light Arts effect, so Regen spells will instead have a +36 HP/tick potency bonus and +72 seconds duration regardless of level or Light Arts/Dark Arts.
Recast timer can be reduced by up to 10 seconds with scholar-specific Grimoire Recast merits (2 seconds per merit).
There is no recast timer while Tabula Rasa is in effect.

lunge.description: Expends all runes to deal damage to a target.
lunge.effect: Increases Lunge Accuracy by 3 per merit level.
lunge.name: Lunge
notes: Damage appears to be based on weapon skill, number of runes, and typical Magic multiplier terms. The proposed formula is:
* Damage = (Main-hand weapon Skill  (100 + Swipe Job Points)  100)  (0.5 + (Number of Runes  0.25) + (n  100) )  Magic Multiplier Terms
**Number of Runes = the number of runes of the same element consumed, not the number of runes active. Number of runes = 1 if not main job RUN regardless of same type bonus.
**n = Lunge effect bonus from gear.
***For example: Aettir's "Lunge"+10 would be n=10
* Treated like an Elemental Magic damage source (can MB, subject to day/weather boosts, etc.)
* Does not feed TP to target
* Lunge is nullified by blink/shadows.
* If the target dies midway through rune consumption, not all runes are expended.

Manifestation also works with certain Dark Magic spells:

* Stun

* Klimaform, notable for being compatible despite its effect being an enhancement
Spells that do direct damage like Kaustra are subject to Multiple Target Damage Reduction when calculating their damage.
*Drain and Aspir do not seem to suffer from this penalty when used with Manifestation.
Activating this Stratagem doubles the MP cost and recast time of the spell and nullifies the Dark Arts recast time and MP cost reductions.
* When used from Scholar support job, Manifestation will triple spell recast.
Compatible Spells

meteor.description: Summons a massive meteor from the heavens to smite your foes.
meteor.name: Meteor
notes: Meteor can only be cast while Elemental Seal is in effect.
Meteor cannot be resisted, although its damage is magical and can be reduced normally by enemy Magic Defense Bonus or MDT.

Meteor is a group cast spell and can be cast by up to six black mages in a party simultaneously. The spell will fire only once and the damage dealt will increase with each additional black mage casting. The spell damage is affected most greatly by the last black mage to cast the spell (and least by the first black mage). Therefore, mages should cast in order of lowest damage potential to highest damage potential, based on their gear and statistics. The last black mage to cast will be considered the spell leader; the spell will fire once their spell casting timer is complete. The enmity is assigned to the final caster, who will likely need to use Enmity Douse to survive if the monster was not killed. Only one party per alliance can cast Meteor simultaneously. Members of other parties who try to cast will have their casting reset.

Unlike other nukes, elemental magic skill contributes directly to Meteor base damage, and the damage is independent of the enemy's INT. Excluding MDB and MDT effects, each BLM's base damage can be approximated as: (1+MAB/100)*floor(INT + Elemental Magic Skill/6)*3.5 The base damage from each BLM is multiplied by up to 6.5 depending on the number of casters and their positions in the casting order.
Meteor damage is non-elemental magic damage and is not affected positively or negatively by any weather/day effects or any damage or accuracy affinity staves.

Monsters will receive heavy damage resistance and magic evasion to Meteor after it has been cast once. This resistance and evasion will diminish gradually over five minutes and be reset completely after five minutes have passed.

migawari: ichi.description: Uses the ninja tool, " mokujin ." Makes you immune from severely damaging attacks.

Threshold works off total HP value, including all gear.

Threshold is based on Ninjutsu Skill when the spell is cast, with a minimum of 100% and no known cap. Some have expressed suspicion that threshold moves over the minimum (100%) when players increase their skill past the cap for level 75 (269 skill). Threshold appears to decrease by 1% every 4-5 Skill and is approximately a 73% threshold at capped Ninjutsu skill for a level 99 player (417).
Threshold is modified by Iga Ningi +1 and +2 , which must be worn when the attack lands to receive the effect.

Damage dealt by this job ability is directly proportional to the player's current HP and can be resisted with enough Magic evasion.
* Average damage dealt is 50% current HP, though it can vary from 6% to 80% from resisting.
*Magic Attack Bonus and Magic Defense Bonus have no effect on this type of attack.
*Addle, Pining Nocturne, Geo-Vex, Indi-Attunement and Odyllic Subterfuge will lower the damage from this attack if it is resisted.
*Magic Shield effects including Rampart, Shining Ruby, Wind’s Blessing, One for All and Sacrosanctity will lower the damage taken from this attack.
*Shell/Magic damage taken - and Damage taken - gear will lower the damage taken from this attack.
*Enfeebling Resistance effects such as Fealty and Asylum greatly increase your chance of resisting this attack.
*Despite looking like fire damage, there is no bar spell for Mijin Gakure since it is non-elemental damage.
When used against the player by enemies above level 99, has a hard cap of 4,000 damage.

*Unclear if this applies to enemies of level 99 and below.

oshala.name: Oshala
notes: Prime Aftermath:

osmosis.description: Steals an enemy's HP and one beneficial status effect. Ineffective against undead.
osmosis.name: Osmosis
notes: Drain formula is (Blue Magic Skill x .11) x 7
The beneficial effect absorption is similar to Aura Steal , but it cannot miss.
Does not steal all abilities that can be Dispeled , but steals most. Does not work on Wamouracampa 's Heat Barrier (Blaze Spikes) for example.
Any enhancement stolen will receive the remaining duration of that enhancement.

overdrive.description: Augment the fighting ability of your automaton to its maximum level.
overdrive.name: Overdrive
notes: The chance to Overload becomes zero as long as the job ability is active, allowing the free use of maneuvers without risk.
*Burden, however, keeps accumulating normally, so you can end with high amounts of burden after the effect ends. Be careful not to overload once Overdrive wears off.
*Since it only prevents Overload from happening, Overdrive will not remove the Overload debuff if you are afflicted with it when you perform Overdrive.
The Automaton parameters will be greatly increased:
*Damage dealt with melee hits and ranged attacks, as well as accuracy, ranged accuracy, damage reduction and evasion will significantly increase.
*The Automaton will also get 25% Haste.
**This attack speed increase is classified as Magic Haste, and thus is cumulative with the Haste effect provided by Turbo Charger, but is subsequently also subject to the Magic Haste cap of 43.75%.

overkill.description: Increases ranged attack speed and the chance of activating Double/Triple Shot.
overkill.name: Overkill
notes: Is thought to halve ranged delay after other delay calculations such as Snapshot.
Forces a Double or Triple Shot on every shot.
* Distribution is about 2/3 Double and 1/3 Triple with limited sample size

palisade.description: Increases chance of blocking with shield, and eliminates enmity loss.
palisade.name: Palisade
notes: Increases shield block rate by +30% regardless of skill levels.
*Paladin  can increase this by an additional +20%, for a total of +50% block rate.

palling salvo.description: Deals dark damage to enemies within range. Additional effect: Bio.
palling salvo.name: Palling Salvo
notes: -15 HP/ tick Bio, -10% attack down.

paralyna.name: Paralyna
notes: Target can be any player character

paralyze.name: Paralyze
notes: Chance of paralysis is highly dependent on the differential between the caster's and target's MND levels. Lowest value at -40 dMND compared to enemy: 5% Highest value at +40 dMND compared to enemy: 25%
-10 Magic Accuracy penalty

paralyze ii.name: Paralyze II
notes: Chance of paralysis is highly dependent on the differential between the caster's and target's MND levels. Lowest value at -40 MND compared to enemy: 14% Highest value at +40 MND compared to enemy: 34%
+10 Magic Accuracy .

paralyzing triad.description: Delivers a threefold attack. Additional effect: Paralysis. Damage varies with TP.
paralyzing triad.name: Paralyzing Triad
notes: Potency of Paralyze is 20%.

parsimony.description: Reduces the MP cost of your next Black Magic spell by 50%
parsimony.duration: 00:01:00 or next black magic cast, whichever first
parsimony.name: Parsimony
notes: While under the effect of this job ability, matching spells actually cost half.
*As such, you are only required to have half of the MP to cast the spell.
:Example: If a spell costs 22 MP, you will only need to have 11 MP to cast it.

penta thrust.description: Delivers a fivefold attack. Accuracy varies with TP.
penta thrust.name: Penta Thrust

penury.description: Reduces the MP cost of your next White Magic spell by 50%
penury.duration: 00:01:00 or next white magic cast, whichever first
penury.name: Penury
notes: While under the effect of this job ability, matching spells actually cost half.
*As such, you are only required to have half of the MP to cast the spell.
:Example: If a spell costs 22 MP, you will only need to have 11 MP to cast it.

perfect counter.description: Allows you to counter the next attack directed at you.
perfect counter.name: Perfect Counter
notes: Gives a Third Eye-esque buff that forces a Counter on the next attack directed at you.
*Can miss, but will still negate the incoming attack.
*If you would have countered normally (minus the accuracy check), the Perfect Counter buff is not consumed.
Has a chance to not wear off after successfully countering an attack, chance is determined by the player's VIT stat (formula is as of yet unknown).
*Job Points invested into Perfect Counter will increase the VIT modifier, increasing the chances of more than one guaranteed Counter.
100% Critical Hit Rate for Counters that are a result of Perfect Counter.

perfect dodge.description: Allows you to dodge all melee attacks.
perfect dodge.name: Perfect Dodge
notes: All melee swings and physical TP moves will be evaded.
The player will still be vulnerable to Ranged Attacks and Magic Spells while Perfect Dodge is active.

perpetuance.description: Increases the enhancement effect duration of your next white magic spell.
perpetuance.duration: 00:01:00 or first white Enhancing Magic cast, whichever first
perpetuance.name: Perpetuance
notes: Doubles the duration of the next White Enhancing Magic you cast.

pflug.description: Enhances resistance. The types of resistance enhanced depend upon the runes you harbor.
pflug.effect: Resistance +1% per rune per merit level.
pflug.name: Pflug
notes: Increases resistance to status ailments by a base value of 15% per defensive/resisted element of each rune active at time of JA activation
* This base value is reduced to 10% if subjob RUN.
* The full formula seems to be: (Base Value + Merits + AF feet) Elemental Status Resistance for each rune's corresponding element.
The Pflug+X gear enhancement is applied during the status resist check (during enemy action finish) rather than on JA activation.
* The equipment must be worn as the enemy's action is finishing for the bonus resistance to apply.
* It does not necesarilly need to be worn during JA activation at all.
See the below table for some example status effects typically resisted by each rune when Pflug is active.
See individual pages for various Monsters, Monster Actions, and Status Effects for their specific corresponding element.

Example:
If a main job RUN, Pflug is activated with 2 Sulpor runes and 1 Tenebrae rune active (and no merits, and no Pflug+X gear on), then via the gained buff you would gain a total of:
* 30% Water Elemental Status Resistance (15% per Sulpor rune) -- thereby having increased resistance against Water based status effects such as Poison
* and 15% Light Elemental Status Resistance (15% per Tenebrae rune) -- thereby having increased resistance against Light based status effects such as Light based Sleep, and Charm
While actively wearing Runeist's Bottes +3 (Pflug+4), then you'd have an additional +4% per rune per element -- resulting for this example in a total of:
* 38% Water Elemental Status Resistance
* and 19% Light Elemental Status Resistance.
Similar results for Merit bonuses

phalanx.description: Gives you a certain amount of damage resistance.
phalanx.name: Phalanx
notes: Reduces damage by a set amount (F), which depends on Enhancing Magic skill, and caps at 500, which gives -35 damage: Up to 300 skill: F = Floor ( Enhancing Magic Skill /10) - 2 Over 300 skill: F = 28 + Floor ( ( Enhancing Magic Skill - 300.5) / 28.5) Damage reduction from Phalanx is calculated after Damage Taken equipment.

Phalanx enhancing gear is added directly to this damage. The target of the Phalanx spell (including in cases of Accession Phalanx) must be wearing their Phalanx enhancing gear at the time of its receipt to benefit from its effects, retaining the bonus even after changing equipment. Overshooting or undershooting a enhancing skill tier does not provide any benefit due to the equation flooring the section where enhancing skill is divided. 410 enhancing skill would still only provide -31 as per the 386 skill tier.

phalanx ii.description: Grants a party member a certain amount of damage resistance .
phalanx ii.name: Phalanx II
notes: Reduces damage by a set amount (F), which depends on Enhancing Magic skill (caps at 500, which gives -35 damage): Up to 300 skill: F = Floor ( Enhancing Magic Skill /10) - 2 Over 300 skill: F = 28 + Floor ( ( Enhancing Magic Skill - 300.5) / 28.5)

Phalanx enhancing gear is added directly to this damage reduction making the flat values quite strong past certain levels of enhancing skill. The target of the Phalanx spell must be wearing the Phalanx enhancing gear to benefit from its effects. Overshooting or undershooting a enhancing skill threshold does not give any benefit due to the result being floored, meaning adding +enhancing skill gear that does not reach a threshold is a waste. ex. 410 enhancing skill would still only provide -31 as per the 386 skill tier.

pianissimo.description: Limits area of effect of next song to a single target.
pianissimo.name: Pianissimo
notes: Allows the next song to target and affect only one person.
Reduces base song casting time to 4 seconds.

piercing arrow.description: Ignores enemy's defense. Amount ignored varies with TP.
piercing arrow.name: Piercing Arrow
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG as your main or sub job.

pinecone bomb.description: Additional effect: sleep. Duration of effect varies with TP.
pinecone bomb.name: Pinecone Bomb
notes: Additional effect of sleep is dark based. Applied after damage is done.
Deals piercing damage.
Accuracy is determined by Blue Magic skill.
Considered a throwing ranged attack.
Interrupts all enemy actions; thus can be used as an alternative to Stun .

pining nocturne.description: Decreases an enemy's magic accuracy and increases its spellcasting time.
pining nocturne.name: Pining Nocturne
notes: Skill cap is .

plasma charge.description: Covers you with magical lightning spikes. Enemies that hit you take lightning damage.
plasma charge.name: Plasma Charge

plenilune embrace.description: Restores HP for the target party member.
plenilune embrace.name: Plenilune Embrace
notes: Roughly the equivalent of an inbetween Cure IV ~ Cure V .
Soft cap of 650 HP restored with a hard cap of 710.

Healing Magic Skill acts as a multiplier. One MND is equivalent to three VIT or five Healing Magic Skill . 1 MND is equal to 1.5 HP and 1 VIT is equal to 0.5 HP. See the cure formula page for more details.
Enhanced via cure potency equipment.
Amount healed is doubled by Divine Seal . Plenilune Embrace cures for 100 more HP than Magic Fruit before cure potency and/or Divine Seal is factored in.
Enhanced via Light Staff and other elemental affinity pieces.
Unlike Magic Fruit , this spell is affected via day/weather effects/obis.
Attack Boost from this spell is overwritten by Nat. Meditation but does not overwrite it.
Attack and Magic Attack boost from this spell are inverted based on the moon phase

poison.description: Poisons an enemy and gradually reduces its HP.
poison.name: Poison
notes: Damage Over Time potency is based on Enfeebling Magic Skill . Minimum potency is 1 damage/tick. Maximum Potency is at 500 enfeebling skill. Formula for skill 400+ is Potency = (Skill-225)/5.
Affected by Resist Poison .

poison breath.description: Deals water damage to enemies within a fan-shaped area originating from the caster. Additional effect: Poison.
poison breath.name: Poison Breath
notes: Damage formula is (Current HP)/10 + (Blue Mage level)/1.25
Gains a 25% damage boost when used against Arcana monsters.
Poison effect is 4/tick

poison ii.description: Poisons an enemy and gradually reduces its HP.
poison ii.name: Poison II
notes: Overwrites lesser forms of Poison , like Poison .

Damage Over Time potency is based on Enfeebling Magic Skill . Minimum effect is 4 damage/tick. Effect has no known cap. Formulas: Base Potency = 82 - Floor[(129/430)(500-Skill)] for skill from 400-500 Base Potency = 82 + Floor[(180/783)(Skill-500)] for skill from 500-??
Affected by Resist Poison .
Has two Resist States by duration (Full and Half duration).
+30 Magic Accuracy .
General Potency Formula: floor(floor((Base Potency × Saboteur) + {dStat Modifier}) × (Enfeebling Magic Effect+ Gear))

poisona.name: Poisona
notes: Target can be any player character.

poisonga.description: Poisons enemies within area of effect and gradually reduces their HP.
poisonga.name: Poisonga
notes: Does not overwrite any other form of Poison .
Damage Over Time potency is based on Enfeebling Magic Skill . Minimum potency is 1 damage/tick. Effect caps at 3 damage/tick.
Affected by Resist Poison .

poisonga ii.description: Poisons enemies within area of effect and gradually reduces their HP.

MND is a heavily influential stat for Pollen, much moreso than Cure . A level 1 character with BLU sub will cure 14 HP with Pollen, where Cure would only cure 10.

provoke.description: Goads an enemy into attacking you.
provoke.name: Provoke
notes: Because Provoke gives the same amount of Volatile Enmity as decays in its recast, it can be used to effectively "freeze" a player's Volatile Enmity level.

:Example: Jim the PLD/WAR claimed Tiamat and built some hate, but Tiamat took off flying. Jim uses Provoke (+1800 VE) every 30 seconds (30-60 = -1800 VE) so that he doesn't lose any Volatile Enmity while Tiamat is flying.
In Ballista and Brenner, Provoke temporarily prevents your opponent from targeting anyone but you.
*This will prevent them from casting self-targetted spells as well.
Automatons can use this ability through the Strobe attachment with at least one Fire Maneuver activated.

quick draw.description: Shoot a bullet charged with the magical energy of an elemental  card.
quick draw.effect: Reduces recast time by 00:00:02 per each merit point.
quick draw.name: Quick Draw
notes: ==Quick Draw Damage==
Quick Draw damage is calculated similar to magic damage by multiplying the following factors in order and flooring after each step.
#Base Damage = [(Gun DMG + Bullet DMG) * 2] (Multiple gear listed below add 10 DMG to Base Damage.)
#resist
#Staff Bonus
#Day & Weather
#Player MAB / Target MDB
#Affinity
Most forms of Damage Taken -% are ignored by Quick Draw.
The general Quick Draw damage formula is:
damage = int{ [ (Gun_DMG + Bullet_DMG)*2 + QuickDraw_Damage_Stat] * (1 + DeathPenaltyBonus) + Magic_Damage_Stat } * Magic_Damage_Multipliers
where QuickDraw_Damage_Stat is a damage statistic unique to Quick Draw, which can be obtained from
# "Quick Draw Effect" Job Points category (up to +40)
# "Quick Draw+" equipment, such as Laksa. Tricorne +3 (+20) and Laksa. Bottes +3 (+20), but excluding Chass. Bottes +3
====Basic Info====
Quick Draw is used by expending charges of the ability; charges regenerate one at a time based on the recast timer and up to two may be stored at once.
Requires a gun, a bullet, and an  :Category:Cardselemental card. One card is consumed for each shot, but the bullet is not.
* In addition to the element-specific cards listed in the table below, players using Corsair as their main job may also use   as a substitute for all element-specific cards.
====Accuracy====
Quick Draw accuracy is modified by the user's AGI stat and Magic Accuracy.
*Marksmanship Skill does not affect Quick Draw accuracy.
Quick Draw accuracy can be increased by 10 accuracy in 2 accuracy increments up to five times by investing in Quick Draw Accuracy, a group 1 Corsair job-specific merit.
====Recast====
Quick Draw recast can be lowered from 00:01:00 to 00:00:50 in 2 second increments with five upgrades to the Corsair job-specific merit category Quick Draw Recast.
*Can be further lowered an additional 10 seconds with the 550 Job Point gift.
*Can be further lowered an additional 4-10 seconds using an augmented Crimson/Blood Mask and/or the ACP Body.
**Note: There is an overall cap of -25 seconds for a 35 second recast even though we can get hit -30 with everything.
Charge system recast: Equipment that reduces the recast time, in order to take effect, must be worn when each charge starts its recast timer.
====Monster TP and Player TP====
Quick Draw generates TP for the user equal to that of a standard ranged attack.
*TP generation is increased by Store TP.
Quick Draw feeds 0 TP to the target.
====Negative Status Effect Bonus====
Quick Draw boosts the effect of certain negative status effects that are present on the target at the time of the shot.
*Only the effects of Dia, Bio, Burn, Drown, Shock, Rasp, Choke, Frost, Slow, Blind, and Paralyze are affected
The enhancement  will take effect even if the shot fails.
Debuff enhancement potency remains the same whether Corsair is set as a main or support job.
Relevant negative status effects from all sources are affected up to a certain potency threshold set for each respective effect.
See the table below for details.
====Elemental Damage Bonus====

If used with Navarch's Bottes +1 or any higher upgrade level of the Empyrean footgear equipped, the next magic damage of the same element used against the target within 10 seconds will be increased by an amount depending on the footgear's upgrade level.
*Using the same Quick Draw again will both consume and reapply the bonus.
*The damage bonus will not be applied if the shot fails to take effect.
==Quick Draw Shots==
