quick etude.name: Quick Etude
notes: Attributes cap at 9 before Etude-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥450.

quickstep.description: Lowers a target's evasion. If successful, will earn you finishing move.
quickstep.name: Quickstep
notes: Applies a stackable debuff called Lethargic Daze, which can be applied up to 10 times (the current level is shown as the effect is applied to the target).
*May only reach up to level 5 when applied with a Dancer subjob.
*Quicksteps past the first extend Lethargic Daze duration by 30 seconds, to a maximum of 2 minutes.
*Lowers evasion by 8 for the first level and 4 for each level after.
**Evasion reduction = (Lethargic Daze Level +1)*4
Grants 2 Finishing moves if it hits (can miss, be Blinked, or be Utsusemi-ed).
*Grants only 1 Finishing move if the target is already afflicted with Lethargic Daze (lv.10) or if used with Dancer support job.

quietus.description: Delivers a triple damage attack that ignores target's defense. Amount ignored varies with TP.
quietus.name: Quietus
notes: Can only be used if you set DRK as your main job.
Penitence, +1, +2, +3, and Umiliati do not give Aftermath

radial arcana.description: Causes your luopan to vanish and restores MP of party members within area of effect.
radial arcana.effect: Increases amount of MP restored by 3 percent.
radial arcana.name: Radial Arcana
notes: Heals for a fixed amount based on Luopan Level:
* MP Healed = (3*Luopan Level[1+(0.03(Merit Level-1))] + 1)1.X
** Where X = Bagua Sandals/+1 bonus such that with 1/5 merits, X = 05; and 5/5 merits, X = 25. (05/merit)
* Luopan Level is capped at 99

The formula above does not seem to take Luopan max HP modifiers into account, and testing has shown that it will affect the amount of MP returned. Luopan max HP can be modified by gear (Bagua Galero +2/+3), "Bolster Effect" JP ranks (during Bolster), or effects such as Atma.
* Further testing has show that weather and day also can affect MP return.

radiant breath.description: Deals light damage to enemies within a fan-shaped area of effect originating from the caster. Additional effect: Slow and Silence.
radiant breath.name: Radiant Breath
notes: Damage formula is (Current HP)/5 + (Level)/0.75
This spell is enhanced from equipment that increases breath damage and Convergence .
Gains a 25% damage boost when used against Demons .

raging axe.description: Delivers a two-hit attack. Damage varies with TP.
raging axe.name: Raging Axe

raging fists.description: Delivers a five-fold attack. Damage varies with TP.
raging fists.name: Raging Fists
notes: Can only be used if you set MNK / PUP as your main or sub job.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

raging rush.description: Delivers a three-hit attack.  Chance of critical hit varies with TP.
raging rush.name: Raging Rush
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR as your main job.

raiden thrust.description: Deals lightning elemental damage. Damage varies with TP.
raiden thrust.name: Raiden Thrust
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / PLD / DRG as your main or sub job.

rail cannon.description: Deals light damage to an enemy.
rail cannon.name: Rail Cannon

rainstorm.description: Changes the weather around target party member to "rainy."
rainstorm.name: Rainstorm

rainstorm ii.description: Changes the weather around target party member to "rainy."
rainstorm ii.name: Rainstorm II

raise.name: Raise
notes: Restores 50% of lost experience points to the target upon revival.
Revived target will have have weakness status for five minutes following revival and will be revived with 10% of the reduced maximum HP.

raise ii.name: Raise II
notes: Restores 75% of lost experience points to targets level 50 or above upon revival. Targets below level 50 will only receive 50% of lost experience points.
Revived target will have have weakness status for five minutes following revival and will be revived with 25% of the reduced maximum HP.

raise iii.name: Raise III
notes: Restores 90% of lost experience points to targets level 50 or above upon revival. Targets below level 50 will only receive 50% of lost experience points.
Revived target will have have weakness status for five minutes following revival and will be revived with 50% of the reduced maximum HP.

raiton: ichi.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Hiraishin ". Deals lightning damage to an enemy and lowers its resistance against earth.
raiton: ichi.name: Raiton: Ichi
notes: See the Ninjutsu page for more information

raiton: ni.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Hiraishin ". Deals lightning damage to an enemy and lowers its resistance against earth.
raiton: ni.name: Raiton: Ni
notes: See the Ninjutsu page for more information

raiton: san.description: Uses the ninja tool, " Hiraishin ". Deals lightning damage to an enemy and lowers its resistance against earth.
raiton: san.name: Raiton: San
notes: See the Ninjutsu page for more information

ram charge.name: Ram Charge
notes: Knockback effect can interrupt spell casting.
Knockback is graphical only - target's position does not actually change.

rampage.description: Delivers a five-hit attack.  Chance of critical varies with TP.
rampage.name: Rampage
notes: The Critical Hit Rates listed are from tests with N<30.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

rampart.description: Decreases damage received by party members in area of effect.
rampart.name: Rampart
notes: Grants all party members within the 13' area of effect -25% SDT.
*SDT is "Special Damage Taken" and is multiplicative with regular Damage Taken; many forms of SDT are for a single type or element of damage, however, 's SDT is for all damage types.
Shares a buff slot and does not stack with, in : Rampart < Shining Ruby < Wind’s Blessing < One for All.
*If the paladin has one or more merits in Iron Will, it cannot be overwritten by anything other than Rampart from another paladin with one or more merits in Iron Will.

randgrith.name: Randgrith
notes: Relic Aftermath: Accuracy +20
* Molva Maul does not give Aftermath
The "Shield Break attack" mentioned in the helptext lowers the target's evasion by 32.

random deal.description: Has the possibility of resetting the recast time of a random ability for party members within area of effect.
random deal.effect: Reduces recast by 00:00:40 per merit level.
random deal.name: Random Deal
notes: Random Deal randomly selects one job ability for each party member in range (under 8 yalms) and, if that ability is waiting on recast, resets the timer. If any abilities are not waiting on recast, it is possible that Random Deal will select one of those and have no noticeable effect.
* Random Deal will not select to restore SP Abilities.

Random Deal strongly favors the restoration of abilities that are grouped on shared timers within a hierarchy (such as Stratagems, Quick Draw, Phantom Roll, etc.), as each ability within the hierarchy is counted separately when an ability to restore is randomly chosen. This basically means that if you're using Random Deal to reset your own Snake Eye, Crooked Cards, or whatever, you have to wait for Phantom Roll and Quick Draw to fully recover.
* While this was planned to be corrected in an upcoming version update, it still hasn't been resolved several years later.
Random Deal will restore all charges of charge-related abilities if they are restored (including Stratagems and Quick Draw).
Loaded Deck guarantees that Random Deal targets an ability waiting on recast if the party member receiving the effect has more than one ability waiting on recast.
* The failure rate of Random Deal with only one ability waiting on recast is reduced with further merits in Loaded Deck.

raptor mazurka.description: Increases movement speed for party members within area of effect.
raptor mazurka.name: Raptor Mazurka
notes: Skill and gear have no bearing on potency.

rapture.description: Enhances the potency of the next White Magic spell.
rapture.duration: 00:01:00 or first white magic cast, whichever first
rapture.name: Rapture
notes: Provides a 50% potency boost to Healing Magic.
Provides a 50% potency boost to the damage of Divine Magic and white magic-based Enfeebling Magic.

* This stratagem does not boost the potency of any debuff effects associated with these spells.  (for example: the defense down of Dia spells will not be enhanced by this ability, nor will the damage type reductions of the Banish line of spells.)

rasp.description: Deals Earth damage that lowers an enemy's dexterity and gradually reduces its HP.
rasp.name: Rasp
notes: Part of the Elemental Debuff family of spells.
Overwrites Shock
Is overwritten by Choke
Can be stacked with Frost and Drown or Burn
Damage over time increases with Intelligence .

rayke.description: Expends runes to reduce elemental resistance of the target.
rayke.effect: Each additional merit increases duration by 3 seconds.
rayke.name: Rayke
notes: Reduces an enemy's elemental resistance ranks, which can have have a variety of effects including increasing skillchain damage and magic burst damage, as well as reducing elemental magic evasion.
Resistance ranks lowered is based on the element of the runes used, and the number of elements used.
*Ignis lowers fire resistance, Sulpor lowers thunder resistance, etc.
*Enemy resistance ranks will fall 1 tier per rune harbored while Rayke is in effect. See the Resist page for tiers.

**This means Rayke may be used not only to increase skillchain damage of the corresponding element(s) and potentially boost magic burst damage, but to remove guaranteed 1/2 resists from any enemy if it can lower their resistance rank to below 50%.

reactor cool.description: Enhances defense and covers you with magical ice spikes. Enemies that hit you take ice damage.
reactor cool.name: Reactor Cool
notes: +12% defense bonus

ready.description: Commands the pet to make a special attack.
ready.effect: Each merit reduces Charge time by 00:00:02.
ready.name: Ready
notes: When using a Jug Pet, the command Sic becomes Ready, which allows you to select a specific TP move for your pet to perform instead of selecting one randomly.
Unlike Sic, Ready relies on a charge system.
*TP moves from pets cost a varying number of charges.
*Charges refill once every 30 seconds without any Sic merits.
** The minimum recast time for Ready is 10 seconds per charge when taking into account all effects from merit points, Job Point Gifts, and equipment.

realmrazer.description: Delivers a sevenfold attack. Accuracy varies with TP.
realmrazer.name: Realmrazer
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / MNK / WHM / BLM / PLD / SMN / BLU / SCH / GEO as your main job.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.
The bolded, italicized Skillchain property is only available while under the effects of Aeonic Aftermath.

reaving wind.description: Reduces TP for enemies within range.
reaving wind.name: Reaving Wind
notes: Reduces enemy TP by 1,000.
This is affected by Monster Correlation . -750 TP against Amorphs . -1250 TP against Aquans .

recall-jugner.description: Teleports qualified party members within area of effect to Jugner Forest (S) .
recall-jugner.name: Recall-Jugner
notes: Transports party members within area of effect to the telepoint of Jugner Forest (S) at (H-9). Party members must have the Jugner gate crystal key item in order to be transported.

recall-meriph.description: Teleports qualified party members within area of effect to Meriphataud Mountains (S) .
recall-meriph.name: Recall-Meriph
notes: Transports party members within area of effect to the telepoint of Meriphataud Mountains (S) at (I-8). Party members must have the Meriphataud gate crystal key item in order to be transported.

recall-pashh.description: Teleports qualified party members within area of effect to Pashhow Marshlands (S) .
recall-pashh.name: Recall-Pashh
notes: Transports party members within area of effect to the telepoint of Pashhow Marshlands (S) at (J-9). Party members must have the Pashhow gate crystal key item in order to be transported.

red lotus blade.description: Deals fire elemental damage to enemy. Damage varies with TP.
red lotus blade.name: Red Lotus Blade
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / RDM / PLD / DRK / BLU / RUN as your main or sub job.

refresh.name: Refresh
notes: Gives 3MP/tick for 150 MP per unenhanced spell cast.

refresh ii.name: Refresh II
notes: Gives 6MP/tick for 300 MP per unenhanced spell cast.
Available from A.M.A.N. Trove.

refresh iii.name: Refresh III
notes: Grants 9 MP/tick for 450 MP per unenhanced spell cast.

refueling.name: Refueling
notes: Haste effect is 10% unlike the 15% from ordinary haste.
Is overwritten by and does not overwrite any other form of haste or slow. Except for the slow from Pugils , Crawlers , or Skeletons .

refulgent arrow.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Damage varies with TP.
refulgent arrow.name: Refulgent Arrow
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG as your main or sub job.
Seems to be only a one-hit attack. It is unclear what the "twofold" in the description refers to.

regen.name: Regen
notes: Recovers 5 HP every tick. This can be improved with merits.
Light Arts provides a bonus of 24 HP/tick recovery and +48 seconds of duration at level 99 ( Scholar main job only).
Tabula Rasa provides a bonus of 36 HP/tick recovery at level 99.
Check Enhancing Magic Duration for precise info on the duration.

regen ii.name: Regen II
notes: Recovers 12 HP every tick. This can be improved with merits.
Light Arts provides a bonus of 24 HP/tick recovery and +48 seconds duration at level 99 ( Scholar main job only).
Tabula Rasa provides a bonus of 36 HP/tick recovery at level 99.
Check Enhancing Magic Duration for precise info on the duration.

regen iii.name: Regen III
notes: Recovers 20 HP every tick. This can be improved with merits.
Light Arts provides a bonus of 24 HP/tick recovery and +48 seconds duration at level 99 ( Scholar main job only).
Tabula Rasa provides a bonus of 36 HP/tick recovery at level 99.
Check Enhancing Magic Duration for precise info on the duration.

regen iv.name: Regen IV
notes: Recovers 30 HP every tick. This can be improved with merits and equipment.
Light Arts provides a bonus of 24 HP/tick recovery and +48 seconds of duration at level 99 ( Scholar main job only).
Tabula Rasa provides a bonus of 36 HP/tick recovery at level 99.
Check Enhancing Magic Duration for precise info on the duration.

regen v.name: Regen V
notes: Recovers 40 HP every tick.
Light Arts provides a bonus of 24 HP/tick recovery and +48 seconds of duration at level 99 ( Scholar main job only).
Tabula Rasa provides a bonus of 36 HP/tick recovery at level 99.
Check Enhancing Magic Duration for precise info on the duration.

regeneration.name: Regeneration
notes: Regen potency is +25 HP/tick for 30 ticks for 750 HP total.
Enhanced in potency via "Regen +" equipment such as the Telchine Augments .
The Telchine Chasuble 's Regen effect Duration will also extend the duration, however "Enhancing Duration +x%" augments on Telchine equipment will not.
Stacks with the regen 30/tick regen effect from Mighty Guard .

regurgitation.description: Deals Water damage to an enemy. Additional Effect: Bind.
regurgitation.name: Regurgitation
notes: Damage generates almost no cumulative Enmity and thus is virtually hateless.
dInt is a x2 Multiplier
Gains a 25% damage bonus when casting behind the target.
Gains a 1.83 multiplier under Azure Lore .
The bind effect is applied after damage allowing it to be reapplied.

rending deluge.description: Deals water damage to enemies within range. Additional effect: Dispel.
rending deluge.name: Rending Deluge
notes: This spell's Dispel effect cannot miss. It removes one beneficial dispellable effect. The Dispel effect is non-elemental and works on foes that are normally dispel resistant, similar to Atomos or Osmosis . Can only be blocked by magic immunity effects, such as Spectral Barrier from Magic Pots .

repair.description: Gradually restores your automaton's HP. Special items required.
repair.effect: Recast is reduced by 00:00:03 per merit level.
repair.name: Repair
notes: You must have an Automaton Oil,  +1,  +2, or  +3 equipped in the ammo slot in order to use this ability, each use expends one oil.
Duration and potency of the ability are based on which type of oil is used.
A base amount of HP is restored and then a regen effect is granted.
The regen effect from Repair wears off upon zoning and may be dispeled.
Equipment that enhances repair potency actually enhances the regen effect and not the recovery amount.
The Artifact Babouches will remove status ailments from the puppet that the Eraser attachment would, essentially a free Maintenance sans erase.
====Recovery Potency====
Automaton Oil 10% max HP
Automaton Oil +1 20% max HP
Automaton Oil +2 30% max HP
Automaton Oil +3 40% max HP
====Regen Potency====
Automaton Oil 20 HP/tic for 15 seconds (100 HP)
Automaton Oil +1 40 HP/tic for 30 seconds (400 HP)
Automaton Oil +2 60 HP/tic for 45 seconds (900 HP)
Automaton Oil +3 80 HP/tic for 60 seconds (1,600 HP)
====Repair Potency====
Multiply the regen effect by the potency % increase and then add it to the existing effect and calculate. For example, with +50% potency:
*Automaton Oil +3 120 HP/tic for 60 seconds (2,400 HP)

repose.name: Repose
notes: Will not cancel out Sleep II, and vice versa.
Overrides Sleep I and Lullabies.
This is light-based Sleep, so it can sleep skeletons.
Not susceptible to Resist Sleep .
Receives a substantial Magic Accuracy bonus.
Interrupts all enemy actions; thus can be used as an alternative to Stun .
+175 Magic Accuracy

reprisal.description: Increases chance of blocking with shield, and reflects portion of blocked damage back to attacker.
reprisal.name: Reprisal
notes: Increases current shield skill by +15%.(1.15x) The skill bonus is based on your current total shield skill at any given time, not statically calculated upon casting.

Reprisal increases block rate by 50% of your current block rate . Current block rate × 1.5. For Priwen and Adamas : Current block rate × 3 Example. If you have a 30% block rate, reprisal would raise this to 45%; or a 90% block rate with Priwen and Adamas equipped.
As of the May 2021 Version Update , Reprisal no longer wears off regardless of the amount of damage received over time. It simply wears off when the effect duration is over.
Is overwritten by Spikes spells but doesn't overwrite.

requiescat.description: Delivers a fivefold attack, non-elemental damage. Attack power varies with TP.
requiescat.name: Requiescat
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / RDM / PLD / DRK / SAM / BLU / COR / RUN as your main job.
Deals property-less damage (not Magic or Physical), but uses regular physical damage equations.
* In this calculation, attack is penalized by a value depending on TP (from 20% penalty at 1000 TP to no penalty at 3000 TP).
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.
The bolded, italicized Skillchain property is only available while under the effects of Aeonic Aftermath.

reraise.description: Grants you the effect of Raise when you are KO'd.
reraise.name: Reraise
notes: Grants a Raise effect upon KO that restores 10% HP and 50% XP Lost

reraise ii.description: Grants you the effect of Raise II when you are KO'd.
reraise ii.name: Reraise II
notes: Grants a Raise effect upon KO that restores 25% HP and 75% XP Lost

reraise iii.description: Grants you the effect of Raise III when you are KO'd.
reraise iii.name: Reraise III
notes: Grants a Raise effect upon KO that restores 50% HP and 90% XP Lost

reraise iv.description: Grants you the effect of Raise with a decreased weakness duration when you are KO'd.
reraise iv.name: Reraise IV
notes: Grants an Arise effect upon KO that restores 100% HP and 90% XP lost and grants 3 minute weakness.

resolution.description: Delivers a fivefold attack. Damage varies with TP.
resolution.name: Resolution
notes: This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.
The bolded, italicized Skillchain property is only available while under the effects of Aeonic Aftermath.
Even if you have the weaponskill acquired from another job and have the combat skill required to perform the WS you will not get it until lvl 96

restoral.name: Restoral
notes: Roughly the equivalent of Cure VI .

Soft cap of 1040 HP restored with a base of 640. Blue Magic Skill contributes to the amount healed instead of Healing Magic Skill like other BLU healing spells. 2 Skill is equal to 1 HP. One MND is equivalent to three VIT or five Healing Magic Skill . 1 MND is equal to 1.5 HP and 1 VIT is equal to 0.5 HP. See the cure formula page for more details.
Amount healed is doubled by Divine Seal .
Enhanced via cure potency equipment.
Enhanced via Light Staff and other elemental affinity pieces.
Unlike Magic Fruit , this spell is affected via day/ weather effects/ obis .

restoring breath.description: Orders the wyvern to heal with its breath.
restoring breath.name: Restoring Breath
notes: Forces your wyvern to use Healing Breath.

restraint.description: Enhances your weapon skill power with each normal attack you land.
restraint.name: Restraint
notes: Increases Melee Weapon Skill Damage with each attack landed.
Depends on effective Weapon Delay after delay reduction (like Dual Wield) and number of first attacks that land (first swings of each attack round).
:Approximate Equation: % Boost per Round = Floor( (Round Delay )/385 )
With a 482 delay weapon, 50% Haste, and 5 minutes of constant attacking, this job ability is worth approximately 0.75 Weapon Skills without any enhancing gear.
* Enhancing gear does not need to be worn during the Weapon Skill to receive the bonus, and can make the bonus worth up to 1.5 Weapon Skills over 5 minutes with 50% Haste.
Upper bonus limit of 30% per WS.
*Restraint Weapon Skill Damage bonus only increases in whole percentage points.
*Bonus resets every WS.

retaliation.description: Allows you to counterattack but reduces movement speed.
retaliation.name: Retaliation
notes: Either a 40% flat rate or a 20-50% Retaliation rate, based on main-hand delay. See references.
Proc system is very similar to Counter, but does not negate damage and does reward TP for successful Retaliations.
*Does not generate Enmity or put the user on the monster hate list. Must be facing the monster.
*Retaliation cannot proc on an attack blocked with a shield.
-14% Movement Speed but is a different term then Movement Gear.
*With Retaliation up and wearing +12% in gear you will be at -4%.

retinal glare.description: Deals light damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area originating from the caster. Additional effect: Flash.
retinal glare.name: Retinal Glare

retrace.description: Transports qualified target party member to their nation of allegiance in the past.
retrace.name: Retrace
notes: If you have not yet allied with a past nation, this spell will have no effect on you.

retreat.description: Orders the avatar to hold back.
retreat.name: Retreat
notes: Calls the pet back to you.
Useful for Avatar pulling linking monsters.

retribution.description: Delivers a single attack. Damage varies with TP.
retribution.name: Retribution
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set WAR / MNK / WHM / BLM / PLD / BRD / DRG / SMN / GEO as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest Blood and Glory
* Or while equipped with Kaja Staff or Xoanon regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
** This is the only way for RDM / BST to use the weapon skill.
PLD cannot complete the Blood and Glory quest before level 71 due to the necessity of equipping Pole of Trials.

retrieve.description: Orders your automaton to return to your side.
retrieve.name: Retrieve
notes: Calls your Automaton back to yourself.
Use of Retrieve will not stop an automaton from finishing its casting of a spell it has already started and its target is still within range. Resting or Deactivate however, will.

reverse flourish.description: Converts remaining finishing moves into TP. Requires at least one finishing move.
reverse flourish.effect: Each merit level adds 30TP to Reverse Flourish
reverse flourish.name: Reverse Flourish
notes: Returns TP based on the number of Finishing moves consumed.
:Equation: TP Return = (95 + Reverse Flourish Bonus)  (Finishing moves) + (Square Mod)(Finishing moves)^2 + 30(Merit Level)
(Square Mod) is normally 5, but may be increased by equipment.
Flourishes II Job Points are added to the "Reverse Flourish Bonus" term and thus are applied on a "per Finishing Move" basis.

reward.description: Restores your pet's HP. Special items required.
reward.effect: Recast is reduced by 00:00:03 per merit level.
reward.name: Reward
notes: Reward can only be used while equipping Pet Food in the ammo slot.
Reward will grant a 3-minute Regen effect to the pet, based on type of pet food used. See the pet food page for more details.
*This Regen effect is strengthened by Beast Healer merits.
Increasing MND improves Reward a variable amount based on which food is being used.
Reward Potency from gear cap at +50%, similar to the 50% Cure Potency cap, so any Reward potency over 50% is wasted.

rock crusher.description: Delivers an earth elemental attack.  Damage varies with TP.
rock crusher.name: Rock Crusher

rogue's roll.description: Improves critical hit rate for party members within area of effect.
rogue's roll.name: Rogue's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Thief Die.

role reversal.description: Swaps the Master's current HP with the Automaton's current HP.
role reversal.effect: Increases HP granted by 5% per merit level past the first.
role reversal.name: Role Reversal
notes: This ability instantaneously swaps current HP between Automaton and Master.
If your Automaton's current HP is higher than your maximum HP, you'll simply get your HP refilled.
* The Automaton will be left with the same HP you had prior to using the ability.

rudra's storm.description: Deals quintuple damage and weighs target down. Damage varies with TP.
rudra's storm.name: Rudra's Storm
notes: Can only be used if you set THF / BRD / DNC as your main job.
Daka, +1, +2, +3, and Khandroma do not give Aftermath
Inflicts an accurate Gravity effect.
* Effect is about -26% movement speed.
* Effect duration is 1 minute.

ruinator.description: Delivers a fourfold attack. Accuracy varies with TP.
ruinator.name: Ruinator
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / DRK / BST / RNG / RUN as your main job.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.
The bolded, italicized Skillchain property is only available while under the effects of Aeonic Aftermath.

run wild.description: Your familiar will gain heightened powers, but will disappear when the effect expires.
run wild.name: Run Wild
notes: Grants a +25% bonus to Pet: Attack, Accuracy, Magic Attack Bonus, Evasion, and Defense bonus and adds a Regen effect, but the pet disappears after 5 minutes.
* Glitch: Using Leave at 4:58 and resummoning a pet at the 5 minute point when it normally would have disappeared grants you a ghost pet. See references.
*Pet is required to be engaged before ability will activate

runeist's roll.description: Enhances magic evasion for party members within area of effect.
runeist's roll.name: Runeist's Roll
notes: This ability is permanently added to a player's list of Phantom Rolls after using a Rune Fencer Die.

ruthless stroke.description: Delivers a fourfold attack. Damage varies with TP.

saber dance.description: Increases Double Attack rate but renders waltzes unusable. Double Attack rate gradually decreases.
saber dance.effect: Each merit level past the first increases Samba duration by 5%.
saber dance.name: Saber Dance
notes: Activating Saber Dance blocks Waltzes until it wears off or is cancelled.
Saber Dance grants 50% Double Attack that decays to 20% in the first 30 seconds (approximately 1%/second). It then remains at 20% for the rest of its duration.
Using Saber Dance automatically cancels Fan Dance, and vice versa.
Saber Dance stacks additively with Double Attack gear, but overwrites the Double Attack job trait.
* DNC/WAR (10% Double Attack Job Trait) with Saber Dance up has a base Double Attack rate of 20%.
* DNC/NIN with 10% Double Attack in gear and Saber Dance up has a base Double Attack rate of 30%.
Samba duration increase will still apply even if Saber Dance is removed after Samba activation.

saboteur.description: Spells you cast gain enhanced magic accuracy, and if it is enfeeblement magic, its effect and duration will be enhanced.
saboteur.name: Saboteur
notes: As of the February 2019 update[http://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/55025-February.-8-2019-%28JST%29-Version-Update?p=613079&viewfull=1#post613079], Saboteur lasts its whole duration and will not wear off even when using an enfeebling spell during its duration.
Increases magic accuracy for the duration.
* This effect’s value scales with character level and applies to all spells.
* Magic Accuracy can be further enhanced by 2 per Job Point Saboteur Effect level.
Increases the potency and duration(Base Duration) of all Enfeebling Magic cast by +100% (double) during its duration.
*On NMs, the potency and duration are only +25%; however the Empyrean Gantherots still grant their full bonus.
Does not affect the dMND or dINT part of spells (such as of Distract or Frazzle).
Increases the chance of Immunobreak occurring when using enfeebling spells during the effect.

sacrifice.description: Transfers one enfeebling effect from target party member to yourself. Afflatus Solace : Increases effect removal potency.
sacrifice.name: Sacrifice
notes: Removes 1 negative Status Effect from the target that is normally removed by Viruna , Cursna , Blindna , Poisona , or Paralyna and applies that status effect to the White Mage .
Cannot remove a Status Effect that would disable the White Mage such as Silence or Petrification .

With Afflatus Solace active, it can remove up to 7 Status Effects , including those normally removed by Erase . In addition to removing spells removed by Erase , Sacrifice is the only spell that can remove the effect of Curse (Recovery) .
Cannot be combined with Accession or Divine Seal (for Divine Veil ).

seigan.description: Grants a bonus to "Third Eye" when using two-handed weapons, but increases recast and casting times.
seigan.name: Seigan
notes: Seigan works only with two-handed weapons.
Seigan and Hasso cannot be used at the same time. Between the two, the most recently activated job ability will be active.
While Seigan is active, a 50% increase in casting and recast time will be applied to any and all magic, songs, and Ninjutsu.
=== Third Eye Effects ===
While active, Seigan reduces the recast timer on Third Eye to 30 seconds.
Seigan also enhances the effects of Third Eye and makes it similar to the Utsusemi series of Ninjutsu.
*A monster's attack can either be evaded (anticipated) or counter-attacked with Seigan and Third Eye up.
*Each time a Samurai evades or counters with Seigan/Third Eye, there is a chance that Third Eye will be removed.
*The longer Third Eye has been active, the higher chance of cancellation.
*The base Counter rate under these circumstances is 25%, which is enhanced by Saotome Haidate/+1/+2 and Kogarasumaru.
**It is not known whether these pieces of gear can simply be worn for JA activation or must be full-timed, but the assumption has always been that they must be full-timed.
=== Zanshin effects ===
Seigan also allows Zanshin-related Counters for players using Samurai main job even without Third Eye or specific equipment.
*Counters occur at a rate of Zanshin rate4.
*These Counters are subject to accuracy checks and do not reward TP.

sekkanoki.description: Limits TP cost of next weapon skill to 1000

sentinel.description: Reduces physical damage taken and increases enmity.
sentinel.name: Sentinel
notes: Sentinel grants a -% Physical Damage Taken for the duration of the ability, starting at -90% and decreasing by -8% every tick, ending at -50%, where it will remain until the effect wears off.
*With Valor Leggings equipped, Sentinel starts at -103% Physical Damage taken (capped at -100%), and decays at the same rate, eventually reaching -50% as well.
*The effect is applied after regular damage taken, and unlike that it's applied by exact percentages, not as fractions of 256.
This ability gives +100 equipment enmity for its duration, and then creates a large hate spike (similar to Provoke).
:*+50 enmity if Paladin is subjob.
With Guardian merited, your hate will also decay more slowly during the duration of Sentinel.
*-19% Cumulative Enmity lost when taking damage per merit level.

shadowbind.name: Shadowbind
notes: Cannot be removed by Erase.
When used on mobs that are immune to Shadowbind, the following message will be displayed:
:"XXX uses Shadowbind, but misses."
The only type of mob (excluding Notorious Monsters) that Shadowbind does not have a 100% success rate on are Ghrahs, located in the Grand Palace of Hu'Xzoi and the The Garden of Ru'Hmet.
When used from a Ranger support job, there will be a penalty for accuracy.
With Orion Bracers +1 and 20/20 Job Points in Shadowbind, the maximum duration is 30 + 12 + 20 = 62 seconds.
Any form of damage tends to break Shadowbind very quickly, even sometimes DoT effects still on the mob or a single ranged attack.

* As a result, players tend to use Shadowbind to deal with pulling enmity/hate resets, running from an engagement, or letting the tank/party recover after a NM uses a dangerous move (such as Death) rather than as a safe damage tool.
* Ex. Arise Weakness is 3 minutes, so a Corsair using Random Deal and two Rangers can keep a mob bound long enough for the tank to fully recover.

shield bash.description: Delivers an attack that can stun the target. Shield required.
shield bash.name: Shield Bash
notes: Stun rate is fairly high, but not 100%.
*Being an instant ability, Shield Bash is very effective at stunning mob TP moves.
Items which enhance Shield Bash only increase its base damage.
Bash damage formula: damage = (Base + ShieldSizeBonus + JobPoints + BashGear) * Randomizer.
*The Randomizer is a random number between 1.0 and 1.05.
Base dmg is 27 at lvl 99 PLD. This value scales with level. 15 base dmg at PLD level 45.
Shield size bonuses are:
*Size 1 Shield (Bucklers): +0
*Size 2 Shield (Round Shields): +13
*Size 3 Shield (Kite Shields): +40
*Size 4 Shield (Tower Shields): +67
*Size 5 Shield (Aegis, Srivatsa): +0
*Size 6 Shield (Ochain, Duban): +0
The Dispel effect from Cab. Gauntlets +2 and Cab. Gauntlets +3 does not display a log message, making it difficult to tell what was dispelled.
*The effect is also incredibly accurate, so if the mob had dispellable effects, you removed one.
*Landed on 139 mobs at 100% rate with a non ilvl weapon and no Magic Accuracy gear. (Small verified samples. See references.)
Shield Bash does feed TP to mobs if it deals damage.
*TP feed is equivalent to the TP fed by a normal melee hit, based on the main hand weapon's delay.

smash axe.name: Smash Axe

smiting breath.description: Orders the wyvern to attack with its breath.
smiting breath.name: Smiting Breath
notes: Forces your wyvern to use an Elemental Breath.

snake eye.description: Your next roll will automatically be a 1.
snake eye.effect: Each merit rank after the first increases the likelihood that the next roll will result in a sum total of XI when starting from V or higher by 10%. This results in a 40% chance if the ability is increased to 5/5.
snake eye.name: Snake Eye
notes: Forces the next Double-Up/Phantom Roll/Wild Card to be a 1.
*Merits past the first rank also allow Snake Eye to give a small chance to automatically roll the number necessary to boost the total to 11 if the previous total is between 5 and 9.
Very useful for adjusting rolls to get maximum potency.
* Be careful that you don't waste it/Wild Card.

snarl.description: Transfer enmity to a pet invoked with the "Call Beast" ability.
snarl.name: Snarl
notes: Transfers 99% of the master's current enmity to the jug pet.
Can work on multiple monsters at once.
Must be within 11.5 yalms distance from jug pet in order to use ability.

sneak.description: Lessens chance of being detected by sound.
sneak.name: Sneak
notes: Duration is variable, but has a minimum of 5 minutes.
This spell can be used to avoid the attention of any aggressive monster that detects by Sound.
The Sneak status will wear off immediately upon being attacked or performing an aggressive action against an enemy.

sneak attack.description: Allows your next melee attack to deal extra damage when striking an enemy from behind.
sneak attack.effect: Each merit level reduces recast by 00:00:02.
sneak attack.name: Sneak Attack
notes: Sneak Attack will wear off after the next melee attack round or weapon skill and have no effect unless the player satisfies one of these activation conditions:
* The monster is currently affected by Bully.
* The player is behind the monster.
* The player is invisible due to the ability Hide.
If an activation condition is met, the ability will force the next melee swing to critical hit.
* This critical will apply only to the first hit of physical or hybrid Weapon Skills that do not normally Critical Hit. However, it does not work on magical weapon skills.
If an activation condition is met, this ability guarantees also the next melee swing will hit (100% hit rate).

* The accuracy bonus also applies to the offhand hit of the melee round or Weapon Skill when dual-wielding, but does not apply to additional attacks (e.g. Triple Attack) or multiple hits of most Weapon Skills (e.g. only the first swing of Asuran Fists).
** There is some old testing indicating that it does apply to multiple hits of Sturmwind and Tachi: Enpi (referenced below), but it is unclear whether that is still the case.

If an activation condition is met and the player is playing Thief main job, they will also receive an additional base damage bonus on the next melee swing or first swing of a physical or hybrid weapon skill.
* Weapon damage is calculated by adding the DEX stat to weapon damage at the time the blow lands.
* When added to Weapon Skills, the DEX is added to base damage after the fTP multiplier is applied.
**For high fTP WSs (like Rudra's Storm) this means that the base damage boost plays a relatively minor role in determining the final damage.
Ranged attacks are unaffected, and do not cancel the ability.
Counter attacks are affected and do not cancel the ability, but are only possible on monsters that do not reposition or when used with Bully.

sniper shot.description: Lowers enemy's INT. Chance of critical varies with TP.
sniper shot.name: Sniper Shot
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG / COR as your main or sub job.
Additional effect gives INT-10 and decays over time.
Accuracy of WS additional effect has increased june 2017 [http://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/52747?p=597349#post597349 SE official]

sonic thrust.description: Delivers an area attack. Damage varies with TP.
sonic thrust.name: Sonic Thrust
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / PLD / DRG as your main or sub job.
Rectangular frontal attack, maybe 3' wide and 10' long.

soporific.description: Puts all enemies within range to sleep.
soporific.name: Soporific
notes: Area of effect is centered around the caster.
Is overwritten by and does not overwrite all forms of BRD, BLM, and WHM sleep.
Interrupts all enemy actions; thus can be used as an alternative to Stun .

soul enslavement.name: Soul Enslavement
notes: Drains a monster's TP, up to some per-swing maximum. (On Locus Dire Bats, was absorbing 500 TP per swing.)
* The per-swing maximum TP drain is proportional to your weapon delay.
**i.e. Foenaria scythe with 513 delay would drain up to 666 TP per swing, whereas single-wield Ternion Dagger +1 with 175 delay would only drain up to 270 TP per swing.
* Subsequent multi-attacks in the same attack round still drain TP but have a progressively lower cap.

**i.e. Foenaria's initial hit consistently drains 666 TP, and the second hit from a double attack proc consistently only drained 366 TP. Ternion Dagger +1 triple attack proc resulted in the initial hit draining 270 TP, the second hit draining 168 TP, and the third hit draining 116 TP.

* TP continues to be removed from the target even if the Dark Knight is at 3000 TP, in the same fashion as Drain still dealing damage to the target even if you cast at Max HP resulting in the log showing 0 HP drained. (Tested via Ballista and can observe target continues to lose TP even though log reports 0 TP drained while DRK is at 3000 TP.)
Removes Endark, Endark II, or Auspice if it is on the player.
Effect does NOT stack with Blood Weapon. Blood Weapon's HP drain effect will take precedence and since only one on-hit effect is allowed at a time, no TP will be drained/removed from the target.

soul jump.description: Delivers a high jumping attack on a targeted enemy which suppresses enmity. Effect enhanced when wyvern is present.
soul jump.effect: Each High Jump recast merit level decreases recast by 00:00:04.
soul jump.name: Soul Jump
notes: No longer shares recast timer with High Jump
Soul Jump receives three bonuses when the user's Wyvern pet is present:
* Forced Critical Hit
* Triple TP return
* 50% attack bonus

soul voice.description: Enhances the effects of your songs.
soul voice.effect: Reduces casting time of songs by 2% per rank.
soul voice.name: Soul Voice
notes: Does NOT stack with Marcato for duration OR effects.
Doubles the effect of the following songs:
*Debuff song types that have their effects doubled:
**Requiem
**Elegy
**Threnody
**Nocturne
*Party Buff song types that have their effects doubled:
**Paeon
**Ballad
**Minne
**Minuet
**Madrigal
**Prelude
**Aubade
**Pastoral
**Fantasia
**Operetta
**Capriccio
**Round
**Gavotte
**March
**Etude
**Carol
**Sirvente
**Dirge
Doubles the Magic Accuracy or duration of the following songs.
*Debuff song types that have their Magic Accuracy doubled:
**Lullaby
**Finale
**Virelai
*Party Buff song types that have their duration doubled (multiplicative with Troubadour effect):
**Hymnus
**Mazurka
**Scherzo

souleater.description: Consumes (10% of your) HP to enhance attacks.
souleater.effect: Reduces recast time by 00:00:12 per each merit point.
souleater.name: Souleater
notes: Consumes and converts 10% of your current HP directly into physical damage, adding it onto your normal melee hits.
*For Sub Job Dark Knight, 10% of your current HP will be consumed but only 5% will be converted into damage.
*The bonus physical damage gained from sacrificed HP is boosted/penalized by Damage Type.
*For Weapon Skills: The bonus physical damage gained from sacrificed HP is boosted by the Weapon Skill Damage stat, but not PDIF or FTP.
Enhances accuracy by 25 while active.
Muted Soul gives -10 Enmity per merit rank to Souleater activation and for the duration.
Many Notorious Monsters have an innate resistance to Souleater now, notably Absolute Virtue.
Commonly combined with Blood Weapon and Haste buffs, so HP is not lost, and large amounts of damage can be dealt quickly.
* Attacking something with Phalanx or Souleater resistance can rapidly deplete Dark Knight HP in these cases.
Wearing multiple 10->12% pieces will still only result in 12% converted, but Dweomer Scythe appears to stack.
Does not synergize with the empyrean aftermath from Caladbolg.

sound blast.description: Lowers Intelligence of enemies within range.
sound blast.name: Sound Blast
notes: Area of effect is centered around the caster.

spectral floe.description: Deals ice damage to enemies within range. Additional effect: Terror
spectral floe.name: Spectral Floe

spectral jig.description: Allows you to evade enemies by making you undetectable by sight or sound.
spectral jig.name: Spectral Jig
notes: Applies the effects of Sneak and Invisible (Status) on yourself.
If used while Sneak is still active, the ability will have no effect and neither Sneak nor Invisible (Status) will be reapplied.
The effects of Sneak and Invisible (Status) will wear off simultaneously.
Jig duration job points are added to base duration before modification.

spinning attack.description: Delivers a twofold area attack. Radius varies with TP.
spinning attack.name: Spinning Attack
notes: Radius scales to 5y at 2334 TP
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

spinning axe.description: Single-hit attack. Damage varies with TP.
spinning axe.name: Spinning Axe
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / DRK / BST / RUN as your main or sub job.

spinning scythe.description: Delivers an area of effect attack. Attack radius varies with TP.
spinning scythe.name: Spinning Scythe

spinning slash.name: Spinning Slash
notes: Can only be used if you set PLD / DRK / RUN as your main job, or by the following jobs with the weapons listed below:
* WAR while wearing a Beryllium Sword/+1

spiral hell.description: Delivers a single attack. Damage varies with TP.
spiral hell.name: Spiral Hell
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set WAR / DRK / BST as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest Souls in Shadow
* Or while equipped with Kaja Scythe or Drepanum regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
** This is the only way for BLM to use the weapon skill.
DRK cannot complete the Souls in Shadow quest before level 71 due to the necessity of equipping Scythe of Trials.
Accuracy of the Weapon Skill itself raised in June 2014 version update

spiral spin.description: Additional effect: Lowers accuracy. Chance of effect varies with TP.
spiral spin.name: Spiral Spin
notes: Accuracy down effect is -15.
Ignores Shadow Images

spirit bond.description: Enables the dragoon to take some damage on behalf of their wyvern. Using Healing Breath also restores the wyvern's HP.
spirit bond.name: Spirit Bond
notes: This ability takes the wyvern's damage taken(after pet DT, but before steady wing) and splits it evenly with the Dragoon.
*The damage taken by the Dragoon is not affected by the Dragoon's DT nor any Phalanx a Dragoon may have, but can be mitigated by Stoneskin.
*Also does not trigger 'converts damage to MP/TP' effects.
Healing Breath cures the primary target and the wyvern at full, unaltered, potency.
You must be within 6.25' of your wyvern to use this ability

spirit jump.description: Delivers a short jumping attack on a targeted enemy which suppresses enmity. Effect enhanced when wyvern is present.
spirit jump.effect: Each Jump recast merit level decreases recast by 00:00:02.
spirit jump.name: Spirit Jump
notes: No longer shares a recast timer with Jump.
Spirit Jump receives three bonuses when the user's Wyvern pet is present:
* Doubled TP return
* Forced Critical Hit
* 25% Attack bonus

spirit link.description: Sacrifice HP to heal your wyvern.
spirit link.effect: Each merit level decreases recast by 00:00:03.
spirit link.name: Spirit Link
notes: Upon activation, a certain amount of the player's current HP (12-17%) is deducted and the Wyvern's HP is restored. The Wyvern also gains a Regen effect similar to when a Beastmaster uses Pet Food.
* Restores ( Player HP Lost + MND +  )2 HP to the Wyvern
** "" = Wyvern Level  0.7
* The percentage of HP deducted from the player is constant, even if the Wyvern can't be healed for the full amount.
* Spirit Link may not be activated unless the Wyvern has less than 100% HP or Empathy is merited.
*Regen Potency = Player level/3 per tick. 90 sec duration. So 33/tick at level 99 for 990 total HP recovered.

Spirit Link removes all applicable DoT effects such as Burn, Poison, Requiem, Dia, Bio, and Doom all at once regardless of how many you have active. If you have Dragoon Empyrean hand armor, such as Pel. Vambraces +2Peltast's Vambraces +2 equipped, it will additionally remove 2 effects that are able to be removed by Erase as well as Blind, Silence, Paralysis, Curse, Petrification, and Amnesia.
If the player has Stoneskin, the HP from Stoneskin is deducted first. The rest of the HP is then deducted from the player's current HP.
* Stoneskin wears off if it can not cover the whole HP required.
Transfers half of the Wyvern's TP to its master.
*The corresponding Armets do not increase TP transfer.
With merits in Empathy, Spirit Link may be used to transfer buffs and EXP (200XP per merit level) to the Wyvern.
*The wyvern caps levels after 1,000 EXP.
**For example 4/5 empathy will level the wyvern up four times in one Spirit Link.
*Everytime the wyvern levels up it will receive a 4% attack and defense boost and the Dragoon will receive 2% Job Ability Haste.
**Attack and defense boost cap at 20%
**Haste caps at 10% after 5 wyvern levels.
***If the wyvern is defeated/dismissed, the Dragoon zones, or enters or starts any instance then these buffs are removed from the Dragoon.

spirit surge.description: Adds your wyvern's strength to your own.
spirit surge.name: Spirit Surge
notes: Your wyvern must be summoned in order to use this ability.
Your wyvern will be absorbed upon using the ability and cannot be resummoned until the ability wears off.
The following effects will be gained by the Dragoon upon using the ability:
:*Accuracy +50
:*Attack and Defense +25%
:*HP increased by 25% of the wyvern's max HP and is healed by the wyvern's remaining HP.
:*A STR boost based on level (1 + level/5), +20 at level 99
:*A Job Ability Haste boost of 25%.
::*The Haste from Spirit Surge is applied towards the Job Ability Haste cap (25%).
Under the effect of Spirit Surge, Jump, High Jump and Super Jump receive additional effects:
*The recast timers for Jump, High Jump and Super Jump reset to 0:00:00.
*Jump lowers the target's defense (-20% for 1 minute).
**Defense Down effects do not stack with each other.
***Dia, Box Step, Defense Down, and Geo-Frailty all stack together.

***Defense Down effects such as Acid Bolts, Angon, Spirit Surged Jumps, Tachi: Ageha, Full Break, Shell Crusher, Tenebral Crush, Sweeping Gouge, Tourbillion, and Frightful Roar, etc do not stack. Only one may be active at a time.
*High Jump lowers the target's TP proportionately to the amount of damage dealt (TP is reduced by damage  2).
*Super Jump will reduce the hate of the nearest person behind the Dragoon by half.
*Jumps will gain 100% accuracy.
Has no notable effect on Spirit Jump and Soul Jump, and it should be noted that the Wyvern is not considered "present" for these jumps after using Spirit Surge.

spirit taker.description: Converts damage dealt to own MP. Damage varies with TP.
spirit taker.name: Spirit Taker

spirited etude.name: Spirited Etude
notes: Attributes cap at 9 before Etude-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥450.

spirits within.description: Delivers an unavoidable attack. Damage varies with HP and TP.
spirits within.name: Spirits Within
notes: Spirits Within uses special damage equations that depend on only current HP and TP rather than typical melee stats. Among other things, this means:
* Spirits Within is unaffected by the target's Defense and Evasion.
* Spirits Within is unaffected by the job abilities Invincible and Perfect Dodge.
* As this is classified as Breath damage, some Notorious Monsters take less or more than normal damage.Category:Elemental Weapon Skill
Does not interrupt skillchains because it lacks any skillchain properties.

split shot.description: Ignores enemy's defense. Amount ignored varies with TP.
split shot.name: Split Shot
notes: Can only be used if you set RNG / COR as your main or sub job.

spontaneity.description: Reduces casting time for the next magic spell the target casts.
spontaneity.name: Spontaneity
notes: The next spell from the target will have nearly no casting time and no recast delay, similar to Chainspell or Quick Magic.
Note that this ability can be targeted to other party members so that their next spell receives the effect.

sprout smack.description: Additional effect: Slow . Duration of effect varies with TP.
sprout smack.name: Sprout Smack
notes: Slow effect is a static 15%.

spur.name: Spur
notes: Grants Pet: Store TP +20 for its duration.
With Job Points, Spur also adds 3 attack per level. The maximum gained is +60 attack.

starburst.description: Delivers a light/dark attack.  Damage varies with TP.
starburst.name: Starburst
notes: Starburst and Sunburst have a base damage of your level + 2 (last verified in Jan 2024)
* The formula is unchanged from the original pre-2014 versions, so it doesn't use your weapon's item level. The base damage is calculated as follows:
Starburst/Sunburst Base Magical WS Damage: floor( ( Level + 2 + floor(Stat Modifier) ) * fTP) + dSTAT + Magic Damage (Statistic)
The final Weapon Skill damage will use the monster's Blunt Damage Taken % instead of Magic Damage Taken % for the TMDA term.
* It's not a hybrid weapon skill, there's no physical damage calculation. It just uniquely uses Blunt DT in the magic damage calculation.
The element will be  Light or  Dark picked at random.

stardiver.description: Delivers a fourfold attack. Decreases enemy critical hit evasion. Damage varies with TP.
stardiver.name: Stardiver
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / SAM / DRG as your main job.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.
Critical Hit evasion down is +5% Critical Hit Rate and the effect lasts for 60 seconds.
The bolded, italicized Skillchain property is only available while under the effects of Aeonic Aftermath.
Accuracy of WS additional effect has increased june 2017 [http://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/52747?p=597349#post597349 SE official]

starlight.description: Restores MP.  Amount restored varies with TP.
starlight.name: Starlight
notes: Self-targeted WS, so it can be used without claiming a monster in any situation where you can pull out your weapon (within 30' of a monster).
This Weapon Skill restores MP equal to: floor( Club Skill  0.11 )  MP Multiplier
* See table above for MP Multiplier value which varies by TP
* This equation only uses the skill in the Combat Skills menu, not invisible sources of skill like those on weapons.

stay.description: Commands the Pet to stay in one place.
stay.name: Stay
notes: Makes your pet stay where they are.
Shares a recast timer with Heel and Leave.
Increases the speed a pet will heal when it is not engaged.
*Similar to the /heal command for player characters.

steady wing.description: Creates a barrier that temporarily absorbs a certain amount of damage dealt to your wyvern.
steady wing.name: Steady Wing
notes: Grants a Stoneskin effect to the wyvern equal to 30% of its maximum HP at the time of ability use plus the difference between the max and current HP.
:Equation: Damage Negated = Max Wyvern HP  0.3  + (Max Wyvern HP - Current Wyvern HP)
:*  As a result, this ability is strongly enhanced by Wyvern HP gear.

Note: As is also the case with the Ptero. Mail +2/ +3, macroing in and out HP gear will not improve Steady Wing. A tic must pass with the HP+ equipment still on before the HP gains are counted for the ability.

steal.name: Steal
notes: Steals an item from the enemy.
* Can steal a buff from the enemy (sometimes an undispellable one) instead of an item if Aura Steal is meritted.
Steal+X is commonly interpreted to mean "Steal success rate +X%"
* The initial success rate is determined by your THF level and the item's base resale price (e.g. when you vendor it to an NPC).
** Non-vendorable items also have a resale value internally, used for trades like region points, fame, or conquest points.

* For high value items, it's said that the "Steal" cap can't be reached even with all available equipment. Answer by Salalaruru in "Oshiete! Kaihatsu-san!" (Teach us, Developer!) - April 19, 2018: https://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/353?p=606486#post606486
"Steal" takes items from a separate treasure pool than the treasure pool used when defeating an enemy.

stealth shot.description: Your next attack will generate less enmity.
stealth shot.effect: Reduces Enmity generated by next shot/Weapon Skill by 10 per merit level.
stealth shot.name: Stealth Shot
notes: The enmity reduction from Stealth Shot does not contribute to or affect the equipment -Enmity cap of -50.
* It is applied in a separate step, multiplicatively (e.g. Enmity-50 plus fully merited Stealth Shot would be equivalent to a 75% enmity reduction).
Stealth Shot only affects the first hit on Barrage.
Can be used in conjunction with Flashy Shot to lower the extra enmity it causes.

steel cyclone.description: Delivers a single attack. Damage varies with TP.
steel cyclone.name: Steel Cyclone
notes: Can only be used if:
* You set WAR / DRK / RUN as your main job, have the requisite skill level, and have completed the unlocking quest The Weight of Your Limits
* Or while equipped with Kaja Chopper or Lycurgos regardless of job, skill level, or quest completion.
WAR can not complete the The Weight of Your Limits quest before level 71 due to the necessity of equipping Axe of Trials.

stinking gas.description: Lowers Vitality of enemies within range.
stinking gas.name: Stinking Gas
notes: Area of effect is centered around the caster.

stona.name: Stona
notes: Target can be any player character.

stone.description: Deals Earth damage to an enemy.
stone.name: Stone
notes: Players who choose Black Mage as their initial job at character creation will receive an version of this scroll of Stone.

stone ii.description: Deals Earth damage to an enemy.
stone ii.name: Stone II

stone iii.description: Deals Earth damage to an enemy.
stone iii.name: Stone III

stone iv.description: Deals Earth damage to an enemy.
stone iv.name: Stone IV
notes: +20 Magic Accuracy .

stone v.description: Deals Earth damage to an enemy.
stone v.name: Stone V

stone vi.description: Deals Earth damage to an enemy.
stone vi.name: Stone VI

stonega.description: Deals Earth damage to enemies within area of effect .
stonega.name: Stonega

stonega ii.description: Deals Earth damage to enemies within area of effect .
stonega ii.name: Stonega II

stonega iii.description: Deals Earth damage to enemies within area of effect .
stonega iii.name: Stonega III

stonega iv.description: Deals Stone damage to enemies within area of effect.
stonega iv.name: Stonega IV
notes: Enemy-only spell used by high level Notorious Monsters.

stoneja.description: Deals Earth damage to enemies within area of effect . Successive use enhances spell potency.
stoneja.name: Stoneja

stonera.description: Deals Earth damage to enemies within area of effect .
stonera.name: Stonera

stonera ii.description: Deals Earth damage to enemies within area of effect .
stonera ii.name: Stonera II

stonera iii.description: Deals Stone damage to enemies within area of effect .
stonera iii.name: Stonera III

stoneskin.description: Absorbs a certain amount of damage from physical and magical attacks.
stoneskin.name: Stoneskin
notes: Absorbs a certain amount of damage (as if you had more HP), which depends on MND and Enhancing Magic Skill
If ( Enhancing Magic Skill ÷ 3) + MND < 80 : Stoneskin Strength = ( Enhancing Magic Skill ÷ 3) + MND
This equation is likely wrong because it is discontinuous with the below one.
If 80 ≤ ( Enhancing Magic Skill ÷ 3) + MND ≤ 130 : Stoneskin Strength = (2× Enhancing Magic Skill ÷ 3) + 2*MND - 60
If ( Enhancing Magic Skill ÷ 3) + MND ≥ 130 : Stoneskin Strength = Enhancing Magic Skill + 3×MND - 190
Stoneskin caps at 350 HP absorbed, though the enhancing items above bypass the cap and allow for up to 475 HP Stoneskins.
Gear which enhances Stoneskin does not affect party members when used with Accession .

striking flourish.description: Allows you to deliver a twofold attack. Requires at least two finishing moves.
striking flourish.name: Striking Flourish
notes: Forces a Double Attack on the first hit of your next attack round and grants the first swing of the Double Attack a base damage bonus equal to 100% of your CHR (at the time the hit lands).
Will stack with Weapon Skills.
*Bonus damage is added outside of the WSC term, like with Sneak Attack for Thieves.
Striking Flourish, Sneak Attack, Ternary Flourish, and Climactic Flourish are mutually exclusive, so the four cannot be combined.
Charis Casaque +1/+2 and Maculele Casaque/+1/+2/+3 increase the critical hit rate of the first hit of your next round and the forced Double Attack.

stringing pummel.description: Delivers a sixfold attack.  Chance of critical hit varies with TP.
stringing pummel.name: Stringing Pummel
notes: Can only be used if you set PUP as your main job.
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

stun.name: Stun
notes: The stun effect can be resisted, and some monsters either build resistance or are outright immune to it.

Stun can be used in conjuncture with Chainspell as Red Mage with Black Mage or Dark Knight subjob to keep a monster immobile for the duration of Chainspell . This is very useful when a monster performs a large number of highly damaging attacks. For instance, when fighting Dynamis Lord .
The relatively high Magic Hit Rate of this spell's Stun effect is thought to be caused by multiple (perhaps three) resist sub-states.
+200 Magic Accuracy . [ 1 ]

sturmwind.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Attack varies with TP.
sturmwind.name: Sturmwind
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / DRK / RUN as your main or sub job.
The English description of this weapon skill was incorrect for years.
* It claimed "damage varies with TP" instead of "attack varies with TP."
When used with Sneak Attack, both hits are guaranteed to hit.

stutter step.description: Lowers a target's magic resistance. If successful, will earn you finishing move.
stutter step.name: Stutter Step
notes: Lowers Magic Evasion by 3 per Daze level.
Applies a stackable debuff, Weakened Daze up to 10 times (indicated by a level).
*May only reach up to level 5 when applied with a Dancer subjob.
Grants 2 Finishing moves if it hits (can miss, be Blinked, or be Utsusemi-ed).
*Grants only 1 Finishing move if the target is already afflicted with Weakened Daze (lv.10) or if used with Dancer support job.

stymie.description: Greatly increases the accuracy of the next enfeebling magic spell.
stymie.name: Stymie
notes: Grants your next enfeebling magic spell 100% magic accuracy, however, it will still be subject to forced resist states.
This job ability will not overcome an enemy's complete immunity to a spell and will have no effect.
"Resist!" type traits take priority over a Stymie-powered spell and will result in a resist if activated.
Stymie's effect will end once an enfeebling magic spell is successfully landed or after 60 seconds have elapsed.

sub-zero smash.description: Additional Effect: Paralysis. Damage varies with TP.
sub-zero smash.name: Sub-zero Smash
notes: Paralyze potency is 10%
Overwritten by, but does not overwrite Paralyze or Jubaku: Ichi

subduction.description: Deals Wind damage to enemies within range. Additional effect: Weight.
subduction.name: Subduction
notes: Gravity effect is about -76% movement speed.
Area of effect is centered around the caster.

sublimation.description: Stores MP while reducing HP.  The effect ends once the MP limit has been reached, or HP is too low.  Stored MP is gained when the ability is reactivated.
sublimation.name: Sublimation
notes: A base of 2 HP/tick is drained to accumulate MP varying by level.
: Lv35: 2HP => 2MP
: Lv45: 2HP => 3MP
: Lv55: 2HP => 4MP
: Lv65: 2HP => 5MP
: Lv75: 2HP => 6MP
: Lv85: 2HP => 7MP
: Lv95: 2HP => 8MP

The effect changes to "Sublimation: Complete" when the total MP stored is equal to 50% of your maximum HP or when the player's HP falls to orange level ( 3 MP/tick at level 99 with level 49 scholar sub job).

subtle sorcery.description: Reduces the amount of enmity generated from magic spells and increases magic accuracy.
subtle sorcery.name: Subtle Sorcery
notes: Reduces cumulative enmity from magic spells to 0.
Removes the forced resistance to spells when the target has a resistance rank of 50% or lower for Elemental Magic.
:*Does not change the damage reduction of magical resistant foes.
::*E.g. A foe with a 50% resistance tier will still take a reduction in damage, but it won't be further hindered by a resist.
:*Lower resistance tiers (greater resistance) impose greater and steeper magic accuracy requirements on the caster, which Subtle Sorcery will not address.

sudden lunge.description: Damage varies with TP. Additional effect: "Stun."
sudden lunge.name: Sudden Lunge
notes: This stun is very potent. Don't be surprised if a ordinary target remains stunned for 7 seconds or even longer.
Does not reduce the user's HP.

The stun effect does not always process regardless of the spell landing on the target. Additional effect is more likely to land as a user increases their Magic Accuracy and Blue Magic Skill . If the monster is being repetitively knocked back then the stun is not landing. After using Sudden Lunge, a successfully stunned target is not knocked back.
Due to being a single physical hit the accuracy for this spell caps at 95%.

sunburst.description: Delivers a light/dark attack.  Damage varies with TP.
sunburst.name: Sunburst
notes: Can only be used if you set WAR / MNK / WHM / PLD / GEO as your main or sub job.
Starburst and Sunburst have a base damage of your level + 2 (last verified in Jan 2024)
* The formula is unchanged from the original pre-2014 versions, so it doesn't use your weapon's item level. The base damage is calculated as follows:
Starburst/Sunburst Base Magical WS Damage: floor( ( Level + 2 + floor(Stat Modifier) ) * fTP) + dSTAT + Magic Damage (Statistic)
The final Weapon Skill damage will use the monster's Blunt Damage Taken % instead of Magic Damage Taken % for the TMDA term.
* It's not a hybrid weapon skill, there's no physical damage calculation. It just uniquely uses Blunt DT in the magic damage calculation.
The element will be  Light or  Dark picked at random.

super jump.description: Jumps out of targeted enemy's perception range.
super jump.effect: Each merit level decreases recast by 00:00:06.
super jump.name: Super Jump
notes: Does no damage to the enemy and gives no TP to the user, as such, it will not claim a monster.
Upon usage the user will set their enmity to 1 CE, but only if the user has registered hate on the enemy (not supertanking).
*This does not mean you have to face the enemy/they have to face you, or that you have to be actively tanking, you just need to have done at least an action that will generate enmity on the target.
*If the user is holding hate on multiple enemies, the user's enmity will be reset for all the targets when Super Jump is used.
*Does not reset aggro.
After usage, user will remain in the air for approximately 5 seconds. During this time the user cannot be targeted by any ally or enemy single-target or AoE attacks, spells, or abilities.
Spells being cast on the user will be interrupted unless the user lands before casting is complete.
Upon usage, the user's wyvern will perform a similar ability known as Super Climb which makes the wyvern untargetable for approximately 5 seconds.
*Super Climb has no effect on the enmity of the wyvern.
*If the user is paralyzed and Super Jump fails to activate, the wyvern will perform a Super Climb regardless.
While Spirit Surge is in effect the closest party member behind the user as seen from the enemy target’s position has their enmity reduced by 50%.
*When Spirit Surge is activated, Super Jump's recast time will be reset to 0:00.

sweeping gouge.description: Delivers a twofold attack. Additional effect: Weakens defense. Duration of effect varies with TP.
sweeping gouge.name: Sweeping Gouge
notes: ~16% Defense Down (40/256) effect. 90 second duration outside of Chain Affinity and/or Efflux use. 112 seconds at 1,500 TP. 135 seconds at 3,000 TP. 180 seconds under Azure Lore .

Defense Down effects do not stack with each other. Dia , Box Step , Defense Down , and Geo-Frailty all stack together. Defense Down effects such as Acid Bolts , Angon , Spirit Surged Jumps, Tachi: Ageha , Full Break , Shell Crusher , Tenebral Crush , Sweeping Gouge , Tourbillion , and Frightful Roar , etc do not stack. Only one may be active at a time.

* WAR / THF / DRK / SAM / BLU / COR while wearing a Hepatizon Sapara/+1 in the main hand
* WAR / RDM / PLD / BRD / DRG / COR / DNC while wearing a Hepatizon Rapier/+1 in the main hand
This is an fTP replicating weaponskill. See :Category:fTP Replicating WS for more info.

swift etude.name: Swift Etude
notes: Attributes cap at 15 before Etude-enhancing gear. Skill cap is ≥475.

swipe.description: Expends a single rune to deal damage to a target.
swipe.effect: Increases Lunge Accuracy by 3 per merit level.
swipe.name: Swipe
notes: Unlike Lunge, only one Rune is ever expended, regardless of how many runes are active. The last activated rune is consumed first.
Damage appears to be based on weapon skill and typical Magic multiplier terms. The proposed formula is:
* Damage = (Main-hand weapon Skill  (100 + Swipe Job Points)  100)  (0.75 + (n  100) )  Magic Multiplier Terms
**n = Lunge effect bonus from gear.
***For example: Aettir's "Lunge"+10 would be n=10
* Treated like an Elemental Magic damage source (can MB, subject to day/weather boosts, etc.)
* Does not feed TP to target
* Swipe is nullified by blink/shadows.

sword madrigal.description: Enhances melee attack accuracy for party members within area of effect.
sword madrigal.name: Sword Madrigal
notes: Accuracy bonus caps at 45 before merits or Madrigal-enhancing gear. Skill cap is < 435

swordplay.description: Increases accuracy and evasion until you take severe damage.
swordplay.name: Swordplay
notes: It is likely that "severe damage" refers to attacks that do most of your HP in damage per the description of Earthen Armor (>75%), Migawari: Ichi (>75~100% depending on Ninjutsu skill), and Sentinel's Scherzo (>75%).
This ability's potency increases accuracy and evasion upon activation and then each tick:
*+3 Accuracy/Evasion upon activation.
**The equipment modifier adds to this base bonus (3 + X*3) where each X+ is a ticks worth.
***E.g. +9 from the Mitons +3 would result in +30 Acc/Evasion upon activation.
*+3/tick for a player regardless of being main or sub RUN.
Caps at +60 Evasion/Accuracy regardless of being main or sub RUN.

tabula rasa.description: Optimizes both white and black magic capabilities while allowing charge-free stratagem use.
tabula rasa.name: Tabula Rasa
notes: Resets Light Arts and Dark Arts timers and restores all Stratagems upon activation
While under the effect of Tabula Rasa, Light Arts, Dark Arts, and all Stratagems have no ability recast delay. Additionally, Stratagems do not consume charges upon use.
During Tabula Rasa, unique spells (Kaustra and Embrava) are accessible.

During Tabula Rasa, Regen and Helix spells receive a bonus much greater than the normal Light Arts and Dark Arts bonuses, regardless of which Arts are in effect.  [http://forum.square-enix.com/ffxi/threads/15845 Source]
* On the test server, the bonus seems to be +24 damage per tick for Helix spells [http://www.bluegartr.com/threads/106679-Test-Server-Findings?p=4829085&viewfull=1#post4829085 Source]
* Helix duration appears to be extended by +16%.

* The bonus is +36 HP recovered per tick for Regen spells (50% greater than the normal Light Arts bonus) and double the normal Light Arts duration bonus at level 99 (Tabula Rasa bonus overwrites the Light Arts bonus).

*Dia, Box Step, Defense Down, and Geo-Frailty all stack together.

With Seigan active, a Samurai will anticipate multiple attacks as well as have the opportunity to Counter (25% of the time).

Unlike the Utsusemi series of Ninjutsu, Third Eye does not absorb magic.

tractor.description: Drags a KO'd target toward you.
tractor.name: Tractor
notes: Moves the targeted corpse to the caster's position.

Counts as moving to a new zone , with all the attendant changes in status: Target will lose Visitant status and all lights when used in Abyssea . They will be moved to the entrance, still dead, with five minutes left before getting kicked out of the area. Target will lose Allied Tags status in Campaign . Target will lose all progress towards Besieged rewards. Target will lose Grounds of Valor progress and rewards. If used in the middle of a fight, the target won't receive "credit" for completing that fight. So don't use it in the middle of a fight spawned from a ??? for a quest or something, because they will have to respawn that fight.

trick attack.description: Allows your next melee attack to deal extra damage when striking an enemy from behind a party member.
trick attack.effect: Each merit level reduces recast by 00:00:02.
trick attack.name: Trick Attack
notes: The Enmity generated by your next attack (or Weapon Skill and potential Skillchain) is transferred to a player in front of you.
* For Thief main job, damage is calculated by adding the AGI stat to weapon damage at the time the blow lands if the enmity-transferring conditions are met.

* When added to Weapon Skills, the base damage is outside of the fTP multiplier. For high fTP WSs (like Rudra's Storm) this means that the base damage boost plays a relatively minor role in determining the final damage.
This ability guarantees 100% accuracy when the activation conditions are met.
* Accuracy bonus also applies to the offhand hit when dual-wielding.
Assassin makes this job ability deal a critical hit if the enmity-transferring activation conditions are met (there is another player between the Thief and the monster).
* This critical will apply even to Weapon Skills that do not normally Critical Hit.
Ranged attacks are unaffected, and do not cancel the ability.
Counter is affected, but does not cancel the ability.
Equipment must remain on to increase effect.

Equipment which enhances Triple Shot does not grant it when the ability is not active.

Shares a recast timer with Double Shot. With Unlimited Shot merits, using Unlimited Shot will reduce the recast of both abilities, but using Double Shot will not reduce the recast of either ability. Due to the much higher utility of Double Shot, Unlimited Shot merits are not considered useful.

velocity shot.description: Increases attack power and speed of ranged attacks, while reducing attack power and speed of melee attacks.
velocity shot.name: Velocity Shot
notes: Reduces  ranged aiming delay by 15% and gives 15% melee Slow.
* Ranger Job Point gifts give additional 5% Velocity Shot aiming delay reductions at 100 and 1200 points, bringing the total reduction to 25%.
* Velocity Shot is considered separately from Snapshot, so in effect bypasses the 70 snapshot cap along with Rapid Shot.  For more info see Delay.
Increases Ranged Attack by approximately 15% and reduces Attack by approximately 15%.
* Effect is multiplicative with Berserk and possibly other attack bonuses.
* Ranger Job Points additionally give up to a flat +40 Ranged Attack.
The total aiming delay reduction from Velocity Shot possible at the time of this writing is 38%:
* Velocity Shot Base: 15%
* Velocity Shot Gifts: 10%
* Amini Caban +3: 11%
* Belenus Cape: 2%
**Equipment must remain equipped to gain the benefit.

vivacious pulse.name: Vivacious Pulse
notes: Restores HP (or MP) based on current runes and stats.
* Does not consume runes.
Most runes restore HP, based on the formula:
* HP Restored = (10 + floor(Divine Skill  2  (1 + [Vivacious Pulse Job Point %]) ) + floor(Stat1 0.5) + floor(Stat2 0.5) + floor(Stat3 0.5)  (1 + [Dynamis Divergence RUN Weapon Augment %] )
** Stat1, Stat2, and Stat3 would be for the first, second, and third rune respectively.
Tenebrae does not have a stat component for the HP restored; instead, it restores MP in addition to the HP restored.
* HP Restored = base 10 + floor(Divine Skill  2  (1 + [Vivacious Pulse Job Point %]) ) × (1 + [Dynamis Divergence RUN Weapon Augment %] )
* MP Restored = floor(Divine Skill  10  (1 + [Vivacious Pulse Job Point %]) )  (Number of Tenebrae Runes + 1)

See the below table for the Stat1, Stat2, Stat3 components. You can maximize your HP gains depending on your runes. For instance, if you maximized STR when you have 3 Ignis runes, you would gain more HP than without maximizing your STR.

weapon bash.description: Delivers an attack that can stun the target. Two-handed weapon required.
weapon bash.name: Weapon Bash
notes: Goes through shadows.
Stun rate is near 100%, and lasts quite a long time.
Being an instant ability, Weapon Bash is very effective at stunning mob TP moves.
At level 75, unenhanced Weapon Bash deals 21 blunt type damage.
Weapon Bash Damage = FLOOR(((LV+11)/4) + Equipment Bonus + Job Point Bonus)
* Level corresponds to Dark Knight level. Thus, if DRK is subbed, damage will depend on the level of your sub.
Equipping the Ignominy Gauntlets +2/3 gives it skillchain properties, allowing it to open a self-skillchain.
* For Drain purposes, the only real options using Weapon Bash are:
** Weapon Bash > Nightmare Scythe = Compression
** Weapon Bash > Insurgency = Compression
** Weapon Bash > Infernal Scythe = Compression
** Weapon Bash > Upheaval = Compression
** Weapon Bash > Keen Edge = Compression

widened compass.name: Widened Compass
notes: Doubles the range of all Geomancy effects until they wear.
Unlike Bolster it will work with Entrusted Spells.
If used while a Geocolure or an Indicolure is active it will NOT retroactively double the range.

This can be useful in some fights where the Blaze of Glory + Ecliptic Attrition Luopan dies to AOE before 10 minute as you can place the Luopan further by holding the alt key in the sub target menu then pressing arrow keys.
* You can get it even further by fine-tuning the Subtarget movement increment by typing /groundtargetst 1 through 100.

wild card.description: Has a random effect on all party members within area of effect.
wild card.name: Wild Card
notes: The die is rolled one time, and the result determines the effect on all party members in the area of effect:
*1 or 2: Restores all job abilities except SP Abilities.
*3: Restores all job abilities except SP Abilities and grants 1000 TP.
*4: Restores all job abilities except SP Abilities and grants 3000 TP.
*5: Restores job abilities including level 1 SP Abilities and restores 50% of maximum MP.
*6: Restores all job abilities including level 1 and level 96 SP Abilities and restores all MP.

**Note: A 5 or 6 roll will not normally restore Wild Card on either the user or another COR. Job Points invested in enhancing Wild Card can allow this to occur with a chance that rises by 1% per job point upgrade.
There is an equal 1/6 possibility of each of the six results.
*This probability is skewed upwards by utilizing augmented Commodore Bottes +2:
** 1 or 2 result: 1/18 chance (each)
** 3, 4, 5, or 6 result: 2/9 chance (each)

Grants 500 TP for every ability evaded.
20% Weapon Skill Damage increase per ability evaded.
60% Weapon Skill Damage cap.
The Weapon Skill Damage +% from Yaegasumi increase is calculated after WSD +% in gear.
Some unique abilities can not be mitigated.
*Tyrant Tusk (HP 50%-), 1000 Needles, Tarsal Slam, etc.
Does not avoid AoE auto-attacks from monsters like Iron Giants or Narakas

yain: ichi.description: Uses the ninja tool, " furusumi ." Suppresses Enmity gain .
yain: ichi.name: Yain: Ichi
notes: Grants the caster Enmity -15.

yawn.description: Puts all enemies within range to sleep.
yawn.name: Yawn
notes: You do not need to be facing the enemy, but the enemy must be facing you for this spell to take effect.
Interrupts all enemy actions; thus can be used as an alternative to Stun .

yonin.description: Increases enmity and enhances "ninja tool expertise" effect, but impairs accuracy. Improves evasion and reduces enemy's critical hit rate when in front of target.
yonin.name: Yonin
notes: Grants approximately +30 Enmity, +30% Ninja Tool Expertise, and -30 Accuracy at the start, which decay to 10 by the end.
* These decay at the rate of 1 point every 5 ticks (15 seconds).
Enemy Critical Hit Rate enhancement and Evasion enhancement have not been tested, but it stands to reason that they are likely -30% and +30, respectively.
Changes Utsusemi spells' base Enmity values to 160 CE / 480 VE. These don't decay over time.
Not available via support job.

yurin: ichi.description: Uses the ninja tool, " jinko ." Reduces an enemy's TP gain.
yurin: ichi.name: Yurin: Ichi
notes: Inhibits monster TP gain by 10%. Applied separately from Subtle Blow , and is unaffected by the cap.

zephyr mantle.description: Creates shadow images that each absorb a single attack directed at you.
zephyr mantle.name: Zephyr Mantle
notes: Grants 4 Shadow Images which function like Blink .
Overwritten by, but does not overwrite Utsusemi , Blink , and Aerial Armor .

zesho meppo.description: Delivers a fourfold attack. Damage varies with TP.
zesho meppo.name: Zesho Meppo
notes: Prime Aftermath:
